On the other hand, the 13 consecutive days (from MJD 55265 to MJD 55277) activity of Mrk 421 occurring in March 2010 show an orphan flaring at the X-ray and GeV-TeV ray bands 2015A&A...578A..22A . In order to attempt to understand the nature of the migration in the peak positions of the SED, in the current context, we apply the proposed model to the 13-day flaring event in March 2010 of Mrk 421, concentrating on the electrostatic acceleration responsible for the evolution of multi-wavelength SED characteristic by shifting the peak frequency.

sources.summary: The paper uses Mrk 421 as a BL Lac worked example, fitting its 2009 multiwavelength radio-to-gamma-ray SED with synchrotron and SSC emission from a single blob.

quotes.quote: We provide, in the online documentation tutorials and in the code associated with this publication, two examples using both gammapy version 0.18.2 gammapy_version and sherpa_1, sherpa_2 version v4.14.0 to wrap 's functions to fit the observed MWL SED of the blazars Mrk 421 (a BL Lac) and PKS 1510-089 (a flat spectrum radio quasar, FSRQ). While the low energy continuum of both source types is interpreted as due to synchrotron radiation of electrons, the high-energy one is commonly explained as due to SSC for BL Lacs and to EC (with a possible additional contribution from SSC process) for FSRQs. These two science cases offer us the possibility to test the fitting procedure with most of the radiative processes implemented in the package.

Mrk 421 We modelled the radio-to-gamma-ray broadband SED of Mrk 421 observed in 2009 by the MWL campaign described in abdo_2011 with the synchrotron and SSC radiation of a single blob accelerating a broken power-law EED. The model is described by nine parameters, six for the EED ( k_ e , p_1 , p_2 , '_ b , '_ min , and '_ max ; see the parametrisation in App. sec:electron_densities ), and three for the blob ( R'_ b , B , and _ D ).

sources.summary: The review describes Mrk 421 as the first extragalactic source observed above a few hundred GeV and an important nearby blazar for early EBL studies.

quotes.quote: The first extragalactic gamma-ray source observed above few hundreds of GeV is the blazar Mrk 421, at z = 0.031 or d = 140 Mpc, which was detected in 1992 . With a flux in March-June 1992 estimated to 30 that of the Crab, even a relatively low emission state See Ref. for historical gamma-ray lightcurves of Mrk 421. of Mrk 421 proved blazars to be extremely bright gamma-ray beacons distributed on extragalactic scales.

For example, spectral observations of nearby blazars such as Mrk 421 and Mrk 501 ( z 0.03 ) at 20 TeV probe the EBL density at typical wavelengths ranging in 10--100 m, that is a fraction of the CIB, while observations at 100 GeV of sources at z=1 typically probe the COB density at wavelengths ranging in 0.1--1 m. The first studies of the EBL based on observed gamma-ray spectra were developed after the discovery of Mrk 421 by the Whipple observatory. The TeV spectrum was jointly modeled with the unabsorbed spectral measurement from EGRET at MeV-GeV energies, assuming that the emitted spectrum follows a straight power-law dependence with energy from MeV to TeV energies .

sources.summary: The paper uses Markarian 421 as a z=0.031 blazar target for HAWC and Fermi-LAT EBL constraints, fitting an SSC intrinsic spectrum and noting a possible 5 TeV cutoff-related EBL bump.

quotes.quote: This energy is close to the one established by the gamma-ray horizon for sources like the two blazars Markarian 501 and Markarian 421 (from now on referred to as Markarians ), putting HAWC in an advantageous position to potentially probe the mid-IR region of the EBL using observations from these sources. For this analysis, specific Fermi-LAT and HAWC data from the blazars Markarian 421 ( z=0.031 ) and Markarian 501 ( z=0.034 ) were selected (redshift sourced from NED The NASA/IPAC Extragalactic Database (NED) is operated by the Jet Propulsion Laboratory, California Institute of Technology, under contract with the National Aeronautics and Space Administration. In the present study, a Fermi-LAT standard fitting analysis was performed to obtain spectral flux points corresponding to Markarian 421 and Markarian 501, using observations from a similar time period as the one observed by HAWC (for details see section sec:mrks ).

Figure fig:Int_Mrk421 shows Mrk 421 Fermi-LAT flux points with its corresponding Naima-SSC fit extrapolated into TeV energies. HAWC observed spectrum of Mrk 421 shows a cutoff energy around 5 TeV, this could, in principle, be related to the bump around 20 m seen in Mrk 421 results (see figure fig:bands_sep ).

sources.summary: The paper uses Mrk 421 as a bright BL Lac calibration source with a featureless flat X-ray continuum, low Galactic neutral gas column density, and a hard spectrum.

quotes.quote: These requirements are satisfied by Mrk 421, a bright BL Lac object with a featureless, flat intrinsic X-ray continuum. There is a large amount of Chandra grating spectroscopy on the object; it has been used extensively to search for intergalactic absorption 39 . The source has a low Galactic foreground neutral gas column density of 1.92 10^ 20 H atoms cm ^ 2 2 . The carbon K edge in the LETGS spectrum has been studied in detail by 4 , who constructed a model for the UVIS filter transmission based on theoretical model calculations for the X-ray absorption cross section of the neutral C atom. The spectrum of Mrk 421 is relatively hard, which means that we will have to pay detailed attention to the treatment of the higher-order contributions to the grating spectrum ( S:3 Section 3 ).

sources.summary: The paper finds Markarian 421 dominates the ray-scaled AGN catalog contribution, but the resulting arrival-direction model is not supported by Pierre Auger data.

quotes.quote: The AGN catalog is dominated by a single source between 10 EeV and 100 EeV, the faraway blazar Markarian 421, which leads to a constant catalog contribution in that energy range of around 15 .

Also, it is visible that the arrival directions test statistic is negative. The best-fit arrival directions are depicted in fig:ADs_AGN ( upper row ). Here, the dominance of Markarian 421 is clearly visible.

In contrast with the case without distance-dependent blurring, now the contribution by faraway Markarian 421 is completely blurred out, and only Centaurus A is visible above 19.5 . These arrival directions now describe the data better than the isotropic reference model, as visible in fig:TS_all_overview . Nevertheless, with TS _ ADs ^ AGN+EGMF 10 , it still does not describe the measured arrival directions as well as the models with only Centaurus A as a single source do. We tested explicitly if this model can reproduce the results from Refs. and found that it cannot reach large enough arrival direction test statistics. For that reason, we can conclude that the AGN model where the flux contribution scales linearly with the ray intensity, and which thus favors blazars like Markarian 421, is not supported by the data of the Pierre Auger Observatory.

sources.summary: The paper analyzes Mrk 421 as a nearby BL Lac blazar, connecting its 2012 and 2013 flares with possible neutrino and UHECR coincidences.

quotes.quote: We estimate the number of neutrino and UHECR events generated from the proposed models, and show that while the neutrino events are low to associate the event IC31 with Mrk 421, the number of UHECRs are similar to those reported by TA collaboration. gamma rays: general Galaxies: BL Lacertae objects individual (Markarian 421) Physical data and processes: acceleration of particles Physical data and processes: radiation mechanism: nonthermal At a distance of 134.1 Mpc, the BL Lac object Markarian 421 Mrk 421; 1991rc3..book.....D, 2005ApJ...635..173S is one of the closest known and brightest sources in the extragalactic TeV gamma-ray and X-ray sky.
The blazar Mrk 421 exhibited one of the major outbursts in temporal and positional coincidence with the neutrino event IC31 reported in the HESE catalog .

On April 10, 2013, Mrk 421 exhibited an unheard-of amount of flaring activity in optical wavelengths, X-rays and extremely high energies 2013ATel.4976....1C, 2013ATel.4977....1P, 2014A&A...570A..77P, 2013ATel.4974....1B,2013ATel.4978....1N, 2013ATel.4982....1S, 2017ApJS..232....7F . In this work, we study the gamma-ray flaring activities of Mrk 421 between July and September 2012 and April 2013 and the possible correlations with the high-energy (HE) neutrinos and the UHECR events detected around the flares in 2012 and 2013.

sources.summary: The paper flags Markarian 421 as a recurrent intrinsic-curvature case, adjusts spectral models for several VERITAS states, and finds MAGIC data consistent with a cutoff spectrum.

quotes.quote: In fact, a recurrent case is Markarian 421, as concludes that the curvature in the MAGIC 2004-2005 data is partially source-inherent and the intrinsic spectrum is compatible with PLC. The same spectral model is also used by to describe HAWC 2015-2017 data in the presence of EBL attenuation, whereas fits VERITAS 2008 data, although not including EBL effects.

Differently from , we adopted the PL model for 1ES 2344+514 (VERITAS/2007-2008), PKS 2155-304 (MAGIC/2006), and Markarian 421 (VERITAS/2008 - high C), instead of a LP model. Also, we adopted the PLC in place of LP (and vice-versa) for Markarian 421 VERITAS/2008 very low, low, high A, high B, and very high data.

Mkn 421 (MAGIC/2004--2005) : have obtained a photon index = 2.20 0.08 and a cut-off energy E_ cut =(1.44 0.27) using a reescaled version of the EBL model from , both consistent with our results of = 2.242^ +0.080 _ 0.079 and E_ cut =1.64^ +0.43 _ 0.29 .

sources.summary: The paper treats Markarian 421 as a firmly established TeV BL Lac source whose spectrum and variability constrain proton cooling and can be fit with proton-synchrotron jet emission.

quotes.quote: First of all this concerns the BL Lacertae (BL Lac) objects - a sub-population of blazars of which two prominent representatives, Markarian 421 and Markarian 501, are firmly established as TeV ray emitters. It ranges from the spectacular 1996 May 15 flare of Markarian 421 with duration less than 1 h to the extraordinary high state of Markarian 501 in 1997 lasting several months (for review see Aharonian 1999; Catanese Weekes 1999). Even so, below I will show that the very fact of observations of multi-TeV rays from Markarian 421 and Markarian 501 allow a rather robust lower limit on the ``photo-meson'' cooling time of protons, t_ p 10^7 s .

In this paper I show that for a reasonable set of parameters, which characterize the small-scale (sub-parsec) jets in Markarian 421 and Markarian 501, the synchrotron radiation of EHE protons not only may dominate over other possible radiative and non-radiative losses, but also could provide adequate fits to the observed TeV spectra of both objects. Thus, for both Markarian 501 and Markarian 421 with redshifts z=0.034 and z=0.031 , respectively, the photo-meson cooling time cannot be significantly less than 10^ 7 s , unless we assume a very high ambient photon density.

sources.summary: The paper presents BeppoSAX timing analysis of Mkn 421, emphasizing its April 1998 X-ray/TeV flare, hard-photon lag, energy-dependent flare asymmetry, and implications for synchrotron acceleration models.

quotes.quote: Mkn 421 was repeatedly observed with Beppo SAX in 1997--1998. This is the first of two papers where we present the results of a thorough temporal and spectral analysis of all the data available to us, focusing in particular on the flare of April 1998, which was simultaneously observed also at TeV energies. Here we focus on the time analysis, while the spectral analysis and physical interpretation are presented in the companion paper.

The detailed study of the flare in different energy bands reveals very important new results: i) hard photons lag the soft ones by 2--3 ks -- a behavior opposite to what is normally found in high energy peak BL Lacs X--ray spectra; ii) the flare light curve is symmetric in the softest X--ray band, while it becomes increasingly asymmetric at higher energies, with the decay being progressively slower than the rise; iii) the flux decay of the flare can be intrinsically achromatic if a stationary underlying emission component is present. The temporal and spectral information obtained challenge the simplest models currently adopted for the (synchrotron) emission and most importantly provide clues on the particle acceleration process.

sources.summary: The paper presents Beppo SAX spectral analysis of Mkn 421, emphasizing rich X-ray variability, synchrotron-peak shifts during the 1998 flare, hard lags, and constraints on particle acceleration.

quotes.quote: Mkn 421 was repeatedly observed with Beppo SAX in 1997--1998. The source showed a very rich phenomenology, with remarkable spectral variability. This is the second of two papers presenting the results of a thorough temporal and spectral analysis of all the data available to us, focusing in particular on the flare of April 1998, which was simultaneously observed also at TeV energies.

Mkn 421 is the brightest HBL at X--ray and UV wavelengths and thus it is the best available target to study in detail the properties of the variability of the highest frequency portion of the synchrotron component, which traces the changes in the energy range of the electron distribution which is most critically affected by the details of the acceleration and cooling processes. This paper is the second of two, which present the uniform analysis of the X--ray variability and spectral properties from Beppo SAX observations of Mkn 421 performed in 1997 and 1998.

sources.summary: The paper describes Mkn 421 as a nearby TeV BL Lac with rapid variable flares, multiwavelength correlations, and a high-state gamma-ray spectrum measured by VERITAS and HEGRA.

quotes.quote: Since the discovery of TeV radiation of Mkn 421 (Punch et al. 1992), this object has been subject of intensive studies through multiwavelength observations. The TeV flux of the source is variable with typical average value between 30 to 50 that of the Crab Nebula, but with strong and rapid, as short as 0.5 h, flares (Gaidos et al., 1996) which correlate with the source activity at other wavelengths. Until the spectacular high state of the source in 2001, the spectral studies were based mainly on the data taken during a quiescent or moderately high states. In particular, the spectrum measured by HEGRA during the 1998 ``ASCA'' campaign (Takahashi et al., 2000) is fitted by a steep power-law with photon index 3 (Aharonian et al., 1999b). The exceptionally bright and long-lasting activity of Mkn 421 in 2001 allowed the VERITAS (Krennrich et al., 2001) and HEGRA groups (Horns et al., 2001) to derive the time-averaged gamma-ray spectrum of the source in the high state which up to 15 TeV is described as d N/ d E = K E^ (-E/E_0) , i.e. by the same canonical ``power-law with exponential cutoff'' function found earlier for the high-state, time-averaged spectrum of Mkn 501 (Aharonian et al., 1999a).

sources.summary: The paper analyzes 13 BeppoSAX observations of Mkn 421, fitting its variable X-ray SED with a log-parabolic model and tracking synchrotron peak shifts.

quotes.quote: We report the results of a new analysis of 13 wide band Beppo SAX observations of the BL Lac object Mkn 421. The data from LECS, MECS and PDS, covering an energy interval from 0.1 to over 100 keV, have been used to study the spectral variability of this source. We show that the energy distributions in different luminosity states can be fitted very well by a log-parabolic law F(E)=K (E/E_1)^ (a+b Log(E/E_1)) , which provides good estimates of the energy and flux of the synchrotron peak in the SED. In the first four short observations of 1997 Mkn 421 was characterized by a very stable spectral shape, with average values a=2.25 and b=0.45 , independently of the source brightness and of the fact that the source luminosity was increasing or decreasing. In the observations of 1998 smaller values for both parameters, a 2.07 and b 0.34, were found and the peak energy in the SED was in the range 0.5--0.8 keV.

sources.summary: The paper uses Mrk 421 as a z=0.03 blazar observed by Chandra LETG during bright outbursts; its spectrum contains absorption lines from intervening WHIM systems.

quotes.quote: Mrk 421 is a blazar at z=0.03 . We observed Mrk 421 with the Chandra LETG during two very luminous outbursts, on 2002, October, 26-27 and 2003, July 1-2, under our TOO programs to observe blazars in outburst phases. These two observations lasted 100 ks each and caught the source at historical maxima, 60 and 40 mCrab in the 0.5-2 keV band, allowing us to collect a total of 5300 Counts Per Resolution Element (CPREs) at 21 \ (Nicastro et al., 2004b: N04b).

We detected 24 absorption lines in the LETG spectrum of Mrk 421, between 10 and 50 \ (see No4b for a full, detailed spectral analysis of both continuum and lines). While the majority of these lines are imprinted by both neutral and highly ionized systems at velocity consistent with zero (ISM gas, and either Local Group WHIM - Nicastro et al., 2002; Nicastro et al., 2003; Williams et al., 2004 - or an extended Galactic Corona - Sembach et al., 2003; Savage et al., 2004), 9 lines are instead identified with two intervening WHIM systems at z = (0.011 0.001) and z = (0.027 0.001) .

sources.summary: The paper describes Mkn 421 as a z=0.03 blazar with strong outbursts, TeV detections, and multiple Chandra observations that reached historical X-ray brightness.

quotes.quote: Mkn 421 is a blazar at z=0.03 (De Vaucouleurs et al., 1991), which undergoes frequent strong outbursts from its quiescent state of 1 mCrab ( 2 10^ 11 erg s ^ 1 cm ^ 2 ) to 20-50 mCrab (Figure 1). When in outburst Mkn 421 is one of the few objects detected at TeV energies (e.g. Okumura et al., 2002). Mkn 421 has been observed by Chandra at four different epochs (Figure 1, Table 1).

Finally, two further 100 ks Chandra ACIS-LETG and HRC-LETG observations of Mkn 421 were performed in 2002, October, 26-27 (Figure 1 and 2, Table 1), and 2003, July 1-2 (Figure 1, Table 1), under our approved Chandra AO4 program to observe blazars in outburst phases. These two observations were performed 24 and 18 hours respectively after the trigger and caught the source at a historical maximum, 100 times brighter than in the first observation, 60 and 40 mCrab in the 0.5-2 keV band (Figure 1, Table 1), and allowed us to collect 2600 CPREs each at 21 \ (Table 1).

sources.summary: The paper uses Mrk 421 as the comparison HBL whose prior X-ray spectral correlations motivate the search for similar TeV BL Lac behavior in other sources.

quotes.quote: A recent analysis of Mrk 421 observations performed with , and ASCA (Tramacere et al. 2007a) has shown two correlations between spectral parameters: the SED peak energy E_p correlates with the peak flux S_p but anticorrelates with the curvature parameter b (as detailed in Sect. 3). These correlations are relevant as signatures of synchrotron emission and of statistical/stochastic acceleration mechanisms for the emitting electrons, respectively.
Here, we use the spectra of Mrk 421 (discussed in Tramacere et al., 2007a) as a comparison term for the behaviour of other HBLs.
For Mrk 421, the results are r_ log =0.67 and r_ log =-0.67 for the E_p^*-L_p^* and E_p^*-b relations, respectively.

sources.summary: The paper reports that Markarian 421 entered a very active TeV phase in 2008; ARGO-YBJ monitored it above 0.3 TeV and found variable flux, March and June flares, and average emission almost twice Crab.

quotes.quote: In 2008 the blazar Markarian 421 entered a very active phase and was one of the brightest sources in the sky at TeV energies, showing frequent flaring episodes. Using the data of ARGO-YBJ, a full coverage air shower detector located at Yangbajing (4300 m a.s.l., Tibet, China), we monitored the source at gamma ray energies E > 0.3 TeV during the whole year. The observed flux was variable, with the strongest flares in March and June, in correlation with X-ray enhanced activity. While during specific episodes the TeV flux could be several times larger than the Crab Nebula one, the average emission from day 41 to 180 was almost twice the Crab level, with an integral flux of (3.6 0.6 ) 10 ^ 11 photons cm ^ 2 s ^ 1 for energies E > 1 TeV, and decreased afterwards.

sources.summary: The paper treats Mkn 421 as a nearby VHE source whose high flux gives strong spectra but whose intrinsic GeV-TeV break complicates EBL measurements.

quotes.quote: Strong flux outbursts in the VHE regime have also been observed in the two nearby sources Mkn 421 and Mkn 501. While the smaller distances result in less EBL attenuation and therefore a weaker signature, the overall higher flux levels of these two sources enable to derive spectra with high event statistics.

One caveat - at least for the case of Mkn 421 - is the fact that there are strong indications for an intrinsic (not EBL related) break between the GeV and TeV range at relatively high energies (around 100 GeV) , which would make it difficult to define a proper low energy region void of any curvature.

For most of the measured sources, a simple power law fit is sufficient to describe the available data well, whereas for sources in a flare state (like the flare of PKS 2155-304 in 2006) or with a generally high emission state (like Mkn 421), either a curved power law or a power law with a cut-off are successfully used.

sources.summary: The paper describes 1101+384 as a BL Lac with a straight VLBA jet, bent VLA jet, radio/optical/X-ray quasi-periodicity, and infrared variability.

quotes.quote: * 1101+384 (Mrk 421) (BL Lac, z=0.031 ): VLBA jet appears to be straight. The VLA jet appears bent. It is almost periodically variable in the radio, optical, and X-rays. It is variable in the infrared.

sources.summary: AGILE detected Mrk 421 during the W Comae target-of-opportunity, measuring hard X-ray and gamma-ray brightening and organizing extensive optical-to-TeV follow-up.

quotes.quote: During the ToO towards W Comae, \ detected also the HBL object Mrk 421, in hard X-rays and rays. SuperAGILE detected a fast increase of flux from Mrk 421 up to 40 mCrab in the 15--50 energy band, about a factor of 10 higher than its typical flux in quiescence ( ), reaching about 55 mCrab on 2008 June 13. This observation was followed by the detection in ray by GRID with a flux, F _ E>100 MeV = (42 13) 10 ^ 8 ph cm ^ 2 s ^ 1 , about a factor 3 higher than the average EGRET value, even if consistent with its maximum. An extensive multi-wavelength campaign from optical to TeV energy bands was organized with the participation of WEBT, Swift , RXTE, , MAGIC and VERITAS, as reported in detail in .

sources.summary: The paper models Mkn 421 in high and low states, noting TeV discovery, GeV-TeV and X-ray variability, an April 2004 outburst, and ARGO-YBJ high-energy detection.

quotes.quote: Mkn 421. It is the first TeV AGN confirmed by Whipple (Punch et al. 1992) and is a high-frequency-peaked BL Lac (HBL) object. Violent variation of the flux at GeV-TeV regime was detected and associated with that observed at the X-ray band (BLazejowski et al. 2005). The data of high state are from BLazejowski et al. (2005), when the source underwent an outburst in 2004 April with the peak flux 135 mCrab in the X-ray band and 3 Crab in the gamma ray band. The variabilities on timescales of hours were observed, so we take t=3 h to model the SED of the high state (to see Figure 10 in BLazejowski et al. 2005).

sources.summary: The paper treats Markarian 421 as a variable TeV blazar target for the ASTRI prototype, estimating detection rates and short integrations across possible flaring states.

quotes.quote: Markarian 421 . This z=0.031 redshift blazar exhibited strong TeV variability during the years, with fluxes going from below 1 up to 10 C.U. Hence, for this source we adopt many different spectral models , in order to constrain its observability at different flaring states. Photon rates span from about 40 to about 600 photons per hour, and imply integration times from 14 to 0.1 hours, assuming a 5 detection. The possibility of high-significance detection in less than one night of observation could allow short time-scale variability studies, eventually in conjunction with other instruments observing at different wavelengths.

sources.summary: The paper compares Mkn 421 PSD slopes with Fermi/LAT and X-ray studies, noting marginal consistency with Abdo et al. averages and shallower slopes than NM13.

quotes.quote: In the BL Lac class, only PKS 2155-304 and (marginally) BL Lac and Mkn 421 have the slopes consistent with the Abdo et al. average at the 2 confidence level, but these are the three source with the broadest posterior slope distributions in our sample (see Figure fig:slope ). In 3C 454.3, PKS 1510-089, 3C 273, BL Lac and Mkn 421 our slopes were shallower than those derived by NM13. Soft X-ray blazar PSD (here below 20 keV) were computed for three BL Lac sources (Mkn 421 and PKS 2155-304 also included in our Fermi/LAT sample, and Mkn 501) by Kataoka et al. (2001), and for FSRQ sources by McHardy (2008; 3C 273), Chatterjee et al. (2008; 3C 279). Hard X-ray (14--150 keV; Swift/BAT) PSD have been recently constructed by Shimizu Mushotzky (2013) for a sample of 30 AGN, which included two FSRQs (3C 273 and 3C 454.3) and one BL Lac (Mkn 421).

sources.summary: The review highlights Markarian 421 as a TeV blazar with an 11-Crab flare observed during multiwavelength campaigns, whose modeled SED evolution favors two emitting blobs.

quotes.quote: Prior planning is generally required for these campaigns, and so the fortuitous detection of a bright flare (exceeding 11 Crab in the TeV band) from Markarian 421 in April 2013 during multiwavelength observations is very exciting. Only preliminary results for an earlier part of the campaign were shown at the ICRC (Figure Nustar_421 ), and the interpretation is still in the earliest stages, but even these provide useful new information, including the absence of any evidence for a cut-off in both components of the SED. More complete modelling has been applied to observations of Markarian 421 from an earlier, 2010, multiwavelength campaign, which captured a decaying flare from the source . The authors model this with both one and two-zone synchrotron self-Compton emission models, and conclude that the evolution of the SEDs favours the presence of two 'blobs' of material, rather than the single blob typically used to describe flares in TeV blazars.

sources.summary: The paper uses Mrk 421 as a BL Lac example, estimating extremely small proton photomeson optical depths in quiescent, flaring, and hadronic gamma-ray scenarios.

quotes.quote: In the cases that gamma rays are of leptonic origin, a recent observational result with good spectral coverage for a quiescent state of Mrk 421 allows us to estimate the optical depth _0 of 10^ 7 if protons are accelerated in the same region as electrons. The optical depth is 10^ 6 even in flaring episodes. Hadronic models for gamma rays require much stronger magnetic fields in the acceleration/emission region, 10 - 100 G, than those in the leptonic model, typically 0.1 G, to accelerate protons up to UHE, in order to open the p channel and also to lead protons to emit synchrotron radiation in gamma-ray bands. However, because of small electron-synchrotron photon density, proton synchrotron radiation dominantly contributes to gamma rays, and therefore the optical depth _0 is still 10^ 7 for Mrk 421.

sources.summary: The paper compares M 87 with the BL Lac object Mrk 421, citing one-zone SSC modeling where strong beaming from a small jet angle boosts its intrinsic emission.

quotes.quote: As FR-I radio galaxies like M 87 are the parent population of BL Lacs, comparing the resulting SED of M 87 with a BL Lac object Mrk 421 will illustrate the differences between these two source classes. Using a one-zone SSC model, Blazejowski2005 found that Mrk 421 ( L_ bol =3 10^ 45 erg s^ 1 ) requires a beaming factor of =10 , an emitting region a radius of R=7.0 10^ 15 cm and magnetic field B = 0.405 G . The stronger beaming, due to the small jet angle of Mrk 421, boosts the intrinsic emission strongly. While the emitting region is smaller compared to that used to model M 87, the magnetic field is much stronger, in combination increasing the flux.

sources.summary: The paper describes Mrk 421 as a VHE BL Lac source in its monitoring sample and reports its optical/radio flux level, variability trends, and correlation behavior.

quotes.quote: Mrk 421 : The brightest optical source in our sample with rather large modulation index. Both light curves show clear outbursts that visually seem correlated which is confirmed by the DCF analysis (see Fig. Fig:lc15 ). Both light curves also show significant increasing trend and polynomial subtraction suggests that at least 50 of the optical flux originates from common optical-radio component. For this source a significant correlation is also found between the radio and ray light curves, when the largest extreme flare in 2012 is studied hovatta15 .

sources.summary: The paper's Mrk 421-only search found no neutrinos during flare-selected windows, set a 90% upper limit of 1.27 flux-associated neutrinos, and could not constrain hadronic emission.

quotes.quote: Our Mrk 421-only search yields no neutrinos during the temporal acceptance windows, while 5 were required for a detection claim. Our non-detection implies an upper limit of 1.27 (2.30 in total, minus 1.03 background) flux-associated neutrinos from Mrk 421 at 90 confidence. If the TeV ray flux from Mrk 421 during our identified periods of excess and flaring emission were entirely due to hadronic interactions, this would give 0.79 neutrinos. As this is smaller than the upper limit, we cannot constrain the hadronic process for Mrk 421; the relevant figure of merit is < 1.6 .

sources.summary: The paper finds significant NuSTAR spectral variability in Mrk 421, with a repeated harder-when-brighter trend attributed to changes in jet-related non-thermal hard X-ray emission.

quotes.quote: Significant spectral variations are seen in the BL Lacs object Mrk 421, the FSRQ 3C 273, the Seyfert 1 galaxy NGC 5548 and the Seyfert 2 galaxy NGC 1365.
For the BL Lac object Mrk 421 a significant harder when brighter trend is seen in 6 epochs of observations.

The hard X-ray band in Mrk 421 is primarily dominated by the non-thermal continuum (not necessarily true for the Seyfert galaxies NGC 5548 and NGC 1365), while in the soft X-ray band there can be contribution from different processes such as power law continuum, soft excess, neutral absorption and absorption features from warm absorbers. Thus, the harder when brighter trend seen in Mrk 421 is most likely due to changes in the power law component in the relativistic jets of these sources.

sources.summary: The paper identifies Markarian 421/Mrk 421 as a high-peaked BL Lac and first multi-TeV extragalactic source, then models its 2004 and 2010 VHE flares with photohadronic emission and EBL attenuation.

quotes.quote: Markarian 421 is a high-peaked BL Lac object and it has undergone many strong outbursts since its discovery as a TeV source in 1992. Markarian 421 has been stud- ied intensively and was observed by various Cherenkov tele- scope arrays ever since. The outbursts of April 2004 observed by the Whipple telescope and of February 2010 by the HESS telescopes are explained well in this work by using the photohadronic model.
Mrk 421 is the first extragalactic source detected in the multi-TeV domain and it is one of the fastest varying ray sources.

sources.summary: The paper selects Mrk 421 for detailed RXTE/PCA blazar analysis, extracts 20.5 flares, finds peak energies below 6 keV, and reports harder-when-brighter X-ray spectra.

quotes.quote: As such, among 32 TeV blazars, we singled out 5 objects (i.e., Mrk 421, Mrk 501, PKS 2155--304, PKS 2005--489, and 1ES 1959+650; hereafter ``the five sources''; see Table table:allta ) and for which RXTE /PCA data allow us to obtain high-quality spectra (detailed analysis of the other 27 TeV blazars will be presented in a future study). Furthermore, the flares were selected with the following criteria: 1) individual flares lasted for several days and were covered by at least two observations in both the rise and decay periods; and 2) the minimum total count rate (summed over available PCUs) in 3--25 keV is above 30 /s. In addition, adjacent outbursts following or followed by those flares were also included. Finally, we picked out 20.5 flares for Mrk 421 (an outburst with observations only in the rise or decay period was considered as 0.5 flare), 7 flares for Mrk 501, 4 flares for PKS 2155--304, and only one flare for both PKS 2005--489 and 1ES 1959+650 from the 16-year data (see Figures fig:spectrum and fig:pfevo for the typical flares of each source; also see the observations annotated with `` '' in Table table:allta .

Combining the 3--25 keV spectral shape information and the synchrotron peak energy obtained by fitting all PCA spectra with the log-parabolic model detailed in Section sec:peakes , we found that: for Mrk 421, the peak energy of all the spectra is below 6 keV, which is consistent with the results in 2004A&A...413..489M , 2004ApJ...601..759T and 2007A&A...466..521T (but 2009A&A...501..879T shows that its peak energy could be up to 30 keV); for Mrk 501, the peak energy of most spectra is above 3 keV, and 2008A&A...478..395M shows that its peak energy could be up to 100 keV; for PKS 2005--489 and PKS 2155--304, the peak energy of spectra is below 3 keV; for 1ES 1959+650, the peak energy of most spectra is below 30 keV.

The X-ray spectra, characterized by , display a harder-when-brighter trend during a number of flares of the five brightest sources (i.e., Mrk 421, Mrk 501, PKS 2155--304, PKS 2005--489, and 1ES 1959+650), which is consistent with previous studies.

sources.summary: The paper uses Mkn 421 as a representative BL Lac SED, showing an anisotropic-electron model can reproduce its emission near equipartition with stronger magnetic fields and typical jet parameters.

quotes.quote: Considering the BL Lac object Mkn 421 as representative of the entire class, we show that in both cases the emission can be satisfactorily reproduced under equipartition between the magnetic field and the relativistic electrons. This is in better agreement with the idea that jets are launched as Poynting dominated flows with respect to the standard isotropic scenario, which requires both a low magnetization and a low radiative efficiency to reproduce the observed emission. The hard spectrum predicted for the inverse Compton continuum at TeV energies could be used as a potential test of the anisotropic model.
Apart from the stronger magnetic field, the values of the parameters derived for the jet of Mkn 421 in the anisotropic scenario are similar the usual ones .

sources.summary: The paper models Mrk 421 as a high-synchrotron-peaked BL Lac with soft radio-to-IR emission, adopting a black-hole mass and fitting its spectrum with an accelerating jet.

quotes.quote: We compare our model synchrotron spectrum with those of some quiescent spectra of blazars, namely Mrk 421 and Mrk 501, which are high-synchrotron peaked BL Lac object at the redshifts of z=0.0308 and 0.03364, respectively. We have chosen those objects because they have soft radio-to-IR spectra with 0.2 , as well as they are ones of the brightest sources in their class, their continua from radio to hard X-rays are well covered and of high quality, see abdo11b and abdo11a , respectively.
The BH mass of Mrk 421 was measured via the M relation by barth03 as _ 10 (M/ )=8.28 0.11 ; here we assume M=2 10^8 , corresponding to r_ g 3 10^ 13 cm.

On the other hand, we find we can indeed reproduce the spectrum of Mrk 421 with an accelerated jet, with q=0.1 , as shown in Fig. \ spectrum421 , which caption also gives all the parameters assumed in the model.

sources.summary: The paper includes Mrk 421 among three blazars monitored by Fermi-LAT, noting its smaller flux span and shared gamma-ray variability analysis.

quotes.quote: Using a continuous gamma-ray light curves over 3-days cadence from August 2008 – October 2015, we present the first systematic study of these features in four sources: the FR I radio galaxy NGC 1275 and three blazars- Mrk 421, B2 1520+31 and PKS 1510-089. For all, except Mrk 421, the flux spans 2 orders of magnitude. For blazars, a log-normal profile describes the flux histograms better compared to a Gaussian, while none is favored for NGC 1275, the only non blazar source, suggesting a complex distribution. Regardless of flux histogram profile, the rms-flux relation is linear for all with PSDs being consistent with a power-law shot noise spectrum despite hints of breaks.

sources.summary: The paper models Mrk 421 as an HBL but finds the tested SEDs fail in the GeV-TeV range and yield EBL-density results below galaxy-count constraints.

quotes.quote: 3. Mrk 421 As an HBL object, we take t_ v,min =3hr and '_ min =180 zhang2012 . As shown in Fig.3, two SEDs fail to fit the GeV-TeV spectrum, disfavoring the one-zone SSC scenario. Besides, the EBL density can't be reduced in BPL EEDs, and it is significantly lower than that obtained by galaxy counts in the PLLP EEDs, shown in the right panel of Fig.8.

Note that the EBL density obtained by lower redshift sources are much closer to the previous results than that reduced by high redshift objects, which disfavor that the EBL is more transparent to distant ray fra2019 ; Besides, for lower redshift objects, Mrk 421 and Mrk 501, they seemly fail to obtain reasonable limits on the EBL density.

sources.summary: The paper examines Mrk 421 light-curve timing as a test of energy-dependent light speed, finding its inferred scale compatible with gamma-ray burst results.

quotes.quote: We check this speed variation from previous observations on light curves of three active galactic nuclei (AGNs), namely Markarian 421 (Mrk 421), Markarian 501 (Mrk 501) and PKS 2155-304. Markarian 421 Markarian 421 (Mrk 421) is a blazar located around 397 million light-years to 434 million light-years from the earth. In an earlier paper , the Lorentz violation effect is studied with the data of the rapid flare with a redshift z = 0.031 of Mrk 421 on 15 May 1996.
As a result, the light speed variation scale from Mrk 421 is compatible with that of GRBs, i.e., with E_ LV ^ grb in Refs. .

sources.summary: The paper analyzes Markarian 421 gamma-ray spectra for ALP-photon oscillations, combining 15 observing phases to strengthen constraints on ALP parameter space.

quotes.quote: We explore the axion-like particle (ALP)-photon oscillation effect in the ray spectra of the blazars Markarian 421 (Mrk 421) and PG 1553+113, which are measured by the Major Atmospheric Gamma Imaging Cherenkov Telescopes (MAGIC) and Fermi Large Area Telescope ( Fermi LAT) with high precision. We investigate the constraints on the ALP parameter space using the Mrk 421 and PG 1553+113 observations of 15 and 5 phases, respectively. We find that the combined analysis with all the 15 phases improves the limits from the Mrk 421 observations. The combined limit set by the Mrk 421 observations has excluded the ALP parameter region with the ALP-photon coupling of g_ a 2 10^ 11 GeV^ 1 for the ALP mass of 8 10^ 9 eV m_a 2 10^ 7 eV at 95 C.L.

sources.summary: The paper models Mkn 421 as a nearby variable BL Lac, using simultaneous Fermi-LAT and MAGIC spectra to scan magnetic-field parameters for photon-ALP mixing.

quotes.quote: Combined spectrum from Mkn 421 The northern, nearby AGN Mkn 421 ( z=0.031 ) is a highly variable BL Lac type object that has been closely monitored since the discovery of its VHE emission Punch:1992xw . While a number of simultaneous multi-wavelength observations have been carried out for this source, we select the result reported by LAT:t on a simultaneous observation campaign with Fermi-LAT and the MAGIC telescopes from January to June 2009. During this campaign, the combined energy spectrum from the two instruments covers a very broad energy range with substantial overlap between the two instruments. The scan of the parameters s and B for the combined spectrum shows several maxima which would favor either a large magnetic field of several G on kpc scales or a very weak magnetic field of several nG over Mpc distances (see Fig. fig:mkn421scan_simu ).

sources.summary: The paper models Mrk 421 as a blazar with relatively lower high-energy-hump peak luminosity, producing larger allowed parameter space and fitting its high-energy hump with proton synchrotron radiation.

quotes.quote: In this energy range, we study 1ES 2037+521 and Mrk 421. The derived results are basically similar to those obtained in the range of 1 TeV 10 TeV. As shown in Table sample , since the high-energy hump's peak luminosities of 1ES 2037+521 and Mrk 421 are lower than those of 1ES 0229+200 and 1ES 1101-232, larger parameter spaces are derived. It can be seen in Figs. 2037 and 421-0.1T that parameter spaces can be found under the observational constraints when R=1 10^ 16 cm and R=1 10^ 17 cm . In the modeling, their high-energy humps are fitted by the proton synchrotron radiation as well.

sources.summary: The paper reports an extreme X-ray flare in Mrk 421, finding harder-when-brighter behavior, rapid variability, negligible lags, and a plasmoid-reconnection interpretation.

quotes.quote: We analysed a target-of-opportunity observation of Mrk 421 during a very bright X-ray flare that occurred simultaneously to VHE ray activity in June 2019. Our spectral analysis reveals typical harder-when-brighter behaviour. From the clock-wise hysteresis loop and a tentative soft lag, which is found by employing the z-transformed discrete correlation function, we conclude that the electron cooling dominated over the acceleration process during the flare. Our timing analysis determines a fractional variability amplitude above the average level of single pointed observations and rapid variability down to time scales of 300 s at the highest energies, and 1 ks in the full energy range.

sources.summary: The review describes Mrk 421 as a rapidly variable TeV active galactic nucleus whose multiwavelength behavior constrains a compact emission region and synchrotron-self-Compton modeling.

quotes.quote: The variability of Mrk 421 is very fast and the doubling time can be less than one hour . Multiwavelength campaigns place constraints on the location and size of the emission region . The spectrum again shows a double-peaked structure which is considered as synchrotron emission of high energy electrons and inverse Compton scattering of synchrotron photons by those electrons . This model gives us parameters such as beaming factor of around 10 and magnetic field of around 0.1 G.

sources.summary: The paper treats Mrk 421 as the exceptional EGRET blazar detected by Whipple, describing hour-long flares, correlated X-ray and gamma-ray variability, and later Whipple/RXTE observations of rapid changes.

quotes.quote: Whipple observations of the vast majority of EGRET blazars have yielded only upper limits ; Mrk 421 ( z =0.031 ) being the exception.

Multiwavelength Observations: Variability Data taken on Mrk 421 over the years 1995 to 2001 show that the ray emission is characterized by a succession of approximately hour-long flares with relatively symmetric profiles (see Figure fig:newmulti ). While most of the multiwavelength observations of Mrk 421 show evidence for correlated X-ray and gamma ray variability, the nature of the correlation is unclear and the data have traditionally undersampled the variability.

Recent multiwavelength observations of Mrk 421 made during the period March 18, 2001 to April 1, 2001 with the Whipple gamma-ray telescope, and the Proportional Counter Array (PCA) detector on the Rossi X-ray Timing Explorer (RXTE) better sample the rapid variability of Mrk 421.

sources.summary: The paper uses Mkn 421 as a nearby TeV blazar whose spectrum shows a turnover and expected cosmological absorption similar to Mkn 501.

quotes.quote: Only three blazars Mkn 421, Mkn 501, and H1426+428 were bright enough to determine their spectra. Mkn 501 and Mkn 421 are showing a turn-over at almost the same energy adopting a power-law-times-exponential model. The spectra show some small changes with flux, and it is under investigation whether this affects the cutoff energy or not. Both sources have almost the same redshift z=0.03 and should show cosmological absorption at the same energy of 6-18  TeV.

sources.summary: The paper presents Mkn 421 as an outbursting blazar target whose two 100 ks Chandra observations produced a high-count spectrum with sensitivity to faint OVII absorption.

quotes.quote: We decided to pursue this second strategy, and during the past year triggered two Target-of-Opportunity Observations (TOOs) of the Blazar Mkn 421 ( z = 0.03 , i.e. d = 128 Mpc, De Vaucouleurs, et al., 1991), during two exceptionally high flux outburst phases. The two observations were carried out with the Chandra ACIS-LETG (2002, October 26-27) and the HRC-LETG (2003, July, 1-2), lasted 100 ks each, and reported 0.5-2 keV fluxes of 100 and 40 mCrab, respectively. This allowed us to collect more than 10 million counts in the coadded first order spectrum, and more than 6000 counts per resolution element at 21.6 . In turn, this guaranteed a 3 sensitivity of N_ OVII 10^ 14.8 cm ^ 2 , more than an order of magnitude fainter than the LG-WHIM.

sources.summary: The paper compares simulated BL Lac X-ray light curves with Mrk 421, describing rapid resolved variability, flux-linked spectral hardening, small lags, and observed multiple-peak flare structure.

quotes.quote: From an analysis of early data taken with the EPIC cameras from Mrk 421, the brightest BL Lac object at X-ray and UV wavelengths, for the first time the evolution of intensity variations could be resolved on time scales of 100 s ( 2001). Temporal variations by a factor of three at highest X-ray energies were accompanied by complex spectral variations with only a small time lag of = 265^ +116 _ 102 s between the hard and soft photons. In an extensive study of all currently available observations of Mrk 421 (2003) find that the source exhibits a rather complex and irregular variability pattern - both, temporarily and spectrally.
A comparison of our results with observed light curves of Mrk 421 shows that some of the observed flares display a multiple peak structure that can be well accommodated by our model.

sources.summary: The paper applies the same SSC framework to Mrk 421, stating that RXTE and HEGRA observations can be fit with emission from the highest-energy synchrotron-tail particles.

quotes.quote: We show that the other TeV sources (Mrk 421, Mrk 501 PKS 2155--304) can be interpreted in the same framework, with a somewhat larger minimum energy.

In this section we apply this model to two other TeV sources, Mrk 421 and PKS 2155--304, and compare the results. The RXTE and HEGRA observations of Mrk 421 Krawczynski01 can be fitted assuming that the X--ray emission is done by the exponential synchrotron tail of the highest energy particles at _ max .

Moreover, in the case of Mrk 421, Mrk 501 and PKS 2155--304 the X-ray and TeV observations were made almost simultaneously, whereas for 1ES 1101--232 we use the X--ray spectrum from January 1997 and the TeV data collected over March-June 2004-2005.

sources.summary: The paper reports VLBI and TeV monitoring of Mkn 421, finding compact-core variability during rising TeV activity and linking the 22 GHz core to synchrotron self-Compton emission.

quotes.quote: Coordinated multi-frequency VLBI polarization and very-high-energy TeV observations of the ray blazar Mkn 421 have provided evidence of variability in the compact VLBI core on a timescale of a few weeks and at a time when the overall TeV activity of the source was rising. Although we cannot be entirely sure about the connection between the two phenomena, our measurements are consistent with a scenario in which the VLBI core at 22 GHz represents the self-absorbed part of the low-energy emission induced by a one-zone synchrotron self-Compton model consistent with simultaneous high-energy data. At the lower radio frequencies (15, 8, and 5 GHz), the VLBI core encompasses additional emission unrelated to the SSC phenomenon and most probably originating from regions outside the ray emitting zone. Based on these observations, we also derived an upper limit of 0.1 pc ( 3 10^ 17 cm) for the projected size of the ray emitting region, in agreement with previous estimates but based solely on our radio-interferometric data and hence free of any theoretical model.

sources.summary: The paper treats Mrk421 as a TeV blazar with 1-20 TeV emission and luminosity near 10^44 erg/s, arguing that inclined rotating magnetospheric field lines can explain its observed ultra-high energies.

quotes.quote: Some blazars, such as Mrk 421, Mrk 501, PKS 2155-304, 1ES 2344-514, H1426+428, and 1ES 1959+650, emit TeV ( 1TeV 10^ 12 eV ) photons and form a special class of ``TeV blazars" ( ).
For TeV blazars like Mrk421 and Mrk501 we have ultra-high energy ray emission in the range [ 1-20 ] TeV . The luminosity power of Mrk421 and Mrk501 is estimated as L 10^ 44 erg/s ( ).
As it is seen from this figure, while considering magnetic field line's inclination in the range 0.6^ 1.2^ , one can explain the observed ultra-high energies from Mrk421 and Mrk501.

sources.summary: The paper describes Mrk 421 as a TeV-detected blazar at redshift 0.031 with rapid flux variability and a Whipple-measured steep spectrum extending beyond 5 TeV.

quotes.quote: Two active galaxies, Mrk 421 and Mrk 501, have thus far been seen to produce TeV gamma-ray emission by independent experimental groups. These active galaxies are both of the blazar class and are located at redshifts of 0.031 and 0.033, respectively. Both objects have exhibited significant flux variability at TeV energies, changing by an order of magnitude or more with characteristic timescales of less than an hour (in the case of Mrk 421, as short as 15 minutes).
Recent analyses of data from Mrk 421 by the Whipple group indicate a differential spectrum with an E^ 2.6 power law extending to energies beyond 5 TeV, possibly as high as 8 TeV.

sources.summary: The paper reports an INTEGRAL campaign on Mkn 421 triggered by exceptional X-ray flux, detecting the source across instruments, showing a synchrotron peak shift and mostly spectrally steady flares.

quotes.quote: Mkn 421 (z=0.03) The INTEGRAL campaign of June 2006 (PI Lichti) on this famous HBL was triggered by an exceptional X-ray flux (up to 80 mcrab measured by the All-Sky Monitor of RXTE in the 2-10 keV energy band). The source was significantly detected with all the instruments onboard INTEGRAL (except SPI, which was annealing) and the SED, built with multiwavelength data, clearly showed the shift of the synchrotron peak from soft to hard X-rays . The study of the variability across about 10 days of exposure revealed several flares, but only one showed significant spectral changes .

sources.summary: The paper describes Mrk 421 X-ray flaring as complex spectral evolution tied to electron acceleration, with signatures of first- and second-order processes and a stochastic-acceleration curvature trend.

quotes.quote: We have shown that the X-ray flaring activity of Mrk 421 results in a complex spectral evolutions due to drastic changes in the electron energy distribution probably related to a complex acceleration scenario. Indeed, in our analysis we found both signatures of first and second order acceleration processes acting at the same time. The E_p-b trend is consistent with a SA scenario with the X-ray spectral curvature related to the acceleration rather than to the cooling process.

sources.summary: The paper presents Mrk 421 as a low-power blazar with TeV flares lacking major VLBI structural changes and a smooth radio jet maintained over long monitoring.

quotes.quote: Also, since this timescale (Eq. tgamma ) is fairly long, we expect occasional ray flares without radio blob ejection; this appears to be the case in TeV flares associated with high-energy peaked AGN such as Mrk 421, and Mrk 501, in which no major changes in VLBI radio structure are seen (MOJAVE program; Ros \ in prep).

Relatively low power blazar jets in the MOJAVE program like Mrk 421 or Mrk 501 show a very smooth fall-off in radio intensity with distance from the core, and have maintained this structure over more than a decade of VLBI monitoring.
For example, with the jets in Mrk 421 or Mrk 501 at z=0.03 oriented at a few degrees to the line of sight, this corresponds to a few milliarcseconds projected on the sky.

sources.summary: The paper identifies Mrk 421 as the sample's most variable blazar in Swift/BAT hard X-rays, with fractional variability around 0.96.

quotes.quote: The blazar Mrk 421 shows a much stronger variability ( F_ var 0.96 ) than the average value of the sample. Similar results are obtained also considering Mrk 421 as an outlier. Studying the 1-month binned Swift /BAT light-curves of the 20 brightest objects after 58 months of observations, we found that all the objects in our sample show variability, ranging between F_ var =0.13 and F_ var =0.96 , for Circinus galaxy and Mrk 421, respectively.

sources.summary: The paper adopts Stocke et al. accretion values for Mkn 421, based on weak Ly emission lines giving a very low Eddington-scaled disc luminosity.

quotes.quote: Recently, weak Ly emission lines (equivalent width <<1 ) have been observed in Mkn 501 and Mkn 421 (Stocke et al. 2011). The disc luminosity [ in Eddington units ] calculated from these lines, by assuming that L_ disc 10L_ Ly , is 5.7 10^ 6 and 3.5 10^ 6 for Mkn 501 and Mkn 421, respectively (see Foschini 2011b). The non-detection of any line in PKS 2155 304 poses an upper limit tighter than the upper limit in Ghisellini et al. (2010). Therefore, in these three cases (Mkn 421, Mkn 501, PKS 2155 304), I adopt the values of the accretion from Stocke et al. (2011).

sources.summary: Mrk421 underwent a bright February 2010 flare; HAGAR detected enhanced >250 GeV flux of 6-7 Crab units and the paper presents its flaring-state SED.

quotes.quote: Mrk421 was observed to undergo one of its brightest flaring episodes on 2010 February 17, and detected by various experiments in X-rays and rays. HAGAR observations of this source during 2010 February 13 - 19, in the energies above 250 GeV show an enhancement in the flux level, with a flux of 6-7 Crab units being detected on 2010 February 17. We present the spectral energy distribution of the source during this flaring episode.

sources.summary: The paper notes Mkr 421 as a bright soft X-ray blazar whose long-timescale ASM power-density spectrum is well fitted by a near-minus-one power law.

quotes.quote: While the average slope for the BL Lacs is at least as steep as that of the FSRQs, the two HSPs, Mkr 421 and PKS2155-305, both have PDS slopes flatter than 1.0 (Figure 5). Mkr 421 is the brightest soft X-ray blazar and a PDS for long time scales at those energies can be computed from the ASM light curve. The PDS is well fitted by a power law with index close to -1, as shown in Figure 6.

sources.summary: Markarian 421 was monitored with ATOM, showing overall behavior consistent with earlier work and about one magnitude of flux variation over the measured optical spectral range.

quotes.quote: Markarian 421: The source was observed during 42 nights in 2007--2012. The overall behavior is present, in the agreement with the results by . About 1 mag flux variations corresponded to the _ BR 1.5-2.0 range covered.

sources.summary: The paper describes Markarian 421 as a variable gamma-ray AGN observed by HAWC, using its nearby TeV photons as an opportunity to study EBL attenuation.

quotes.quote: Markarian 421 Markarian 421 (Mrk 421) is one of the most studied gamma-ray AGN as well as the one that shows high degree of variability. It has been observed by HAWC, which found a possible correlation between the TeV and X-ray activity .The fact that its distance from us is relatively short provides the opportunity to study the EBL using very high energy photons coming from it.

sources.summary: The paper reports HAWC archival detections of TeV blazar Markarian 421, highlighting its week-binned HAWC-111 light curve and significant flux variability during the observation.

quotes.quote: In archival HAWC data, the blazars Markarian 421 and 501 show substantial, significant flux variability . Figure mrk421 shows an example from this analysis, the long-term light curve of Markarian 421, in data from HAWC-111, binned in week-long time bins. During the HAWC observation, the blazar showed significant, substantial flux variability.

sources.summary: The paper treats Mrk 421 as one of the bright Markarian exceptions, placing it among HBL-like EHBLs with decreasing TeV flux and unusually detailed non-power-law TeV spectra.

quotes.quote: We notice that the two Markarians (Mrk 421 and Mrk 501 from now on) make an exception and are particularly bright also in this energy band.

Remembering that all sources in our sample are compatible within the errors with the definition of EHBL (meaning the synchrotron peak position above 10^ 17 Hz), we see here that some of them show their second IC peak in the 0.1-1.0 TeV band and with decreasing flux above 1 TeV (e.g. Mrk 501 and Mrk 421), but some others (e.g. the already known hard-TeV EHBLs 1ES 0229+200 and 1ES 0347-121) show an increasing flux up to the TeV regime, and without clear sign of peak before 10 TeV.

In tab:tevdetected , the TeV slope column reports the value of the slope in this band, and the TeV ^2 column indicates that all sources except for Mrk 421 This is expected because there is a very detailed spectrum available for this source, and the power-law fit is no more a good approximation even in a relatively small energy range. are now well modeled by the power-law fit in this band.

sources.summary: The paper analyzes Suzaku observations of the TeV blazar Mrk 421, finding large X-ray intra-day variability and harder-when-brighter behavior.

quotes.quote: Wiita Department of Physics, The College of New Jersey, 2000 Pennington Rd., Ewing, NJ 08628-0718, USA Tao An Shanghai Astronomical Observatory, Key Laboratory of Radio Astronomy, Chinese Academy of Sciences, 80 Nandan Road, Shanghai 200030, China Minfeng Gu Key Laboratory for Research in Galaxies and Cosmology, Shanghai Astronomical Observatory, Chinese Academy of Sciences, 80 Nandan Road, Shanghai 200030, China Dan Hu Department of Astronomy, Shanghai Jiao Tong University, 800 Dongchuan Road, Minhang, Shanghai 200240, China Haiguang Xu School of Physics and Astronomy, Shanghai Jiao Tong University, 800 Dongchuan Road, Minhang, Shanghai 200240, China Tsung-Dao Lee Institute, Shanghai Jiao Tong University, 800 Dongchuan Road, Minhang, Shanghai 200240, China IFSA Collaborative Innovation Center, Shanghai Jiao Tong University, 800 Dongchuan Road, Minhang, Shanghai 200240, China We present X-ray flux and spectral analyses of the three pointed Suzaku observations of the TeV high synchrotron peak blazar Mrk 421 taken throughout its complete operational duration.
We found large amplitude intra-day variability in all soft and hard bands in all the light curves.
The source is harder-when-brighter, following the general behavior of high synchrotron peak blazars.

sources.summary: The paper treats Mrk 421 as an archetypal TeV blazar whose data and SSC flare modeling constrain acceleration parameters and motivate typical physical parameter choices.

quotes.quote: Applying them to data from Mrk 421, they were able to characterize some acceleration parameters in that source.

Such physical parameters correspond to standard values as previously deduced with the same SSC packages and others for the archetypal TeV sources Mrk 501, Mrk 421, and PKS 2155-304 kat:2001,kat:2003,abr:2012 and can be considered as typical values expected in blazar flares.

A value m_ b = 1 was found adequate to reproduce flares observed in Mrk 421 within the SSC scenario adopted here kat:2003 , and m_ b = 2 corresponds for instance to the case of magnetic flux conservation in a blob propagating along a quasi-conical jet.

sources.summary: The paper uses ten activity phases of Mrk 421, an active high-synchrotron-peaked BL Lac blazar, to constrain ALP-photon oscillations in its gamma-ray spectra.

quotes.quote: In this work, we focus on the very high energy (VHE) ray observations of the blazar Markarian 421 (Mrk 421), which is one of the most widely studied and brightest sources in the extragalactic TeV sky with the redshift of z_0 = 0.031.
Mrk 421 is classified as a high synchrotron-peaked BL Lac object and is a very active blazar with major outbursts , which are composed of many short flares in both the X-ray and ray regions.

In this work, we consider the effect of the ALP-photon oscillation in the high energy ray spectra of Mrk 421 with these ten phases and combine the results of all the phases together to set constraint on the ALP parameter space.

sources.summary: The paper describes Mrk 421 as a nearby bright blazar used for long-term observations and calibration, with a continuum that has varied strongly over two decades.

quotes.quote: Mrk 421 ( l, b = 179.83 ^ , 65.03 ^ ) is one of the nearby ( z=0.031 ) brightest blazars in the sky.
For the past 20 years, it has been observed by multiple detectors and gratings of both as a target of opportunity (ToO) and as a calibration source.
The continuum of Mrk 421 has varied remarkably over 20 years, both in shape and normalization.

sources.summary: The paper reports that Mrk 421 has a transient QPO candidate near 300 days, compatible with a previously reported 280-day oscillation and showing possible period change.

quotes.quote: Mrk 421: reported an oscillating ray emission at 280 d, compatible with our QPO candidate at 300 64 d ( 300 65 d) with significance exceeding 5 in both time binnings. This QPO candidate is also not a persistent one, being seen between MJD 55800 and MJD 57000 approximately (August 2011 to December 2014). In our analysis, both maps show a complex structure, with a possible period change at the end.

sources.summary: The paper uses Mkn 421 as a Swift-XRT blazar case study and finds its characteristic photon-energy break is not readily explained as a simple radiative cooling break.

quotes.quote: Though these scenarios are in principle capable of reproducing the correlation results, they deviate significantly from the observed properties. galaxies: active -- BL Lacertae objects: individual: Mkn 421 -- X-rays: galaxies -- galaxies: jets -- radiation mechanisms: non-thermal The BL Lac object Mkn 421 is one of the nearest ( z=0.031 ) and extensively studied blazars with a non-thermal spectrum extending from radio to very high energy gamma rays 2009ApJ...703..169A, 2011ApJ...736..131A,2012A&A...542A.100A,2016ApJ...819..156B,2016ApJS..222....6B .
We analysed the Swift XRT 2005SSRv..120..165B observations of Mkn 421 during 2008--2019.

Using a large number of Swift-XRT observations corresponding to different flux states, we have show here that a simple cooling break interpretation for the characteristic photon energy of Mkn 421 is inconsistent with the observations.

sources.summary: The paper uses Mkn421 as a nearby, well-measured TeV BL Lac; its optical depths are higher than common models, and it helps constrain EBL SED upper limits.

quotes.quote: To get lower statistics and the FIR limits due to a pile-up at energies, we use two near and well-measured BL Lacs, Mkn421 and Mkn501.

In addition, from Table Table:1 , we find that the optical depths obtained by MKn421(z=0.03) and Mkn501(z=0.03) are systematically higher than those obtained by the common models and the observations.
We summarize the results as following: (1) Four TeV BL Lacs of MKn421, MKn501, 1ES1101-232, and 1ES0229+200 can well constrain the upper limits of EBL SED.

sources.summary: The paper uses Mrk 421 as a TeV blazar benchmark, noting observed spectra, EGRET/TeV indices, and multiwaveband observations showing correlated X-ray/TeV variability.

quotes.quote: This is inconsistent with the observed spectra in TeV blazars like Mrk 501 or Mrk 421 (see below).

The spectral indices expected from the narrow cascades below and above the opacity break ( narrow ) are consistent with the typical EGRET and TeV indices, respectively, observed in Mrk 421 and Mrk 501 .

Variability and correlated flares Flares and the quiescent background A major result of the simultaneous multiwaveband observations of Mrk 421 and Mrk 501 was that their variability in the X-ray and TeV-regime is largely correlated, and stronger than in other wavebands.

sources.summary: The review identifies Markarian (Mrk) 421 as the first TeV blazar detected and discusses how its GeV/TeV spectrum was used to test CIB attenuation.

quotes.quote: The first extragalactic ray source detected at TeV energies was the blazar Markarian (Mrk) 421, located at redshift z 0.031 (Punch et al 1992).

In the first applications of this technique, Stecker De Jager (1993), De Jager et al (1994), and Dwek Slavin (1994), combined GeV observations of Mrk 421 obtained by the Energetic Gamma Ray Experiment Telescope (EGRET) on board the Compton Gamma--Ray Observatory ( CGRO ) (Lin et al 1992), with TeV observations obtained by the Whipple Observatory (Punch et al 1992), to infer the intrinsic energy distribution of the source, and to search for evidence of a high energy cutoff in its spectrum due to attenuation.

The problems of ascertaining reliable evidence for intergalactic attenuation were illustrated by Biller et al (1995), who showed two equally acceptable fits to the spectrum of Mrk 421, the first requiring no attenuation, the second requiring significant CIB attenuation.

sources.summary: The paper cites Mrk 421 as an HBL whose 1994 May flare spectral evolution and simultaneous gamma-ray fluxes suggest SSC cooling dominated during the flare.

quotes.quote: During the 1994 May X-ray/TeV flare of the HBL Mrk 421, the X-ray energy spectral index was seen to evolve from = 1.3 during the rise of the flare to a maximum value of = 1.5 as the flux diminished (Takahashi et al. \ 1996). This spectral evolution was attributed to synchrotron cooling, but the extremal value of = 1.5 suggests that SSC cooling may be the primary mechanism. Nearly simultaneous ray fluxes measured at 100 MeV through TeV energies seem to indicate that the SSC luminosity was indeed greater than the synchrotron luminosity during this flare episode (Macomb et al. \ 1995).

sources.summary: The paper identifies B2 1101+38 as a nearby BL Lac object with a detected central optical source and an optical excess relative to the FR I radio-galaxy correlation.

quotes.quote: Of the 18 unresolved optical sources, 2 are associated to B2 1615+32 (3C 332) and B2 1833+32 (3C 382), which are both Broad Line Radio Galaxies with a clear FR II radio morphology (Chiaberge et al. , ); as expected, we also detected a central source in B2 1101+38 (Mrk 421), a well known nearby BL Lac object.

The only B2 sources which significantly deviate are B2 1615+32, B2 1833+32 and B2 1101+38 which, as mentioned above, are two BLRG FR II and a BL Lac. This agrees with the results of Chiaberge et al. ( , CCCG00) that both BLRG and BL Lacs (in particular those belonging to the high energy peak class Instead of classifying BL Lacs according to their selection spectral band, we adopt the definitions of high and low energy peaked BL Lacs (HBL and LBL, respectively), which are based on the position of the (synchrotron) emission peak in the spectrum and therefore more indicative of the physical characteristics of the objects (Giommi Padovani , Fossati et al. ) , as it is for B2 1101+38) present a large optical excess with respect to FR I galaxies.

sources.summary: The paper treats Mrk 421 as a TeV blazar whose proton-synchrotron fits, rapid variability, X-ray-correlated TeV flares, and BL Lac gamma-ray detection constrain emission models.

quotes.quote: Aharonian (2000), Protheroe M ucke (2000), and M ucke Protheroe (2000) have shown that for a reasonable set of parameters characterizing the small-scale (sub-parsec) jets in Mrk 421 and Mrk 501, the synchrotron radiation of extremely high energy protons not only may dominate over other possible radiative and non-radiative losses, but also could provide adequate fits to the observed TeV spectra of both objects.

Variability at the highest energies (TeV) ranges from less than 1 hr (flare on 1996 May 15 of Mrk 421) to long high states lasting several months (Mrk 501 in 1997); see Aharonian (1999) and Catanese Weekes (1999) for reviews.

Multi-wavelength campaigns revealed that the TeV flares of both, Mrk 501 (see, e.g., Pian et al. 1998; Catanese et al. 1997; Aharonian et al. 2001; Sambruna et al. 2000) and Mrk 421 (see, e.g., Buckley et al. 1996; Maraschi et al. 1999; Takahashi et al. 1999, also Rebillot et al. 2003) were correlated with X-ray emission.

sources.summary: The paper cites Mrk 421 as a TeV blazar whose jet-dominated emission resembles low/hard-state X-ray binaries, with hard lags, a positive hardness/flux correlation, and hysteresis in its hardness response.

quotes.quote: Within the jet/disk picture of MarkoffFalckeFender2001 , TeV Blazars like Mrk 421 or Fanaroff-Riley class I radio galaxies ( FanaroffRiley1974 , the unbeamed parent population of BL Lacs within the unified scheme, PadovaniUrry1995 ) show many features of BHXRBs in the low/hard state, namely a domination of the spectral energy distribution by jet emission.

Mrk 421, for example, shows hard lags and a positive hardness/flux correlation Zhang2002 . The hardness seems to show a hysteresis effect, e.g. the power law index seems to respond slightly after the variation of the total intensity.

sources.summary: The paper reports significant TeV excess from Markarian 421 during a high state, identifies Mrk 421 as an active transient source, and detects it near its nominal position.

quotes.quote: From 0^ to 60^ in declination, significant excesses from the well-known steady source Crab Nebula and the high state of the flare type source Markarian 421 are observed.
The dominant contributions are due to the stable ray source Crab and transient source Mrk 421 which was in an active state from Feb. 2000 through Oct. 2001 .

As can be seen in Table 1, two established sources Crab and Mrk 421 are detected in an angular distance not larger than 0.5^ from their nominal positions, in agree with the 0.4^ positional uncertainty estimated for the both methods.

sources.summary: The paper treats Mkn 421 as an extensively observed XMM-Newton calibration target and selects six small-window EPIC PN observations for homogeneous analysis.

quotes.quote: This is one of three sources in the present sample that has been extensively observed, being a calibration target (the others are 3C 273 and PKS 2155-304 ). 32 observations are present in the XMM-Newton data archive, but for sake of homogeneity in the present analysis, we selected only the 6 observations with EPIC PN in small window mode. We refer the reader to the several papers published with more detailed analysis of Mkn 421 data with different observing modes (e.g. Brinkmann et al. 2001, 2003, Sembay et al. 2002, Ravasio et al. 2004).

sources.summary: The paper uses Mrk 421 as a BL Lac example whose 1994 flare showed correlated X-ray/TeV behavior, energy-dependent X-ray lags, and a distinctive spectral-index/flux trajectory.

quotes.quote: A general trend is becoming apparent in blazar studies (see Wagner Wagner97 1997 and Shrader Wehrle sw97 1997 for reviews): variability of optical and > 100 MeV emission in flat spectrum sources such as 3C 279 (e.g., Hartman et al. Hartman96 1996) and BL Lacertae (Bloom et al. Bloom97 1997), and of X-ray and TeV emission in the BL Lac objects Mrk 421 and Mrk 501 (e.g., Macomb et al. Macomb95 1995; Buckley et al. Buckley96 1996; Catanese et al. Catanese97 1997) appears to be temporally correlated, implying that the same population of electrons produces both the optical/X-ray and the 100 MeV - TeV emission. In the specific case of the 1994 May flare of Mrk 421, ASCA observations (Takahashi et al. Takahashi96 1996) also show that the time lag of photons in the 0.5-2.0 keV range relative to 5 keV X-rays varies as E( keV )^ 1/2 5^ 1/2 . Moreover, the Mrk 421 flare data follow a well-defined trajectory in a spectral index/flux display, and this behavior is also found in several other BL Lac objects (e.g., OJ 287, Idesawa et al. Idesawa97 1997; PKS 2155-304, Sembay et al. Sembay93 1993)

gmw_00000358.name: NGC 3077
gmw_00000358.overview: NGC 3077 is an irregular dwarf starburst galaxy [arXiv:astro-ph/0501528, arXiv:1801.04328] in the interacting M81 group [arXiv:1002.0456, arXiv:2401.16002]. Gravitational encounters have stripped most of its atomic gas into an extended tidal arm called the Garland [arXiv:1808.02840, arXiv:1507.04889], which contains young star clusters and massive molecular complexes [arXiv:1507.04889, arXiv:astro-ph/0008347].

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 3077 as part of the interacting M 81 triple, with HI tidal arms and dust/CO structures whose emission is partly tidal and partly Galactic foreground.

quotes.quote: NGC 3077 is the smallest member of the interacting M 81 galaxy triple. Intense HI tidal arms have been found surrounding this galaxy (Yun et al. ). Towards these arms Walter Heithausen ( ) have detected a giant molecular complex. Walter et al. ( ) have used the SPIRE instrument to search for the thermal emission of dust asociated with the tidal arms.

Towards NGC 3077 the situation is more complex: here we are faced with one molecular cloud of Galactic and another molecular complex of extragalactic origin. Both show up in all SPIRE bands with similar color ratios. Our incomplete CO map presented in this paper shows that the Galactic cloud SAMS1 is probably more extended than previously thought. This suggests that even more of the dust seen towards NGC 3077 is located in the Galactic foreground.

sources.summary: The paper detects diffuse X-ray emission in NGC 3077, links its hot coronal gas to a galactic wind, and says cooler neutral material still contains the hot gas.

quotes.quote: We present a comprehensive comparison of the X-ray properties of a sample of eight dwarf starburst galaxies observed with Chandra (I Zw 18, VII Zw 403, NGC 1569, NGC 3077, NGC 4214, NGC 4449, NGC 5253, He 2--10). In Paper I we presented in detail the data reduction and analysis of the individual galaxies. For the unresolved X-ray sources we find the following: point sources are in general located close to bright \ regions, rims of superbubbles, or young stellar clusters.

Of the eight dwarf starburst galaxies we detect diffuse X-ray emission in all but two objects (detected in NGC 1569, NGC 3077, NGC 4214, NGC 4449, NGC 5253, and He 2--10) which can be attributed to hot, coronal gas stored in galactic winds (Paper I). The X-ray emission of NGC 1569 extends well beyond its optical and \ counterparts perpendicular to the disc. The situation is different for the other five galaxies: The X-ray, \ (see Fig. fig:profileboth ), and \ emission of He 2-10 are all fairly circularly shaped and have about the same size. Hot gas is almost entirely bound to the central region of NGC 4214 (note that the X-ray emission to the north has low significance and might be an artefact from the adaptive smoothing process). The optical and X-ray emission of NGC 3077 and NGC 4449 is surrounded by an extended \ envelope which can be ascribed to tidal debris due to a current interaction see, e.g., yun94,hun98 .

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 3077 as a dwarf star-forming disk companion whose close interaction with M81 helped strip gas and shape tidal features in the M81 group.

quotes.quote: The observed gas distribution throughout the nearby M81 group of galaxies shows evidence for past significant galaxy--galaxy interactions but without a merger having occurred. This group is here studied for possible dynamical solutions within the dark-matter standard model. In order to cover a comprehensive set of initial conditions, the inner three core members M81, M82 and NGC 3077 are treated as a three-body model based on Navarro-Frenk-White profiles. The possible orbits of these galaxies are examined statistically taking into account dynamical friction.

The M81 group of galaxies is comprised of a major Milky-Way type galaxy (M81, baryonic mass M_ M81    3   10^ 10 M_ ) and four companion galaxies, two of which are themselves dwarf but nevertheless substantial star-forming disk galaxies (M82, M_ M82    1   10^ 10 M_ and NGC 3077, M_ N3077    2   10^ 9 M_ ). The interesting observational finding is that these galaxies are enshrouded by HI-emitting gas spanning the dimensions of the inner group which is about 50-100 kpc. This requires the galaxy pairs M81/M82 and M81/NGC 3077 to have interacted closely at least once, in order to strip the outer gaseous disks and rearrange the matter in the long tidal features ( Cottrell1977 , Gottesman1977 , Hulst1979 , Yun1993 and Yun1994 ).

sources.summary: The paper maps extended molecular gas in tidal arms near NGC 3077, finding massive bound CO complexes likely formed in situ and possibly marking dwarf-galaxy formation from interaction debris.

quotes.quote: Using the IRAM 30 m radio telescope we have mapped the tidal arm feature south--east of NGC 3077 where we recently detected molecular gas in the CO ( J =1 0) and (2 1) transitions. We find that the molecular gas is much more extended than previously thought (several kpc). The CO emission can be separated into at least 3 distinct complexes with equivalent radii between 250 pc and 700 pc -- the newly detected complexes therefore range among the largest molecular complexes in the local universe.

Our new observations have revealed extensive molecular gas in the tidal arms near NGC 3077. The CO emission is much more extended than previously thought -- the detected complexes range among the most extended complexes in the local universe. We have detected at least three independent complexes of molecular gas. The complexes are most probably gravitationally bound objects and have formed in situ .

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 3077 as an interacting M81-group galaxy whose star formation has ejected gas and whose nearby filament/cloud structures fit tidal-interaction debris.

quotes.quote: Recently, wal02 have shown that episodes of star formation in the dwarf galaxy NGC 3077 have disrupted and ejected the gas outside of the region where the stars are optically identified. Our observations cover a large angular area around this galaxy (as well as others in the group) to search for these blown out components.

Cloud 2 Cloud 2 sits immediately to the south-east of NGC 3077, at a distance of 23 kpc. It is a distinct clump of situated within a filamentary structure that extends from NGC3077 south and west, encompassing the dwarf galaxies BK5N, IKN, and K64.
