The cloud is in the upper range of the filament velocity. The morphology of the filament to the west of the cloud suggests that it may be plunging into NGC3077.

A useful insight on whether the newly discovered clouds are part of the system of tidal debris produced by the recent collisions involving M81, M82, and NGC 3077 can be obtained by comparing their observed locations and kinematics with the results of a numerical simulation of the tidal interactions in this group.

sources.summary: The paper places NGC 3077 in the interacting M81 group, connected to M81 by a southern H I tidal arm with young clumps and modeled recent interaction.

quotes.quote: To do so, we have obtained deep and wide-field optical imaging data of the nearby early-type spiral M81 (NGC 3031), resolving stars well below the tip of the red giant branch of metal-poor stellar populations. The M81 group of galaxies is one of the nearest groups to our own. It contains one large spiral, two peculiar galaxies (M82 and NGC 3077), two small spirals galaxies (NGC 2976 and IC 2574), as well as a large number of dwarf galaxies e.g. karachentsev85, karachentsev01, chiboucas08 . The core galaxies of the group are strongly interacting. Atomic hydrogen observations have revealed the presence of a large number of tidal streams with large, dynamically complex atomic hydrogen clouds embedding M81, M82, NGC 3077, and NGC 2976 vdh79,appleton81,yun94,boyce01 .

More strikingly, a number of previously unknown overdensities of young star candidates can be seen along the tidal bridge of gas stretching from M81 to its neighbour NGC 3077. Interestingly, BK3N and Garland appear to lie at the two extremities of this chain of concentrations of young stars, close to M81 and NGC 3077 respectively.
Detailed modelling of the dynamics of the M81 group suggests that the three largest galaxies in the system had an interaction 250 Myr ago for M81 and NGC 3077, and 200 Myr ago for M82 and M81 yun99 .

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 3077 as tidally disturbed, with displaced H I in the Garland, young stars near its debris, an extended RGB halo, and streams linked to M81 and M82.

quotes.quote: Our survey also reveals for the first time the very extended ( 2 ) halos of RGB stars around M81, M82 and NGC 3077, as well as faint tidal streams that link these systems. The halos of M82 and NGC 3077 exhibit highly disturbed morphologies, presumably a consequence of the recent gravitational encounter and their ongoing disruption. While the halos of M81, NGC 3077 and the inner halo of M82 have the similar colors, the outer halo of M82 is significantly bluer indicating it is more metal-poor.

In NGC 3077, 90 of the atomic hydrogen is located eastward of the center, in the tidal arm called ``the Garland" where young stars have been observed Karachentsev1985MNRAS, Sakai2001ApJ, Weisz2008ApJ . Near NGC 3077, many MSs are found in the Garland and up to about 8 kpc to the south and 10 kpc to the east where HI gas and dust emission have been observed Walter2011ApJ .
In the contour map of red RGBs in Figure fig: map_cont , a tidal stream between M81 and M82 can clearly be seen, and the outer regions of M82 and NGC 3077 exhibit an ``S-shaped" morphology.

sources.summary: The paper studies CO(2-1) gas conditions and estimates magnetic-field strengths for NGC 3077, reporting outskirts temperatures, columns, H2 masses, and 6-31 G field strengths for the spiral-galaxy sample.

quotes.quote: Based on CO(2-1) public data, we study the monoxide oxygen gas excitation conditions and the magnetic field strength of NGC 2841, NGC 3077, NGC 3184, NGC 3351 spiral galaxies. For their galaxy outskirts, we found kinetic temperatures in the range of 35 - 38 K , CO column densities 10^ 15 10^ 16 cm^ 2 and H2 masses of 4 10^6 - 6 10^8 M_ .

In this work, we study the magnetic field strength in the outskirts of four spiral galaxies: NGC 2841, NGC 3077, NGC 3184, NGC 3351, following a different approach to those commonly based on Faraday rotation, dust polarization, synchrotron emission, and so on.

In the present contribution, we have estimated the magnetic field strength in the galaxy nuclei and in the outskirts of NGC 2841, NGC 3077, NGC 3184, NGC 3351 spiral galaxies. For that, we have used an approximated expression of the magneto-hydrodynamics to find an upper limit for the magnetic field magnitudes. The magnetic field strength lies within the range of 6-31 G, which are in good agreement with the values provided by other author for spiral galaxies.

sources.summary: The paper maps extensive molecular gas in tidal arms near NGC 3077, linking the complexes to past interactions and possible future dwarf-galaxy formation from stripped enriched material.

quotes.quote: Using the IRAM 30 m radio telescope we have mapped the tidal arm feature south--east of NGC 3077 where we recently detected molecular gas in the CO ( J =1 0) and (2 1) transitions. We find that the molecular gas is much more extended than previously thought (several kpc). The CO emission can be separated into at least 3 distinct complexes with equivalent radii between 250 pc and 700 pc and all well confined over a narrow range in velocity -- the newly detected complexes therefore range among the largest molecular complexes in the local universe.

Evidently, the galaxies NGC 3077, M 81, and M 82 have undergone some violent interaction in the past. Visible signs of this interaction are the long tidal arms seen in the 21 cm line of atomic hydrogen (van der Hulst , Yun et al. \ ) connecting the galaxies. According to numerical simulations the closest encounter between NGC 3077 and M 81, which presumably redistributed much of the interstellar gas in the outskirts of NGC 3077, happened some 3 10^8 years ago (Yun et al. ).

sources.summary: UGC 5398 is presented as a cautionary case whose unusual line-emission plumes and bubble-like structures may reflect supershell or extended nuclear emission processes.

quotes.quote: Finally, we include UGC 5398 (Fig. Figf ) as a cautionary example of the difficulties in interpreting H emission in a uniform manner. The morphology of the line emission in UGC 5398 is very different to that of most of the sample, showing plumes and possible bubble structures. This galaxy was interpreted by Martin ( ) as an example of the `supershell' phenomenon, where large numbers of supernovae in a short time interval create large-scale galactic winds, which blow shell structures of this types.

However, this morphology is also similar to the Extended Nuclear Emission-line Regions (ENERs) found by Hameed Devereux ( ) in H imaging of the central regions of early type galaxies, which they ascribe to gas that is either shock-ionized or photoionized by UV radiation from bulge post asymptotic giant branch stars. This, and the likely contribution of AGN line emission from the central regions of at least some of our sample galaxies, are reminders that not all line emission need be related to star formation.

sources.summary: The paper finds cold, enriched dust and gas in NGC 3077's tidal arm, arguing that interaction with M 81 and M 82 stripped pre-enriched ISM from the galaxy.

quotes.quote: We present the detection of extended ( 30 kpc ^2 ) dust emission in the tidal \ arm near NGC 3077 (member of the M 81 triplet) using SPIRE on board Herschel . Dust emission in the tidal arm is typically detected where the \ column densities are > 10 ^ 21 cm ^ 2 . The SPIRE band ratios show that the dust in the tidal arm is significantly colder ( 13 K) than in NGC 3077 itself ( 31 K), consistent with the lower radiation field in the tidal arm.

We conclude that the interstellar medium (atomic , molecules and dust) in this tidal feature was pre--enriched and stripped off NGC 3077 during its recent interaction ( 3 10 ^8 yr ago) with M 82 and M 81. This implies that interaction can efficiently remove heavy elements and enriched material (dust, molecular gas) from galaxies.

sources.summary: The paper studies NGC 3077 as a tidally affected triplet member, finding it dust deficient and unusually high in dust-to-gas ratio compared with isolated spirals.

quotes.quote: To investigate possible effects of the environment on the dust of a nearby spiral galaxy, we study the dust content and the dust-to-gas ratio of the galaxy NGC 3077, that is part of a galaxy triplet and therefore affected by tidal interactions. For comparison purposes, we include in our sample the spiral galaxies NGC 2841, NGC 3184 and NGC 3351, which we have considered as isolated galaxies. The positions, morphology and distances of the galaxies of our sample are given in Table tab1 .

We found dust masses in the range of 6.5--9.1 10 ^7 M _ for NGC 2841, NGC 3184 and NGC 3351. NGC 3077 has a dust mass that is a factor of 10 lower than the dust masses of the other galaxies included in our study. NGC 3077 is the nearest galaxy in our sample and its average size (observed at 70 m) is a factor of 4.2--4.8 smaller than those (observed at 70 m) of NGC 2841, NGC 3184 and NGC 3351. Based on this fact, the dust mass of NGC 3077 is expected to be a factor of 4.2--4.8 lower than the other galaxies, which contrasts with what has been previously estimated. This result implies that NGC 3077 is dust deficient, which may be caused by the tidal interactions that this galaxy suffers. Dust deficiency is also observed in cluster galaxies .

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 3077 as an optically smooth, actively star-forming galaxy stripped of much of its gas, with nearby gas spread across velocities and molecular gas outside its bright body.

quotes.quote: The third galaxy is NGC 3077, an optically smooth galaxy with an actively starforming core, which has been stripped of most of its (e.g., walter02b ). Much of this is now found immediately to the east of the main galaxy as part of the ``Garland'' feature (e.g., yun93a,walter11 ).

In contrast, the high values in the immediate proximity of NGC 3077 are intrinsic again, and indicate the presence of a gas component spread over a large range in velocity, as shown by the feature in slice 4 (Fig. \ fig:slices ) at 0.55^ .

The CO in NGC 3077 is discussed in walter02b and meier01 . As is the case with the as well, much of the molecular gas associated with NGC 3077 is found outside the optically bright part of the galaxy walter99,walter06 .

sources.summary: The paper presents NGC 3077 as one of the main M81-group galaxies, with most of its atomic gas displaced into a tidal arm after interaction with M81.

quotes.quote: The smallest of the three galaxies, NGC 3077, was found to have lost its gas during a close encounter with M81 e.g., Brouillet1991,Thomasson1993,Yun1993 and high resolution observations show that as much as 90 of the galaxy's atomic gas is situated in a prominent tidal arm Walter2002a .

There seems to be a velocity continuity between the western side of IC 2574, the gas cloud HIJASS J1021+68, and the region of the system containing NGC 3077 and the eastern bridge of the system. This, together with the fact that there exists a peak of an apparent cloud in the space between HIJASS J1021+68 and the NGC 3077, may suggest that IC 2574 is not completely isolated from the system.

These interactions have caused the truncation of the M82 disk Sofue1998 and the displacement of the atomic and molecular gas of NGC 3077 from its stellar component Walter2002a . These effects suggest that the interactions between the galaxies are violent, and they are thought to be the origin of the increased star formation rates in M81, M82 and NGC 3077 Yun1999 , in agreement with the idea proposed by Toomre1972 that such tidal disruptions could enhance starburst or AGN activity in the interacting galaxies.

sources.summary: The paper identifies NGC 3077 as a tidally disrupted M81 satellite around which the new ultrafaint candidates cluster, suggesting possible satellite-of-satellite accretion.

quotes.quote: Intriguingly, all of these candidates are spatially clustered around NGC 3077, which is itself an M81 group satellite in an advanced state of tidal disruption. This is somewhat surprising, as M81 itself and its largest satellite M82 are both substantially more massive than NGC 3077 and by virtue of their greater masses, would have been expected to host as many or more ultrafaint candidates.

Yet, instead, they are clustered around NGC 3077. One immediate implication is that most of these candidates are likely to be real --- our COSMOS blank-field study shows that spurious candidates are more spatially uniform.
In NGC 3077's field, excluding NGC 3077, there are five known fainter satellites and five new candidates (10 total).

sources.summary: The paper finds centrally concentrated H emission, filamentary outer emission, and a southern chain of blue-star associations and HII regions associated with tidal material near NGC 3077.

quotes.quote: NGC 3077 and Garland . The H emission in NGC 3077 shows strong concentration towards the galaxy center, where dust clouds also occured. Closer to the outskirts the emission has a filament structure looking like crab's claws. At the southern periphery of NGC 3077 a chain of associations of blue stars and HII regions is seen, which was called ``Carland'' (Karachentseva et al. \ 1985, Karachentsev et al. \ 1985). With this unusual structure which is likely to be of tidal origin (Makarova et al. \ 2003), neutral hydrogen filament and molecular clouds are associated ( Yun et al. 1994, Walter et al. \ 2002).

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 3077 as part of the M 81 triplet interaction, whose passage and tidal bridge may have helped produce the south-eastern HI cloud debris.

quotes.quote: Possible explanations for these free--floating HI clouds are that they are related to the dSphs found to the South--East of M 81, that they belong to the galaxies of the M 81 triplet (equivalent to HVCs), that they are of primordial nature and provide fresh, unenriched material falling into the M 81 group, or that they are tidal debris from the 3--body interaction involving M 81--M 82--NGC 3077.

using the 76--m Lowell telescope and later mapped the large--scale HI distribution in the group, tracing the complex tidal tails and bridges resulting from the 3--body interaction between M 81, M 82, and NGC 3077.

The numerical simulation published by shows material from M 81 being dragged out by the passage of NGC 3077 and spread out towards the South--East. It therefore seems likely that the HI clouds found there are, in fact, tidal debris.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 3077 as a low-luminosity irregular companion of M81, showing previous interaction signs but no SED requirement for an AGN.

quotes.quote: NGC 3077 : NGC 3077 is a low-luminosity irregular galaxy. We measure the AGN contribution at 34 , which is not consistent with results from her16 and L13. NGC 3077 is a fairly isolated companion of NGC 3031 = M81, 46 away kna14 , indicating it is in the earliest stages of merging. It does show signs of previous galaxy interaction, but nothing in the SED requires an AGN.

sources.summary: The paper discusses NGC3077 as a peculiar M81 Group galaxy in the interacting central triplet, with tidal bridges, stripped gas, and linked stellar-halo debris.

quotes.quote: It contains more than 40 member galaxies, including the large Sb spiral galaxy M81, the peculiar galaxies M82 and NGC3077, 9 late-type galaxies, at least 20 low-luminosity early-type dwarfs and a variety of stellar debris features, some of which are tidal dwarf galaxy candidates. Numerical simulations by 1999IAUS..186...81Y successfully reproduced the HI tidal features if the closest encounters of M82 and NGC 3077 to M81 had taken place 220 and 280 Myr ago, respectively.

Tidal interactions between M81, M82, and NGC3077 have stripped gas from the main bodies of these systems and a number of young stellar clumps and streams are known to coincide with the densest concentrations of neutral hydrogen gas 1985MNRAS.217..731K, 2004IAUS..217...90D, 2005ApJ...630L.133S, 2008PASP..120.1145D, 2009MNRAS.399..737M, 2013AJ....146..126C, 2015ApJ...809L...1O .

Early highlights from this survey include the discovery of very extended (and rather perturbed) stellar halos around M81, M82 and NGC3077 as well as an enormous tidal stream which links the three systems 2015ApJ...809L...1O .

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC 3077 to demonstrate MaNGaL imaging, connecting its dust lanes with ionized gas filaments and identifying about twenty compact emission objects as planetary-nebula candidates.

quotes.quote: NGC 3077. The dust lanes in optical images of this dwarf starburst galaxy in the Messier 81 group are related to the ionized gas filaments . Our image (total exposure of 900 sec) reveals all structural features appeared in one of deepest images in the literature . In contrast to the image, the emission map revealed about twenty compact emission objects. The observed / ratio allows us to consider the most of them as planetary nebulae (PNe) candidates.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 3077 as an irregular member of the triplet and finds few old-GC-like candidates near it, likely because its clusters are younger.

quotes.quote: NGC 3077 is an irregular galaxy located at 3.8 Mpc ( tully2013 ) with a baryonic and halo mass of 2 10^ 10 M_ and 5 10^ 11 M_ respectively ( oehm2017 ). Its foreground extinction is of 0.14 in the -band ( Schlafly_Finkbeiner2011 ). investigates the near-infrared photometric properties of NGC 3077 through the Canada-France-Hawaii Telescope and studies the star clusters candidates of NGC 3077 through HST/ACS broad (F300W, F547M, and F814W) and narrow-band (F487N and F656N) filters.
Moreover, we do not see many GC-like objects close to NGC 3077.

Interestingly, we do not recover a significant population of GC-like objects around NGC 3077, probably due to the fact that the star cluster population of such galaxy is not made of old GCs, but of younger objects, whose SEDs are not part of our training sample.

sources.summary: NGC 3077 is treated as an interacting M81-group member; the paper notes its central old-cluster candidates and includes it in tentative globular-cluster assignments.

quotes.quote: However, M81 is currently interacting with M82 and NGC 3077 and will merge with them in the next few Gyr, likely resulting in a system with a large, metal-rich stellar halo similar to M31's Smercina20 .
Davidge2004 identified some candidate old GCs and young star clusters in the central parts of NGC 3077 based on near-infrared colours and brightness.

We start with a discussion of M81 group GC radial velocities --- M82 globular clusters clearly stand out from the rest and motivate a tentative assignment of candidate GCs to M81, M82 or NGC 3077. Given the maturity of the published measurements of M81's GC population, we compile what we consider to be a trustworthy catalogue of M81 candidates and a supplementary list of literature GCs that are likely to be contaminants; M82's and NGC 3077's cluster populations are less well-studied, and we do not attempt to compile a catalogue of their GCs beyond our own.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 3077 as an interacting major/bright M 81 group member, included among massive dwarfs and associated with clustered dwarf irregular velocities suggesting possible group infall.

quotes.quote: The central region of the group is characterized by three major galaxies (M81, M82 and NGC 3077) that are interacting with each other, as demonstrated by a complex HI network of filaments, tidal tails and candidate tidal dwarf galaxies 1994Natur.372..530Y,2008AJ....135.1983C,2008ApJ...689..160W .

This effectively removes the two gravitationally dominant galaxies M 81 and M 82 from the list, but still includes the bright galaxies NGC 2976 (with M_r=-18.0 mag) and NGC 3077 (with M_r=-17.8 mag). M 81 and M 82 have stellar luminosities (derived from the K_s band) of 10^ 10.95 M _ and 10^ 10.59 M _ 2013AJ....145..101K , respectively. To date, the 2013AJ....146..126C data represents the most complete survey of the M 81 group and its surroundings, going beyond the second turnaround radius of M 81 (which is 230 kpc). Because 2013AJ....146..126C do not compile the distances of the literature data, we have taken the values from the online version of the Local Volume catalog 2013AJ....145..101K https://relay.sao.ru/lv/lvgdb/, last accessed 15.09.2022. . A recent deep survey by 2022ApJ...937L...3B uncovered another six ultra-faint dwarf galaxies, which are clustered around NGC 3077.

Comparing the flattening and motion of the M 81 dwarf galaxy system with TNG50 of the IllustrisTNG suite we find good agreement between observations and simulations, but caution that i) velocity information of half of the satellite population is still missing, ii) current velocities are coming mainly from dwarf irregulars clustered around NGC 3077, which may hint towards an infall of a dwarf galaxy group and iii) some of the dwarfs in our sample may actually be tidal dwarf galaxies.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC3077 as a dwarf starburst with 100-300 Myr extended, spatially chaotic cluster formation, clusters as old as 300 Myr, and lower inferred velocity dispersion.

quotes.quote: The two dwarf galaxies, NGC3077 and NGC5253, show time--extended star formation, spanning the last 100--300 Myr, and a very `chaotic' spatial distribution of the cluster ages. This similarity between the two galaxies is counterbalanced by striking differences. In NGC3077, there are star clusters as old as 300 Myr, while in NGC5253 the oldest clusters unambiguously detected in the HST images do not seem to be significantly older than 20 Myr ( ).
A smaller velocity dispersion may be present in NGC3077, as inferred from molecular cloud velocities ( ), implying longer survival times for its clusters.

sources.summary: The paper reports diffuse X-ray emission spanning about 1 kpc in NGC 3077 and six point sources, including thermal-plasma SNR/bubble candidates, power-law sources, and one supersoft source.

quotes.quote: Chandra observations revisited The diffuse X-ray emission of NGC 3077 has an extent of 1 which corresponds to about 1 kpc (see (Fig. fig:olay3077 a ). Six point sources are detected within the optical and \ extent of NGC 3077 (Fig. fig:point3077 ): Three sources (P2, P3, and P4) are well modeled by thermal plasmas and are likely young supernova remnants or small bubbles filled with hot gas. Two (P5 and P6) show power law spectra and one (P1) is a supersoft source in the outskirts of NGC 3077. Only P1, P4, and P6, however, exceed our threshold for spectral fitting (see Tables tab:point3077a and tab:point3077b for the point source properties).

sources.summary: The paper identifies NGC 3077 as the nearest sample galaxy, an interacting system with unusual dust morphology, excess optical attenuation relative to dust emission, and younger stellar ages in star-forming regions.

quotes.quote: Three galaxies, NGC 2798, NGC 5713 and NGC 3077, have regions that even given the estimated errors would appear to be forbidden by a foreground screen model, having significantly more optical attenuation than would be expected given the amount of dust detected in emission.
NGC 3077, on the other hand, is the nearest galaxy in our sample, however it is also part of an interacting system and is well known for its unusual dust morphology Walter2011 .
NGC 3077 does show some evidence for lower stellar ages in regions of high star formation, with large scatter.

gmw_00000359.name: NGC 2841
gmw_00000359.overview: NGC 2841 is an isolated, fast-rotating Sb spiral galaxy [arXiv:1709.05280, arXiv:1411.5792] characterized by a flocculent spiral structure [arXiv:0810.2116] and a classical bulge [arXiv:1004.5393]. It features a gigantic, warped outer stellar disk that extends beyond its gas disk [arXiv:2109.00686, arXiv:1802.02583], likely shaped by co-planar satellite accretion [arXiv:2109.00686].

sources.summary: The thesis chapter finds NGC 2841 has a gigantic low-surface-brightness stellar disk extending beyond HI and UV observations, remaining more massive than gas and likely shaped by co-planar satellite accretion.

quotes.quote: In Chapter 3, I present a study of the outer disk of the nearby spiral galaxy NGC 2841 using Dragonfly (in Sloan g and bands) as well as archival data in UV from the Galaxy Evolution Explorer Satellite and rest frame 21 cm data using the Very Large Array. While it is commonly accepted that gas dominates over stars in galaxy outer disks, I find that in NGC 2841, this is not the case. The stellar disk extends to five times R _ 25 , and there is more stellar than gas mass at all radii. Surprisingly there is a constant ratio of stellar to gas mass beyond 30 kpc, where the disk is also warped. I propose the most likely formation mechanism for this outer disk is co-planar satellite accretion.

The Dragonfly Telephoto Array was used to observe a nearby spiral galaxy, NGC 2841. Comparisons of the stellar disk was made to the THINGS HI and GALEX UV disks of this galaxy. The underlying stellar disk in NGC 2841 discovered using Dragonfly is gigantic, reaching beyond the size of the most sensitive HI and UV disk observations to 70 kpc (∼5 R _ 25 ).

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC2841 as a case study for beam-smearing correction and Fourier analysis, finding a fast-rotating inclined disc with cross-shaped velocity-dispersion structure, strong k=2/k=4 perturbations, and inner lopsidedness.

quotes.quote: An example of BS modelling with the galaxy NGC2841 is shown in Fig. fig:n2841-ex . This fast-rotating galaxy (second panel) has an inclination of 74 . The fourth panel shows the corresponding _ bs , which, once subtracted to the observed dispersion (middle panel), yields the corrected velocity dispersion map (right panel) from which asymmetries are measured. The general behaviour of the smearing effect is thus that _ bs is larger at low radius and exhibits a typical X-shape pattern.

In NGC2841, ^2_ corr is high along a cross shaped pattern, with strong k=2 and k=4 perturbations and the k=2 perturbation grows stronger with radius. The residual ^2_ res is weak, showing that the first 4 orders reproduce the structure in ^2_ corr . The galaxy is lopsided at small radii e.g. 1980baldwin and this is detected by the k=1 mode of the FFT. The phase angle of the bisymmetry does not vary much beyond R 170 , at a value of 0.7 times the period of the k=2 asymmetry.

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 2841 as a high-surface-brightness MOND rotation-curve puzzle, finding that alternative interpolating functions fit its Cepheid distance well while independent evidence for a larger distance keeps the issue open.

quotes.quote: This raises the possibility that with the new forms of studied here we may solve a long standing problem concerning NGC 2841. This is an HSB galaxy that has posed a puzzle in the context of MOND.

We now find that with some of our new interpolating functions, which are also expedient for ring formation, the MOND fit is very good for the Cepheid distance of NGC 2841. We show in Fig. fig9 the fit with _2 , reproducing the old conundrum, and we see that using _1 alleviates the discrepancy somewhat but not completely. The forms _2 and _ 1.5 do a much better job. This may then be the correct solution to the puzzle: not to put NGC 2841 at a larger distance but to use a more appropriate form of (a combination of the two is also possible, of course). It should be noted, however, that the larger distance of 23 is indicated both by the Tully-Fisher (TF) relation and by a type Ia supernova in NGC 2841; so the issue remains moot.

sources.summary: The paper adopts Cepheid-based distance and Kent photometry for NGC 2841, notes a warped but symmetric gas distribution, and uses extended rotation curves that remain flat inward and agree with THINGS.

quotes.quote: NGC 2841 A distance of 14.1 1.5 Mpc has been determined by HST measurements of Cepheids (Macri et al. 2001). Photometry is available by K87 in the Thuan Gunn (1976) -band giving both, the photometric profile and total magnitude. Because there is a substantial difference in observed ellipticity between the bulge and disc a decomposition of the light of these components according to Kent's procedure is well determined. The rotation curve has initially been measured by Bosma (1981) and is later refined by Begeman (1987).

The gas distribution is symmetric, but the kinematics displays a sizable amount of warping, making the determined rotation in the outer regions slightly less certain. The rotation curve remains more or less at a constant level all the way inwards to the centre. The THINGS rotation curve reaches out to 36 kpc and displays a sizable uncertainty between 25 and 36 kpc, while the present one goes out to 64 kpc, both are fully consistent.

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC 2841 to model molecular disc scale heights, noting missing central CO data and sensitivity to gas velocity dispersion and halo profile.

quotes.quote: For NGC 2841, CO was not detected within a central region of radius 2.6 kpc and hence, for this galaxy, we only solve Eq. eq_hydro outside this region.

To investigate this in more details, we solve Eq. eq_hydro for a representative galaxy, NGC 2841 assuming different atomic and molecular velocity dispersions and estimate the scale heights. In Fig. sclh_depend we show the dependence of the gas scale heights on the assumed velocity dispersions. The left panel depicts the variation of the atomic and the molecular scale heights for a set of assumed _ CO .

To do that, we solve Eq. eq_hydro for NGC 2841 considering both the NFW and the ISO dark matter halo profiles (from Table. 5 of deblok08 ) and estimate the scale heights. In the right panel of Fig. sclh_depend we plot the resulting atomic and molecular scale heights. As it can be seen from the figure, a different dark matter halo profile (ISO) for NGC 2841 produces a maximum difference of 15-20 in the molecular scale height at inner radii.

sources.summary: The paper models NGC 2841 as a barless spiral with a gently falling rotation curve, finding physical halo parameters and baryonic matter nearly matching dark matter within four disc scales.

quotes.quote: NGC 2841 --- a spiral galaxy without a bar in the constellation Ursa Major. The rotation curve of this galaxy is traced up to 50 kpc and shows a gentle but clear fall by V=40  km/s. If the value of (M/L)_ d is fixed at 0.48, which corresponds to the Kroupa IMF, then all the obtained solutions will give high values of . The best solution with a minimal value of can be reached if (M/L)_ d and (M/L)_ b are free parameters.

The resulting mass of the disc approximately equal to the mass of the dark halo within four exponential scales of the disc (for all types of halo). This is seen in Fig.  fig:N2841_ISO - fig:N2841_NFW ( right plots) and in Fig.  fig:N2841_Burk : at R=4h , the circular speed associated with the disc alone is greater than the circular speed associated with the halo alone. The halo-to-baryonic mass ratio within four exponential scales of the disc varies from 0.8 to 1.2 for the free parameter (M/L)_ d (see Tables  tab:ISO_RCdec tab:Burkert_RCdec ).

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC 2841 as a THINGS spiral test case and finds the modified potential fails to reproduce its declining rotation curve.

quotes.quote: NGC 2841. This is an early type (Sb) spiral with absolute B mag M_B = -21.21 and distance D = 14.1 \ Mpc . Its rotation curve was first measured by and shows a good overall agreement with the THINGS data we use here. Similarly, the position angle and the inclination differ by only few degrees from P.A. = 153^ o and i = 74^o recommended by .

NGC 2841. This is the worst fitted galaxy in the sample with only the very inner regions well fitted by the data. While the data shows a decreasing trend, the best fit circular velocity profile starts diverging after an initial decrease. Notwithstanding the relatively small ^2 value, we can nevertheless safely argue that the modified potential is not able to fit the NGC 2841 rotation curve.

sources.summary: The paper models NGC 2841 with THINGS HI data and two stellar components, finding molecular gas rotation curves nearly identical for both conversion factors and only small inner total-curve differences.

quotes.quote: NGC 2841 In Figure fig:N2841-MMs we plot the results of the mass modelling for NGC 2841. ISO and NFW halo models were fitted using the THINGS H i observed rotation curve. We fit models to the THINGS rotation curve, which starts at 4 and extends to 50 .

The stellar mass distribution is decomposed into 2-components, which we use to fit the observed rotation curve. The value 2g=7.1 is very close the Milky Way value. The predicted rotation curves for both 2g and are almost identical, as are the resultant best fitting halo model rotation curves for both the NFW and ISO cases.

sources.summary: The paper presents Dragonfly imaging of NGC 2841, discovering a giant low-surface-brightness stellar disk that traces HI and UV emission, warps beyond 30 kpc, and remains more massive than the gas disk.

quotes.quote: This paper presents low surface brightness optimized visible wavelength observations of the extreme outskirts of the nearby spiral galaxy NGC 2841. We report the discovery of an enormous low-surface brightness stellar disk in this object. When azimuthally averaged, the stellar disk can be traced out to a radius of 70 kpc (5 R_ 25 or 23 inner disk scale lengths). The structure in the stellar disk traces the morphology of HI emission and extended UV emission. Contrary to expectations, the stellar mass surface density does not fall below that of the gas mass surface density at any radius.

Beyond 30 kpc, the low surface brightness stellar disk begins to warp, which may be an indication of a physical connection between the outskirts of the galaxy and infall from the circumgalactic medium.

sources.summary: The paper studies CO(2-1) gas conditions and estimates magnetic-field strengths for NGC 2841, reporting outskirts temperatures, columns, H2 masses, and 6-31 G field strengths for the spiral-galaxy sample.

quotes.quote: Based on CO(2-1) public data, we study the monoxide oxygen gas excitation conditions and the magnetic field strength of NGC 2841, NGC 3077, NGC 3184, NGC 3351 spiral galaxies. For their galaxy outskirts, we found kinetic temperatures in the range of 35 - 38 K , CO column densities 10^ 15 10^ 16 cm^ 2 and H2 masses of 4 10^6 - 6 10^8 M_ .

In this work, we study the magnetic field strength in the outskirts of four spiral galaxies: NGC 2841, NGC 3077, NGC 3184, NGC 3351, following a different approach to those commonly based on Faraday rotation, dust polarization, synchrotron emission, and so on.

In the present contribution, we have estimated the magnetic field strength in the galaxy nuclei and in the outskirts of NGC 2841, NGC 3077, NGC 3184, NGC 3351 spiral galaxies. For that, we have used an approximated expression of the magneto-hydrodynamics to find an upper limit for the magnetic field magnitudes. The magnetic field strength lies within the range of 6-31 G, which are in good agreement with the values provided by other author for spiral galaxies.

sources.summary: The paper derives center estimates and harmonic-decomposition results for NGC 2841, describing its disk morphology, non-circular motions, and whether the inferred potential is consistent with being round.

quotes.quote: NGC 2841 NGC 2841 (Fig. fig:ngc-2841 ) a) Center estimates [ 1.5ex ] NGC 2841 is an early-type spiral galaxy. Its \ distribution has a central hole and the IRAC 3.6 m image shows a distinctive bulge and a flocculent spiral structure. The radio continuum map of NGC 2841 shows a strong nuclear source coinciding within 0 2 with the position of the central source in the 3.6 m IRAC image. We used the position of the radio continuum source ( = 6 3, =0 5) as input for \ and made a fit with all parameters left free.

sources.summary: The paper revisits NGC 2841 as a known MOND problem, arguing the distance discrepancy is resolved with the simple interpolation function and acceptable stellar mass-to-light ratio.

quotes.quote: NGC 2841 is, e.g., the most well-known and most persistently problematic galaxy for MOND. Begeman et al. (1991) pointed out that a good MOND fit could only be obtained if the galaxy was a factor 2 further away than the Hubble distance of 9.5 Mpc. This large discrepancy was alleviated somewhat when HST Cepheids measurements suggested a distance of 14.1 Mpc (Macri et al. 2001), but the discrepancy remained. However, these fits were performed with the standard function (Eq. 3) and not the simple one (Eq. 4). Our fits here show that the problem of the distance is solved when using the simple function (see the reduced ^2 in Table 2), and that the stellar mass-to-light ratio is also in accordance with population synthesis models.

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC 2841 as a star-dominated spiral RAR example, finding a good fit within distance and inclination uncertainties despite its history as a MOND problem case.

quotes.quote: Figure N2841 shows an example of an MCMC fit for a star-dominated spiral galaxy (NGC 2841). This object has historically been regarded as a problematic case for MOND Begeman1991, Gentile2011 , but a good fit is obtained allowing for uncertainties in distance and inclination within 1 . The values of _ disk and _ bulge are relatively high but not unreasonable for such a massive, metal-rich galaxy.

The Navarro-Frenk-White (NFW) halo fit to NGC 2841 Katz2017 looks indistinguishable from that in Fig. \ N2841 : the two extra fit parameters available with a dark matter fit do not alter the shape of the continuous line that best approximates the data. We therefore consider fits of this type to be good even if ^2 is larger than unity.

sources.summary: NGC 2841 is treated as a critical MOND case whose preferred distance remains significantly larger than the Cepheid value, despite possible distance-scale doubts.

quotes.quote: For the large Sc spiral, NGC 2841, MOND requires a distance which is at least 20 larger than the Cepheid-based distance. However, the discrepancy of the Tully-Fisher and SNIa distances with the Cepheid determination casts some doubt upon the Cepheid method in this case.

NGC 2841, however, remains a difficult case for MOND. The minimum distance which is consistent with MOND is about 17 Mpc whereas the Cepheid-based distance is 14.1 1.5 Mpc. This discrepancy would be even larger if the value of a_o derived from the Ursa Major galaxies is used.

sources.summary: The paper models NGC 2841 with two central components, infers a dense central concentration, and finds the two-regime theory works for this galaxy type.

quotes.quote: Figs fig:fig6 and fig:fig7 , respectively, show NGC 2841 and IC 0342, which have been modeled with two central components with different scale radii. Both seem to have a dense mass concentration toward their centers. The scale radii for these inner most concentrations are a = 0.09 kpc and a = 0.05 kpc for NGC 2841 and IC 0342 respectively. The combined rotation curve for IC 0342 is a much better fit to the data using the two-acceleration-regime model than that of the former, NGC 2841. Nevertheless the theory works well in these type of galaxies also.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 2841 as having an hourglass continuum structure, polarized brightness along the minor axis, a minimum near the receding major axis, and aligned magnetic vectors.

quotes.quote: NGC 2841 In Stokes I , braun_etal_2007 note a diffuse ``hourglass'' structure, with the long axis of the hourglass oriented perpendicular to the disk major axis. In the polarized emission, the highest brightnesses are seen along the minor axis trailing away slowly to the northwest and more rapidly to the southeast. The lowest brightness of polarized emission occurs near the receding major axis (PA = 153 ^ , as tabulated in Table table:summary ). The directions of the magnetic field vectors are primarily aligned along the polarized arcs.

sources.summary: The paper places this THINGS low-surface-brightness galaxy in Group A, finding fitted BDM cores with r_s exceeding r_c, typical transition energy near 0.11 eV, and BDM fits preferred over other cored profiles.

quotes.quote: We obtain seven galaxies (DDO 154 (A1), NGC 2841 (A2), NGC 3031 (A3), NGC 3621 (A4), NGC 4736 (A5), NGC 6946 (A6), and NGC 7793 (A7)) (we call Group A) with fitted values r_s > r_c 0 that are shown in Table tab:onlydm_bdm . For these galaxies we obtain average values for: the core r_c 40 pc , r_s 5 kpc , and the energy E_c 0.11 \ eV for the min.disk analysis, that are typical values for a galaxy, except for DDO 154 which has a larger core, r_c = 1.35 kpc . We notice that for all these galaxies the ^2 in BDM is smaller than NFW's but the extra parameter ( r_c ) in BDM makes the reduced ^2_ red equivalent for both profiles. When comparing the BDM profile with the other two cored profiles, Burkert and ISO (Ref. ) we conclude that BDM is still better.

sources.summary: The paper finds NGC 2841 has high annular contamination between instruments, preventing long-term comparison, and Chandra imaging reveals multiple X-ray sources nearby.

quotes.quote: NGC 2841 : One observation per instrument is available. When they are compared, the emission from the annular region contributes with 60 in Chandra data. Thus, we do not perform a simultaneous fit and we can not use this object to discuss long term variations. In this case Chandra image reveals at least three X-ray sources within the annular region (see Appendix Ximages ).

sources.summary: The paper discusses NGC 2841 as a key early HI case with gas extending far beyond its optical image and a warp not attributed to tidal interaction.

quotes.quote: In the 1970s I found very extended HI disks in galaxies such as NGC 5055 and NGC 2841, out to 2 - 2.5 times the Holmberg radius. In my thesis work ( ), I found several galaxies for which the HI extends much farther out than the optical image, the best case being the galaxy NGC 2841, cf. Figure 1. The warp was modelled with tilted rings, now using the circular velocity in each ring as a free parameter. Since NGC 2841 does not have an obvious companion, it was concluded that the warp was not due to tidal interaction, as for NGC 5907 and NGC 4565.

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 2841 as an isolated comparison spiral, estimating its dust mass within 6.5-9.1 x 10^7 solar masses and including it in the average dust-to-gas ratio.

quotes.quote: For comparison purposes, we include in our sample the spiral galaxies NGC 2841, NGC 3184 and NGC 3351, which we have considered as isolated galaxies. The positions, morphology and distances of the galaxies of our sample are given in Table tab1 .
For the isolated NGC 2841, NGC 3184 and NGC 3351 galaxies, we find dust masses in the range of 6.5--9.1 10 ^7 M _ .
NGC 2841, NGC 3184 and NGC 3351 show an average dust-to-gas ratio of 1.8 that is consistent with the average value of 1 found in a sample of nearby star-forming galaxies .

sources.summary: The paper finds NGC 2841's recovered H I velocity dispersion higher than earlier work, attributing this to its high inclination, coarse resolution, steep rotation curve, and possible smearing.

quotes.quote: However, for the galaxy NGC 2841, the iterative method recovers much higher _ HI values as compared to what is obtained by bacchini19a . The inclination of NGC 2841 is 74^o , and it was observed with a coarse spatial (760 pc) and spectral resolutions (5.2 ). Its rotation curve also shows a significant gradient (Fig. rotcur , second panel on the top) across all radius. It is possible that significant smearing is introduced due to these effects, and in these circumstances, the iterative method might not be fully effective in recovering the _ HI correctly.

sources.summary: The paper reports that NGC 2841 required a mild parameterized warp; its flat-disk fit was unsatisfactory and the warp model behaved reasonably.

quotes.quote: NGC 2841 As fits to the velocity map of this galaxy were unsatisfactory when \ assumed a flat disc, we allowed for a gradual mild warp. The parameterized warp fitted by \ allows for a change of both inclination and position angle with radius that varies in a quadratic fashion from zero change at the inner radius R_ warp to a maximum at the edge. Thus there are three additional global parameters to be fitted: the radius at which the warp starts, R_ warp , and the maximum changes in ellipticity w_ , max and PA w_ , max . The results are displayed in Figure  fig.N2841 .

sources.summary: The paper analyzes NGC 2841 as an early-type Sb spiral whose outer orientation changes may indicate either a mild warp or radial velocity field.

quotes.quote: NGC 2841 NGC 2841 is an early-type (Sb) spiral. The TRM shows that both the P.A. and inclination angle exhibit monotonic variations of about 15^ going from the inner to the outer regions of the galaxy, i.e. for R>R_ 25 300'' (see Fig. NGC2841 ). The variation of the two orientation angles from the inner to the outer disk can be interpreted as a mild warp Sellwood_etal_2021 but of course, in view of the degeneracy between geometrical deformations and radial flows, also as true radial velocity field if a flat disk is assumed.

sources.summary: The paper finds nuclear emission, a diffuse hourglass-like structure, possible bipolar outflow morphology, and an H i disk extending beyond the stellar disk with a warped position angle.

quotes.quote: A nuclear emission component is detected as well as a more diffuse hour-glass structure of 5 \ major-axis diameter. The two brighter regions mark the apparent waist of this diffuse structure (see also Fig. 1 in Hummel Bosma ). This feature is reminiscent of a large diameter bi-polar outflow along the minor axis, but may well have some other origin. The H i map exhibits low column densities within the stellar disk, and extends well beyond this, but with a different position angle, indicative of a warp, as has been previously documented (eg. Bosma ).

sources.summary: The paper classifies NGC 2841 as a classical-bulge galaxy and reports its central morphology, Sersic index, and mid-infrared star-formation state.

quotes.quote: NGC 2841 .SAR3*./Sb Classical Bulge. At the very center of the bulge there is a small nuclear spiral, however the vast majority of bulge light is dominated by smooth isophotes, that appears similar to an E-type galaxy. The decomposition yields n_b=3.6 , and the bulges is not active with M_ 3.6 M_ 8.0 =-0.8 mag.

sources.summary: The paper identifies NGC 2841 as the fastest-rotating and most deviant zero-point calibrator, but finds nothing unusual besides its extreme rotation rate.

quotes.quote: The most deviant point is the fastest rotator, NGC 2841, with a deviation of 2.7 with respect to the template dispersion. This galaxy was a 2.3 deviant in the I band calibration. There is nothing unusual about this galaxy other than its extreme rotation rate so we see no reason to disregard it as a calibrator.

sources.summary: NGC 2841 is highlighted as a high-rotation Sb galaxy with unusually low spiral amplitude and an uncertain small pitch angle because of its high inclination and complex structure.

quotes.quote: Figure 4 is particularly suggestive in that 11/13 of the galaxies appear to broadly follow a trend of increasing amplitude of spiral features with increasing rotation velocity; the low amplitude of spiral features for the two objects with the largest rotational velocities (the Sb galaxies NGC 4579 and NGC 2841) are notably discrepant.

There is one galaxy, NGC 2841 (pitch angle 7 ^o ), for which the pitch angle should be treated with an extra degree of caution, because as described in Paper I the high inclination and complex structure make the extraction of a single pitch angle problematical (see caption to Table 3).

The appearance of an anti-correlation is strongly driven by one galaxy (NGC 2841) which combines a small pitch angle with a large rotational velocity, though as noted above the pitch angle in this case is particularly uncertain due to its large inclination and complex structure (Paper I).

sources.summary: The paper reports a sharp NGC 2841 velocity-dispersion increase, treats it as the most distant galaxy in the sample, and groups it among the high-inclination systems.

quotes.quote: NGC 2841 velocity dispersion shows a peculiar sharp increase of 10 extending from 15 kpc to 30 kpc, whose origin we discuss in Appendix ap:ngc2841_xshape .
The most distant galaxy, NGC 2841, was observed at about 400 pc of spatial resolution, and consequently this is our upper limit for homogeneous resolution.
NGC 2841, NGC 3198, and NGC 7331 are the galaxies in the sample with i>70 .

sources.summary: The paper finds NGC 2841 among the galaxies with the lowest model values, with gas Toomre parameter above four throughout the disk and total Toomre parameter exceptional in the outer disk.

quotes.quote: The of NGC 2841, NGC 3198 and NGC 4736 are the lowest of all galaxies, with values below 30.
An exception is NGC 2841, where Q_ gas > 4 in the entire gas disk.
Q_ tot is close to unity in the outer disks, except for NGC 628 and NGC 2841.

sources.summary: NGC 2841 is presented as a flocculent, star-dominated spiral historically problematic for MOND; the paper compares MOND-RAR and RCFM fits to its rotation curve.

quotes.quote: NGC 2841 NGC 2841 is a star dominated, flocculent spiral galaxy, which historically has been regarded as a problematic case for MOND ( Gent ). MOND-RAR finds a good fit for this galaxy after adjusting the Cepheid distance of 14.1 Mpc, by 1 , to 15.5 Mpc. Cepheid variable stars are the most accurate distance indicators currently available. The RCFM fit is shown in Fig.  fig:CompareNGC2841 .

gmw_00000360.name: NGC 3184
gmw_00000360.overview: NGC 3184 is an isolated [arXiv:0908.0693, arXiv:1709.05280], nearly face-on grand-design spiral galaxy [arXiv:2001.00168, arXiv:1912.05352] that hosts a pseudobulge [arXiv:1004.5393]. Its disk exhibits active star formation [arXiv:1509.02356] with numerous star-forming regions [arXiv:1509.02356, arXiv:1907.02934] and faint polarized radio emission [arXiv:0905.3995].

sources.summary: The paper argues NGC 3184 likely hosts a low-luminosity AGN, citing its nuclear X-ray source and infrared line diagnostics despite an optically HII-like nucleus.

quotes.quote: As examples, we present here two late-type spiral galaxies, NGC 3184 and NGC 5457 (M101). Their nuclear optical spectra show no signs of AGNs . But using archival \ data and multi-wavelength data from the literature, we show that the sum total of evidence strongly suggests that the galaxies host AGNs.

This galaxy is of type Scd, classified as having an Hii nucleus by hfs97-3 , and is at a distance of 8.7 Mpc t88 . The argument for the presence of an AGN is based on the following: (a) The nucleus is an x-ray source . Observations with \ totaling 60 ks detect a point-like source with 36 counts and a more extended component with 117 counts (Fig. fig:n31n54 ).

This nucleus falls into the ``transition'' region between AGNs and Hii regions. This suggests that the emission has an AGN component that was diluted because of the large aperture used ( 20'' ) to extract the fluxes.

sources.summary: The paper presents new direct abundances for NGC 3184, including MODS/LBT spectra of H II regions, and compares its abundance gradients with the CHAOS spirals.

quotes.quote: So far, results for individual galaxies have been reported for NGC 628 (M74) in hereafter, B15 berg15 , NGC 5194 (M51a) in hereafter, C15 croxall15 , and NGC 5457 (M101) in hereafter, C16 croxall16 . Here we present new direct abundances for NGC 3184 and, combined with past results, present the first analyses of a sample of four CHAOS galaxies, totaling 190 H 2 regions with measured auroral line based temperatures.

The N/O abundances for the four CHAOS galaxies are presented in Figure fig8 . Galactocentric radii are normalized to the isophotal radius, R_ 25 , of each galaxy in the top panel and to the effective radius, R_e , in the bottom panel. Once again we analyze gradients of galaxies by comparing their individual Bayesian linear regression fits (solid lines).

Interestingly, when trends in N/O versus R_g/R_ 25 are considered as a single, linear relationship as was done with O/H in Section 4.2, all four galaxies appear to have similar gradients, only offset from one another. Additionally, as noted in previous CHAOS papers, the N/O relationships are more tightly ordered with radius than the O/H gradients, presented by smaller dispersions. On the other hand, when the N/O trends are normalized by their effective radius (bottom panel), three of the four galaxies (NGC 628, NGC 5457, and NGC 3184) shift to lie nearly on top of one another, while NGC 5194 emerges as an outlier once again.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 3184 as an Scd galaxy with soft nuclear X-ray emission, optical structure, likely heavy obscuration, and a possible low-luminosity AGN.

quotes.quote: NGC 3184 This is of type Scd, classified as having an H 2 nucleus by hfs97-3 , and is at a distance of 8.7 Mpc t88 . It was observed with \ twice, one month apart, for 40 ks and 25 ks. Both observations used the ACIS-S3 chip. The nuclear x-ray emission appears to consist of two components (Fig. fig:n3184i ), a fainter, point-like source in the north and a brighter, more extended source in the south.

The fact that we see x-ray emission in the soft band but almost none in the hard band argues against the emission being the continuum from the AGN, whether direct or scattered. The source is not seen in the UV image. Both of these points suggest the presence of heavy obscuration.

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC3184 as a barred, low-inclination spiral with HII-dominated nucleus, grand-design arms, and residual X-ray emission extending from the nucleus to 8 kpc.

quotes.quote: NGC3184 is an SAB(rs)cd barred spiral seen at low inclination ( i<24 ; Lyon-Meudon Extragalactic Database [ LEDA ] at http://leda.univ-lyon1.fr ) at a distance of 11.6 Mpc ( leonard02 ). The nuclear region of the galaxy is dominated by HII emission with no compelling evidence for an AGN ( doane04 ). Grand-design twin spiral arms are observed, although the contrast in brightness between ``arm'' and ``off-arm'' regions in the galaxy is low. In X-rays NGC3184 has been studied by with an emphasis on both the point source population ( colbert04 ) and the diffuse X-ray emitting gas ( doane04 ; tyler04 ). The diffuse X-ray emission shows a strong correlation with regions which are bright in ( tyler04 ).

In the present study we measure the aggregate luminosity of the 18 brightest point sources to be 6 10^ 39 , consistent with earlier estimates ( colbert04 ). The residual X-ray emission has an X-ray luminosity of 2.5 10^ 39 and extends from the nucleus out to a distance of 8 kpc ( 2.5 ).

sources.summary: The paper studies NGC 3184 as a non-interacting face-on spiral whose outer disk contains numerous cluster-like knots correlated with H I structure beyond the optical radius.

quotes.quote: We use deep ( 27.5 mag V band point-source limiting magnitude) V \ and U band LBT imaging to study the outer disk (beyond the optical radius R_ 25 ) of the non-interacting, face-on spiral galaxy NGC 3184 ( D = 11.1 Mpc; R_ 25 = 11.1 kpc) and find that this outer disk contains >1000 objects (or marginally-resolved `knots') resembling star clusters with masses 10^2 - 10^4 M_ and ages up to 1 Gyr. We find statistically significant numbers of these cluster-like knots extending to 1.4 R_ 25 , with the redder knots outnumbering bluer at the largest radii. We measure clustering among knots and find significant correlation to galactocentric radii of 1.5 R_ 25 for knot separations <1 kpc. The effective integrated surface brightness of this outer disk cluster population ranges from 30 - 32 mag arcsec ^ 2 in V .

We compare the H 1 extent to that of the correlated knots and find that the clusters extend at least to the damped Lyman- threshold of H 1 column density ( 2 20 2 ; 1.62 R_ 25 ). The blue knots are correlated with H 1 spiral structure to 1.5R_ 25 , while the red knots may be correlated with the outer fringes of the H 1 disk to 1.7R_ 25 . These results suggest that outer disks are well-populated, common, and long-lasting features of many nearby disk galaxies.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 3184 as a late-type galaxy with many star-forming regions, asymmetric outer spiral arms, active disk star formation, and a need for UV follow-up.

quotes.quote: NGC 3184. This is a late-type galaxy with numerous star-forming regions hod82 and a high integrated SFR you96,mar97 . The outer regions of the spiral arms have a curved, asymmetrical shape. The disk is located above and to the right of the NCS on the (B-V) (V-R) and (B-V) (V-I) diagrams. The photometric parameters of the NGC 3184 disk are characteristic of spiral arms.

Apparently, active star formation is occurring in the NGC 3184 disk, which can be described in a model with numerous brief ( 10-100 Myr) bursts of star formation. This is also indicated by the high, diffuse background in the H line in the disk between the spiral arms of the galaxy gus02b . Clarifying the star-formation history in the disk of NGC 3184 will require surface photometry in U and other UV bands.

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC 3184, a face-on spiral, to test how CLEAN depth, weighting, residual scaling, and blanking affect recovered H i velocity-dispersion profiles.

quotes.quote: We use the dirty image of NGC 3184, a face-on spiral galaxy, and clean it down to 3.5, 2.5 and 1.5 times the rms noise using Natural and Robust weightings. The 2.5 times rms cube is identical to the standard THINGS cube. We also create blanked non-residual scaled and (blanked) residual-scaled versions of these cubes. We divide the image of NGC 3184 into series of elliptical annuli with 0.1 _ 25 width we adopt _ 25 = 222 , walter08 , with identical orientation parameters (inclination, position angle, center position) as taken from walter08 .

We choose NGC 3184 because of its face-on orientation so that projection and beam smearing effects are minimal. For the Natural non-residual scaled cubes, there is a noticeable difference between the velocity dispersion derived from the (3.5, 2.5, 1.5) times rms cubes. This difference is smaller for the Robust non-residual scaled cubes.

sources.summary: The paper incorporates calibrated U and BVRI imaging of NGC 3184 and analyzes 70 selected star-cluster/H II region pairs, finding regular morphology but uncertain outer-range slopes.

quotes.quote: We did not observe NGC 3184 from our sample in U filter. Therefore we used an U image of NGC 3184 obtained by larsen1999b and available in the NED http://ned.ipac.caltech.edu/ database. The absolute calibration of the U image of the galaxy was carried out using parameters of descriptors from original FITS files (images) and their explanations in larsen1999b .
The final sample includes 503 SC-H ii R pairs in NGC 628, 70 pairs in NGC 3184, 254 pairs in NGC 3726, 40 pairs in NGC 5585, and 577 pairs in NGC 6946.

The graphs in Fig. figure:age_sep and the coefficients given in Table table:coeff show closely resembling slopes for cluster samples in three galaxies (NGC 628, NGC 3184, and NGC 6946) with a regular structure. The calculated slopes lie in the range 1.0-1.2 for separations from 40 to 200 pc. In the outer range of S (100-500 pc), the slopes 0.5 for pair samples in NGC 628 and NGC 6946. This slope turned out to be larger in NGC 3184 ( =0.91 ), however, it is calculated with poor accuracy due to insufficient statistics.

sources.summary: The paper analyzes NGC3184 as one of eight nearby face-on spirals, adopting DustPedia properties and finding its central bulge is less star-forming than its spiral arms.

quotes.quote: The final sample includes 8 objects: NGC0628, NGC3184, NGC3938, NGC4254, NGC4321, NGC4535, NGC5194, NGC5457. Distances, sizes, inclinations and morphologies are those adopted by the DustPedia collaboration, based on the HyperLEDA database The HyperLEDA database is available at http://leda.univ-lyon1.fr/ 2014A&A...570A..13M , and are available on their website (see Tab. tab:sample ). The sample stellar masses are in the range between 10^ 10 10^ 11 M_ , and star formation rates between 1 and 4 M_ yr ^ 1 These sources represent the evolved descendants of high-redshift normal star-forming galaxies, that are rotationally supported systems, at least by z=2 e.g. 2015ApJ...799..209W, 2009ApJ...706.1364F, 2018ApJS..238...21F . Moreover, their regular dynamical properties suggest that they have quiescently assembled their stellar mass through cosmic times.

The central bulge can be less (as in NGC0628, NGC3184, NGC3938, NGC5457) or more (as in NGC4254, NGC4321, NGC5194) starforming than the spiral arms. For those galaxies that have a 'red' bulge, a 'bending' of the MS relation is observed at the high _ end, as the cells corresponding to the bulge component are also the more massive ones. It is worth noting that for the galaxies in our sample few spaxels would be considered passive (1 dex below the MS). This is a consequence of the sample selection, made of grand-design spirals. Also, due to the sample selection, there are no morphological trends in the log _ log _ SFR plane, i.e. in scatter from the global (or individual) MS or in slope.

sources.summary: The paper studies CO(2-1) gas conditions and estimates magnetic-field strengths for NGC 3184, reporting outskirts temperatures, columns, H2 masses, and 6-31 G field strengths for the spiral-galaxy sample.

quotes.quote: Based on CO(2-1) public data, we study the monoxide oxygen gas excitation conditions and the magnetic field strength of NGC 2841, NGC 3077, NGC 3184, NGC 3351 spiral galaxies. For their galaxy outskirts, we found kinetic temperatures in the range of 35 - 38 K , CO column densities 10^ 15 10^ 16 cm^ 2 and H2 masses of 4 10^6 - 6 10^8 M_ .

In this work, we study the magnetic field strength in the outskirts of four spiral galaxies: NGC 2841, NGC 3077, NGC 3184, NGC 3351, following a different approach to those commonly based on Faraday rotation, dust polarization, synchrotron emission, and so on.

In the present contribution, we have estimated the magnetic field strength in the galaxy nuclei and in the outskirts of NGC 2841, NGC 3077, NGC 3184, NGC 3351 spiral galaxies. For that, we have used an approximated expression of the magneto-hydrodynamics to find an upper limit for the magnetic field magnitudes. The magnetic field strength lies within the range of 6-31 G, which are in good agreement with the values provided by other author for spiral galaxies.

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 3184 as a nearly face-on grand-design spiral, finding spatial gas-dust correlations in which molecular gas strongly drives the total gas-dust relation.

quotes.quote: NGC 3184 NGC 3184 has strong and definite spiral arms such that it is classified as a ``grand design spiral", as seen in Figure overlays_3184 . Located at a distance of 11.3 Mpc, it has a nearly face-on orientation (i=16 deg) which makes it an ideal candidate to study gas-dust interactions in the galaxy. Being a galaxy similar to the Milky Way, NGC 3184 has been used as a sample galaxy to determine the spatial correlation between diffuse hot gas and star-forming regions by .

The molecular gas in NGC 3184 shows a good positive correlation ( 0.7) with all IR bands and it also dominates the total gas (atomic+molecular) vs dust correlation which is tighter ( 0.8) than either individual case as seen in Figure 3plots_irHI8 . If we only look at the spiral arm locations in NGC 3184, we do not see any correlation due to the narrow range of IR emission values covered. We see the overall positive correlation only when we consider all locations.

sources.summary: NGC3184 is treated as a nearby face-on main-sequence spiral; its central region falls below the resolved sequence, while arm regions show elevated star formation and varied gas efficiency.

quotes.quote: This work is based on multiwavelength observations of five nearby face-on spiral galaxies: NGC0628, NGC3184, NGC5194, NGC5457, and NGC6946.

We highlight two properties of our sample: i) according to the integrated SFR and M _ values, the objects in our sample are MS galaxies, located within 0.2 dex from the relation obtained in Paper I, and ii) three out of five sources are classified as SAB spirals (NGC3184, NGC5457 and NGC6946), thus show evidence of a bar component.

NGC3184 also has a central region characterised by mostly negative _ MS and _ f_ gas,tot , while _ SFE,tot is, for the majority of cells, positive. In the spiral arms, we observe regions where the SFR is significantly higher than the MS value (B and C) that correspond to gas fractions above the average, and varying SFE. We identify three blobs (A, D E) with negative _ f_ gas,tot and _ MS varying slightly around the MS value (as in A E) or _ MS <0 (as in D).

sources.summary: The paper applies multiwavelength morphology parameters to NGC 3184, using it as the isolated, unperturbed comparison galaxy against interacting NGC 5194/M51 in UV through H I data.

quotes.quote: We applied these contours on images of two galaxies, NGC 3184 and NGC 5194, spanning UV, optical, near and far-infrared from GALEX, SDSS and Spitzer see also in preparation Holwerda10a .

In Figure f:morph we can also compare the isolated, unperturbed galaxy NGC 3184 to the interacting NGC 5194 (M51). In the morphological parameters we can see that Concentration increases in the optical and near-infrared from isolated to interacting. Asymmetry decreases a little from isolated to interaction in UV and 24 m emission (the star-formation tracers) but increases for \ 21 cm emission.

sources.summary: The paper uses resolved observations of NGC 3184 to test a local metallicity relation, deriving stellar and gas surface densities and finding the resolved data follow a tight stellar-fraction relation.

quotes.quote: In order to test this prediction, we have compiled resolved HI, H _2 , stellar mass, and metallicity observations for the galaxies NGC 628 and NGC 3184. We have estimated stellar surface densities from the surface brightness of the 3.6- m emission detected by the Spitzer Infrared Nearby Galaxies Survey SINGS http://irsa.ipac.caltech.edu/data/SPITZER/SINGS , Kennicutt+03 , using a conversion factor Leroy+08 _* pc^ 2 = 280 i I_ 3.6 MJy sr^ 1 where i denotes the inclination angle of each galaxy (7 and 16 degrees for NGC 628 and NGC 3184, respectively). For the neutral and molecular gas, we have used the moment-0 maps available from The HI Nearby Galaxy Survey THINGS http://www.mpia-hd.mpg.de/THINGS , Walter+08 and the HERA CO-Line Extragalactic Survey HERACLES http://www.mpia-hd.mpg.de/HERACLES , Leroy+09 , estimating the surface densities as _ HI pc^ 2 = 0.020 i I_ 21 K km s^ 1 for atomic hydrogen and _ H_2 pc^ 2 = 5.5 i I_ CO K km s^ 1 for the molecular component, where I_ 21 and I_ CO correspond to the surface brightness of the 21-cm line and the 2 1 transition of carbon monoxide, respectively.

SDSS and XMP galaxies) as well as resolved observations of the stellar and total hydrogen column densities in NGC 628 and NGC 3184 describe a fairly tight relation, well described by equation eq_Z_s .

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 3184 as an isolated comparison spiral, estimating its dust mass within 6.5-9.1 x 10^7 solar masses and including it in the average dust-to-gas ratio.

quotes.quote: For comparison purposes, we include in our sample the spiral galaxies NGC 2841, NGC 3184 and NGC 3351, which we have considered as isolated galaxies. The positions, morphology and distances of the galaxies of our sample are given in Table tab1 .
For the isolated NGC 2841, NGC 3184 and NGC 3351 galaxies, we find dust masses in the range of 6.5--9.1 10 ^7 M _ .
NGC 2841, NGC 3184 and NGC 3351 show an average dust-to-gas ratio of 1.8 that is consistent with the average value of 1 found in a sample of nearby star-forming galaxies .

sources.summary: The paper analyzes NGC 3184 as a low-inclination spiral where the disk is assumed coplanar and small inner radial velocities give way to outer perturbations.

quotes.quote: NGC 3184 NGC 3184 is a spiral galaxy with a global inclination angle of i=29^ deBlok_etal_2008 : this is the value measured by the TRM at large enough radii, whereas in the very inner disk the inclination angle results to be highly fluctuating (see Fig. NGC3184 ). The P.A. determined by the TRM is constant for R>150'' , while it shows some large fluctuations at smaller radii. Both of these fluctuating behaviors are due to the TRM's inability to treat galaxies with small inclination angles as they occur inside the optical radius R_ 25 220'' .

sources.summary: The paper reports faint polarized emission over the northern half of NGC 3184's disk, a minimum near the receding major axis, and possible interarm enhancement.

quotes.quote: NGC 3184 In this galaxy, faint polarized emission is apparent over much of the northern half of the disk. The lowest brightness of polarized emission occurs near the receding major axis (PA = 179 ^ , as tabulated in Table table:summary ). Interarm regions may be enhanced relative to the spiral arms, but deeper observations would be required for a definitive analysis.

sources.summary: The paper classifies NGC 3184 as a pseudobulge galaxy, citing The bulge has a prominent spiral that extends to the center; n_b=1.7 , and the bulge is actively forming stars with M_ 3.6 M_ 8.0 =1.4 mag.

quotes.quote: NGC 3184 .SXT6../Sc() Pseudobulge. The bulge has a prominent spiral that extends to the center. The decomposition yields n_b=1.7 , and the bulge is actively forming stars with M_ 3.6 M_ 8.0 =1.4 mag.

sources.summary: NGC3184 is included as a large late-type grand-design spiral with moderate star formation whose outer disk shows dynamical surface density above the H I surface density.

quotes.quote: The first four galaxies NGC628, NGC6946, NGC3184 and NGC4214 are large, late type spirals that have H i to stellar disk mass ratios M(HI)/M(star) < 1 (Table 1) and host star formation in their inner regions.
The galaxies NGC628, NGC6946 and NGC3184 are grand design spirals with moderate star formation rates (SFRs) of values 1 to 5, but NGC4214 has a much lower SFR of 0.05 walter.etal.2008 .
From Figure 3 it is clear that _ dyn as traced by the H i velocity dispersion is at least 1.5 to 2 times the value of _ H i in the region R>R_ 25 for the galaxies NGC628, NGC3184 and NGC4214.

sources.summary: For NGC 3184, the model mass accretion rate rises inside 2 kpc and then flattens; velocity dispersion is about 10 km/s and the CO conversion factor is about twice Galactic.

quotes.quote: In NGC 628 and NGC 3184 the mass accretion rate increases in the inner 2 kpc and stays approximately constant at larger radii.

In NGC 5194 the mass accretion rate is about =10^ 2 M _ yr ^ 1 in the inner disk and increases to almost 10^ 1 M _ yr ^ 1 at R=5.5 kpc The resulting velocity dispersions are about 10 km s ^ 1 in NGC 628, NGC 3184, NGC 3627, and NGC 5055.
Whereas NGC 628 and NGC 3184 show a CO conversion factor about twice as high as the Galactic value, those of NGC 3627 and NGC 5055 are about Galactic.

sources.summary: The paper lists NGC 3184 among the large spirals and reports that its outer-disk q profile spikes at large radius, probably because of H I measurement errors.

quotes.quote: We also included two galaxies NGC 4190 and UGC 8833 from the VLA ACS Nearby Galaxy Survey Treasury (VLA ANGST) survey ott.etal.2012 . Table 1 shows the sample of 20 galaxies, of which first 8 are relatively large, massive spirals (NGC 0628, NGC 3184, NGC 4736, NGC 5194, NGC 5236 and NGC 6946), and the remaining 12 are smaller spiral, dwarf or irregular galaxies. The procedure for determining the velocity dispersion across the galaxy disks as a function of radius is described in ianjamasimanana12 and ianjamasimanana.etal.2017 as well as in das.etal.2020 . We basically co-added the individual velocity profiles over radial bins to obtain azimuthally averaged, high signal-to-noise ratio (S/N) velocity dispersion values for each radial bin. The stacked profiles were fitted with single Gaussian functions, where the half width of the fitted Gaussian represents the velocity dispersion of the gas. This method gives a better estimate of the velocity dispersion compared to the moment2 maps. Figure 4 in the appendix shows the radial variation of the velocity dispersion across the galaxy disks. Its value is less than 10 kms ^ 1 at the R _ 25 radius, except for a few cases (NGC 5194, NGC 4190, DDO 187 and IC 10), where _ H i lies between 11 and 14 kms ^ 1 .

A clear trend is that q does not vary significantly beyond the R_ 25 radius (Figure 1). For the 6 large galaxies that have M(*)>10^ 9 M_ , q is close to constant in the outer disk region, except for NGC 3184 which shows a spike in q at large radii. However, this spike maybe due to errors in _ H i measurement as the column density decreases at the disk edge. A similar trend is present for the less massive galaxies ( M(*)<10^ 9 M_ ), where M81dfwA shows a similar spike in q at the disk edge. The dwarf galaxy IC1613 definitely has q 1 around R_ 25 and 0.5 < q < 1.0 beyond that. This is an exceptional case for the dwarfs. The gas rich dwarf DDO46 is the only galaxy that shows a significant decrease in q from q=0.5 at R_ 25 to 0.3 at 2R_ 25 , which indicates a fairly rapid change in halo shape with increasing radius.

gmw_00000361.name: NGC 3351
gmw_00000361.overview: NGC 3351 is an early-type barred spiral galaxy located in the Leo I group [arXiv:1004.4493, arXiv:2012.00757]. The galaxy features a stellar bar [arXiv:1812.04178] and a circumnuclear star-forming ring [arXiv:1004.4493, arXiv:2305.05650], while its bar region is depleted of neutral and molecular gas [arXiv:1812.04178].

sources.summary: The paper studies MERLIN radio observations of NGC 3351, describing its barred spiral structure, circumnuclear star-forming ring, molecular gas, and compact radio sources in the field.

quotes.quote: NGC 3351, also known as M95 and UGC5850, is a nearby 10.05 Mpc, 49 pc/arcsec, Graham+07 SBb()II early-type barred spiral galaxy Sandage+87 in the Leo group. Its coordinates are _ 2000 =10^ h 43^ m 57 7 , _ 2000 =+11^ 42 12 7 , and its major- and minor-axis diameters at the 25 magnitude isophote (D _ 25 ) are 7.4 5.0 arcmin, with a position angle (PA) of 13 ^ RC3 catalogue, deVaucouleurs+91 . NGC 3351 has a star-forming circumnuclear ring, and has been classified as a ``hot-spot'' galaxy by .

We report sub-arcsecond scale radio continuum observations of a field of 35 by 22 arcmin centred in NGC 3351 obtained with the Multi-Element Radio Linked Interferometer Network (MERLIN). We found 23 radio sources in this field, 6 of which are projected within the D _ 25 isophote of the galaxy, and 3 are located inside the central 100 arcsec in radius. Two of these three are significantly extended, while the third one is relatively compact. This one is the only source with a previously detected counterpart at other wavelengths and could constitute the radio counterpart of a young supernova remnant. The other two are probably related to jets from a background AGN.

sources.summary: The paper portrays NGC 3351 as a barred spiral whose inner pseudoring appears to be a separate spiral mode from its superfast bar.

quotes.quote: The appearance of NGC 3351 can garner much preconception. The bar is surrounded by a bright inner spiral pseudoring that could easily be interpreted in terms of the inner 4:1 resonance of the bar, as suggested by Schwarz (1984a,b). This would imply that the bar CR is outside the inner pseudoring.

The phase shift distributions, however, suggest a very different interpretation. Figure ps3351mass shows the phase shift distributions for NGC 3351 based on total mass maps that include both H i and H _2 gas. The figure shows that the 3.6 m and SDSS i band maps give very similar results: the phase shift is negative throughout the main part of the bar. This means that of the two CR radii indicated, CR _1 is the bar CR, and thus NGC 3351 is another example of a `superfast' bar. In this circumstance, the spiral pseudoring around the bar is a separate mode , not a bar-driven spiral.

sources.summary: The paper presents Messier 95 as a nearby early-type barred spiral whose stellar bar suppresses star formation by redistributing gas into the nucleus and depleting the bar region.
sources.paper_coordinate_text: M95 (J2000) = 10:43:57.7 and (J2000) = + 11:42:14 according to Nasa/IPAC Extragalactic Database (NED).

quotes.quote: In this context here we present a multi-wavelength study based on the archival data of a barred spiral galaxy, Messier 95 (M95). M95 (J2000) = 10:43:57.7 and (J2000) = + 11:42:14 according to Nasa/IPAC Extragalactic Database (NED). (also known as NGC 3351) is a nearby (10 0.4 Mpc, Freedman_2001 ) early-type barred spiral galaxy (Morphology; SBb). The angular scale of 1" corresponds to 48 pc at the distance of the galaxy.

We present here observational evidence for star formation quenching due to the presence of a stellar bar and the mechanism responsible for quenching in galaxy Messier 95 based on a multi-wavelength analysis using the archival data. Based on the FUV--NUV pixel colour map we demonstrate that the central 4.2 kpc diameter region along the stellar bar of galaxy is composed of stellar population with equivalent ages 350 Myr. This imply that currently there is no ongoing star formation along the region covered by the bar. The central sub-kpc region of the galaxy is hosting abundant supply of molecular hydrogen with the region along the bar devoid of neutral and molecular hydrogen, but is present outside the stellar bar-region.

sources.summary: NGC 3351 is a barred nuclear-ring galaxy with Milky-Way-like stellar mass, metallicity, and ring star formation; the paper finds a radial ionized outflow and transverse molecular/dust shell likely driven by stellar feedback.

quotes.quote: We present kinematic observations of the multi-phase ISM in the nuclear ring galaxy NGC 3351, as part of our VLT/MUSE TIMER survey (PI: Gadotti) https://www.muse-timer.org/ , with the goal of understanding the survival of cold gas in outflows and providing quantitative tests for sub-grid stellar feedback prescriptions. NGC 3351 is comparable to our own Galaxy in total stellar mass, metallicity ( M_ * = 3.1 10^ 10 M _ , Z/Z_ = 0.4 ) and star formation (SFR = 0.6 M _ yr ^ 1 in the nuclear ring alone; Gadotti19 ). As with all of the galaxies in the TIMER survey, it was selected because of its strongly barred morphology and presence of a nuclear ring - with its classification listed as (R^ ' )SB(,bl,nr)a in Buta15 . NGC 3351 exhibits dust lanes throughout the spiral arms, along the bar and nuclear ring - along with a transverse dust lane offset from the nuclear ring and orthogonal to the bar lanes. This feature will be discussed throughout this work and is indicated by the arrow in Figure fig:hst3col .

The MUSE observations presented here show a serendipitous and visually clear signature of a planar/radial outflow from the nuclear ring of NGC 3351 in the ionised gas flux and kinematic maps.
