However, this source shows broad and variable nuclear emission lines in optical and UV bands ulrich81, scarpa95 with equivalent width (EW) much larger than 5 (rest-frame), the threshold historically proposed to distinguish between BL Lac objects and FSRQ e.g., stickel91,stocke91 . This suggests that PKS 0521 36 is a misclassified AGN.

For this source, a large-scale optical jet well aligned with the kiloparsec radio jet was observed, with a clear correspondence between the radio and optical structures and references therein scarpa99 . In radio and optical bands the jet resembles that of the nearby radio galaxy M 87 e.g., sparks94 . Chandra detected the X-ray counterpart of the innermost 2-arcsec jet birkinshaw02 .

sources.summary: The paper describes PKS0521-365 as a nearby radio-loud BL Lac object resolved by ALMA, with a one-sided jet, knots, hotspot, lobe, and millimeter-band extended structures.

quotes.quote: PKS0521-365 . This nearby ( z=0.0554 ) radio-loud object is a bright ray source and exhibits a variety of nuclear and extranuclear phenomena Falomo2009 . It is one of the most remarkable objects in the southern sky: it is one of the three known BL Lac objects showing a kiloparsec-scale jet well resolved at all bands Liuzzo2011 . The ALMA image (Fig. fig:PKS0521-365 ) shows a one-sided radio jet extending in the N-W direction up to 7 arcsec from the nucleus.

The jet exhibits many knots, also detected from the optical to X-rays Falomo2009 . A hotspot located at 8 arcsec from the nucleus in the south-east direction is also detected in all bands. At low frequency, the arcsecond-scale radio structure is dominated by an extended lobe.

sources.summary: The paper describes PKS 0521 36 as a FSRQ with an optical jet, central SMBH, weak beaming, constrained jet orientation, and a difficult Seyfert-like/BL Lac state.

quotes.quote: PKS 0521 36 PKS 0521 36 is a FSRQ object with an optical jet located at z=0.055 1985AJ.....90.2207K . The central SMBH has a mass of 3.3 10^8 M_ 2005ApJ...631..762W . 1996ApJ...459..169P , and more recently 2002AJ....124..652T , mention the absence of superluminal motions in its jet, contrary to the case of 3C 273, implying that the beaming effect is much less important and thus strengthening their result on the viewing angle. Indeed the only constraint on the jet orientation comes from 1996ApJ...459..169P who deduce 30 6 from SSC models. We should also note that PKS 0521 36 seems to oscillate between a Seyfert-like and a BL Lac state e.g. 1981A&A...103L...1U , making this source difficult to interpret within a pure non-thermal scenario, especially since we are confronted with non-simultaneous data.

sources.summary: The paper keeps PKS 0521-365 as the EGRET counterpart despite source-confusion concerns and reports its first XMM-Newton analysis, noting prior radio/optical/X-ray jet evidence.

quotes.quote: The blazar PKS 0521-365 has been associated with the EGRET source in the Second Catalog (Thompson et al. 1995), but a stronger detection in the Cycle 4 placed this blazar outside the 99.99 probability contours. Sowards-Emmerd et al. (2004) suggested that the counterpart of 3EG J 0530-3626 source could be another radio source (PMN J 0529-3555 ) of unknown nature. This can be another case of a possible double source not resolved by EGRET (like, e.g. 3EG J 0222+4253 ), an interesting target worth observing with GLAST . In the present work, we keep as valid the association with PKS 0521-365 . The XMM-Newton observation is analyzed here for the first time.

sources.summary: The paper studies PKS 0521--365 as a nearby BL Lac with nuclear and extranuclear phenomena, broad emission lines, and a prominent radio, optical, and X-ray jet.

quotes.quote: The radio source PKS 0521--365 is a well-studied nearby object . It is one of the most remarkable extragalactic objects of the southern sky, because it exhibits a variety of nuclear and extra-nuclear phenomena. First classified as an N galaxy, and then as a BL Lac object, it shows the strong narrow and broad emission lines typical of type 1 active nuclei both in optical and UV Ulrich81,Danziger83,Scarpa95 . Interestingly, the source has a prominent radio, optical, and X-ray jet Danziger79,Keel86,falomo94,Macchetto91,Scarpa99,Birkinshaw02 , which resembles that of the nearby radio galaxy M87 .

sources.summary: The thesis detects two VLBI components in PKS 0521-365’s southeast hot spot, fits a power law through radio and Chandra data, and compares its structure to Pictor A.

quotes.quote: PKS 0521-365 Wide-field VLBI observations of PKS 0521-365 ( z=0.05534 ), obtained with the LBA, reveal two components associated with the south-east hot spot of the galaxy at 1.6 GHz and 2.3 GHz. A simple power-law, with spectral index =-1.06 , provides a good fit between our VLBI data and Chandra X-ray data. The size of the individual components and the overall extent of the hot spot is similar to that observed in Pictor A using similar methods.. The similarities suggest that the same emission mechanisms may be at work in the two galaxies, however, further multi-wavelength observations at optical and infrared wavelengths will be required to verify this.

sources.summary: The paper reports extended S-shaped emission-line regions along the optical jet of PKS 0521 365, interprets their velocities as helicoidal motion, and estimates ionized outflowing gas.

quotes.quote: We report on the discovery of extended emission line regions exhibiting an S-shaped morphology along the optical jet of the radio-loud AGN PKS 0521 365 ( z=0.055 ), by using long-slit spectroscopic observations obtained with FORS2 on VLT. The velocity pattern derived from the [ O II ] 3727 , H \ 4861 \ and [ O III ] 4959,5007 emission lines is well-fitted by a sinusoidal function of the form: v()= ^ 1/2 sin( ^ 1/2 + ) , suggesting helicoidal motions along the jet up to distances of 20 kpc. We estimate a lower limit for the mass of the outflowing ionized gas along the jet of 10 ^4 M_ . Helical magnetic fields and jet precession have been proposed to explain helicoidal paths along the jet at pc scales; nevertheless, it is not clear yet whether these hypotheses may hold at kpc scales.

sources.summary: The review describes PKS 0521--365 as a nearby BL Lac with an HST-studied optical jet superposed on a massive host galaxy and knot structure similar across wavebands.

quotes.quote: A detailed study of the optical jet based on HST images was completed for PKS 0521--365, PKS 2201+04 and 3C 371 scarpa99 . In Figure fig:imajet0521 we show an example of optical jet of the BLL PKS 0521--365 (z = 0.0554) superposed onto the prominent massive host galaxy. Detailed analysis of the jet structure at radio, near-infrared and optical frequencies reveals a number of knots (see Fig. fig:jet0521 ) that exhibit remarkable similarity at all frequencies falomo2009 .

sources.summary: The paper describes PKS 0521 365 as an FSRQ whose XMM-Newton spectrum is fit by a power law, with free NH improving the model modestly.

quotes.quote: PKS 0521 365: The X-ray spectrum of this blazar is studied by Donato et al. (2005) and found to be well characterized by simple power law with 1.7. Foschini et al. (2006) also fit the spectrum with broken power law and found break energy at 1.5 keV. In our studies, X-ray spectrum analyzed for one pointing of XMM Newton is well fitted by the power law when we fixed N_ H . This spectrum shows significant improvement in the model when we kept N_ H as free parameter. N_ H is found to be 3.8 which is very close to the Galactic absorption column density [ 10^ 20 cm ^ 2 ] from Dickey Lockman (1990).

sources.summary: The paper identifies PKS 0521-36 as a strong UHECR-source candidate outside vV12 and says it should contribute significantly to modeled UHECR flux and dipole anisotropy.

quotes.quote: We particularly note that 6 objects out of this list of 16 --- Pic A, 3C 111, Cen B, PKS 0521-36, PKS 1610-60 and PKS 1814-63 --- are not contained in vV12. This figure shows that besides the ``usual suspects'' Cen A (310,+19) , Vir A (284,-74) , For A (240,-57) and Cyg A (76,+6) , for the case of canonical boosting also Per A (151,-13) , 3C 111 (162,-9) , PKS 0521-36 (241,-33) and PKS 1814-63 (331,-21) are expected to contribute to the UHECR flux on a similar level.

We see that for > 8 EeV the direction of its total dipole is in excellent agreement with Auger results, mostly caused by the strong contributions of 3C 111, Per A and PKS 0521-36, and still in reasonable agreement for > 32 EeV.

gmw_00000371.name: ID 4590
gmw_00000371.overview: ID 4590 is a compact, gravitationally lensed dwarf galaxy at a redshift of approximately 8.5 [arXiv:2208.02562, arXiv:2208.10450]. It is characterized by extremely low metallicity [arXiv:2207.10034, arXiv:2208.03281], high ionization [arXiv:2207.12388], and vigorous star formation driven by young stellar clumps [arXiv:2208.10450, arXiv:2212.08670].

sources.summary: The paper treats s04590 as a gravitationally lensed z>7 JWST/NIRSpec galaxy, detecting rest-frame optical nebular lines and measuring a systemic redshift of 8.495 plus nebular properties.

quotes.quote: We consider here the three lensed galaxies of the SMACS J0723.3-7327 galaxy cluster at z>7 , which have the JWST source IDs of s04590, s06355, and s10612. The object ID numbers come from the input source catalog used for the NIRSpec micro-shutter array MSA; ferruit22 configuration planning. These objects are gravitationally-lensed galaxies by a massive galaxy cluster at z = 0.3877 , discovered by the southern extension of the Massive Cluster Survey MACS; ebeling01, repp18 .

The JWST/NIRspec spectra detect several rest-frame optical nebular emission lines in the two different visits (o007 and o008) for s04590, s06355, and s10612, which provide the opportunity to measure the redshift and important physical properties, including the reddening, the total and relative metallicities, and the ionization parameter. Source Redshift We have measured the systemic redshift of s04590,s06355, and s10612 using the most reliable strong emission lines in the spectra covered by NIRSpec. Specifically, we measured the observed central wavelengths of the five strongest emission lines in the NIRSpec spectrum (H , H , H , and [ O 3 ] 4959,5007) using Gaussian profile fits. Relative to the rest-frame vacuum wavelengths, we determine average redshifts of 8.495 for s04590, 7.665 for s06355, and 7.659 for s10612.

sources.summary: The paper treats I1 as a spectroscopically confirmed reionization-era dwarf galaxy, revises its delensed mass, and finds two very young clumps whose feedback likely ionizes the host.

quotes.quote: Three of these galaxies, I1, I4 and I10, have been recently studied by Schaerer2022 , who reported (M_*) of 9, 9.2, 8.9, respectively. the lensing model used in this work, predicts slightly lower magnification for system I1. We apply our mean magnification to estimate the delensed masses to be 2.3 10^8 , 1.3 10^9 , and 5.8 10^8 for system I1, I4, I10. These systems are all consistent with being metal poor, dwarf galaxies Schaerer2022 .

The three galaxies investigated by Schaerer2022 host all very young clumps. The two clumps in I1 have ages of 2 Myr. The clumps in I4 have ages from 1 to 8 Myr, while the two clumps in I10 have ages of 5 and 9 Myr. Feedback from these young, massive and compact clumps is most likely the source of ionisation in their host galaxies, responsible for very high O equivalent width and high O /O ratio.

sources.summary: The paper identifies 04590 as the z=8.495 galaxy with the lowest metallicity among the three z~8 sources, based on direct and strong-line estimates.

quotes.quote: The spectra of the three sources show nebular emission lines, many of them detected with high significance ( >3 ). These include Balmer lines (H , H , H ), [ O ii ] and [ O iii ] , [ Ne iii ] , and He i . In particular, the auroral \ line -- key for accurate determinations of the O/H abundance using the direct method -- is detected in all galaxies with a significance of 3.9-5.7 .

Different metallicity estimates can be obtained for the z=8.495 galaxy 04590 (see Table 1): From their finding of T_e 20 kK, conclude that < 7.69 ( <1/10 solar), from the empirical relation between T_e and \ cf. Perez-Montero2021Extreme-emissio . For 04590 we find = 7.39 with our measurements, and = 7.24 using the line ratios reported by , who derived = 6.99 0.11 by the direct method. All studies so far agree that 04590 shows the lowest metallicity among the three z 8 galaxies.

sources.summary: The paper identifies 04590 as a SMACS 0723 EoR galaxy whose [O III]/H ratio resembles local dwarfs, with large colour-based and spectroscopic [O III] equivalent widths.

quotes.quote: The other two objects (04590 and 05144) have comparable [ O III ] 5007 /H line ratios to z=0 dwarf galaxies. These results are entirely consistent with the (extreme ends) of the FLARES predictions, which do not include the AGN contribution to these lines. Hence the [ O III ] 5007 /H emission in these four galaxies is consistent with what is seen in what is considered extreme, vigorously star-forming dwarf galaxies in the local Universe see e.g. Izotov2021a .

We find colour-based rest-frame equivalent widths of 488 , 611 , 1033 \ and 660 , for galaxies 04590, 05144, 06355 and 10612, respectively. These are indeed roughly comparable (to within 0.15 dex) to our estimates from spectroscopy, namely 529 , 440 , 1002 \ and 944 .

As mentioned earlier, while the continuum is clearly detected for 06355, the derived measurements are more sensitive to the exact wavelength range adopted for continuum estimation for galaxies 04590, 05144 and 10612.

sources.summary: The paper finds Galaxy ID 04590 is a compact, extremely metal-poor z>7 galaxy with a steeply rising star-formation history and rapid pristine gas accretion.

quotes.quote: curti23 used the [ OIII ] 4363 auroral line to measure metallicities with the direct T_ e method, finding Galaxy ID 04590 to be extremely metal poor ( 12+ ( O / H ) 7 ), Galaxy ID 10612 to be about 1/10 solar and Galaxy ID 06355 to be about one-third solar. The latter two objects are marginally consistent with the Fundamental Metallicity Relation FMR; mannucci10, onodera16, curti20, suzuki21, papovich22_clear , while Galaxy ID 04590 deviates significantly from it, consistent with being far from the smooth equilibrium between gas flows, star formation and metal enrichment in place at later epochs. This possibly indicates that we enter at z>7 another epoch of galaxy formation, where galactic systems are out of equilibrium and evolve more stochastically.

The possible cause from this can be found in the right panel of Figure fig:metallicity_sfh : these two galaxies have very different sSFR surface densities with _ sSFR =328_ 94 ^ +156 Gyr ^ 1 kpc ^ 2 (Galaxy ID 04590) and 79_ 21 ^ +42 Gyr ^ 1 kpc ^ 2 (Galaxy ID 06355), given by the fact that Galaxy ID 04590 is a factor of 2-3 times more compact and lower in stellar mass than Galaxy ID 06355 (Figures fig:data4590 and fig:data6355 ). Galaxy ID 04590 is vigorously forming stars in a very compact configuration. This is consistent with a scenario in which Galaxy ID 04590 is undergoing rapid accretion of pristine gas, which leads to a steeply rising SFH and a high (s)SFR surface density.

sources.summary: The paper finds 4590 has C III] emission comparable to Hβ, consistent with either non-thermal ionization or a moderate-to-high ionization starburst rather than a clear AGN classification.

quotes.quote: Starting first with 4590, it shows 1907,1909 in emission as also noted by  arellano-cordovaFirstLookAbundance2022 . This line is ubiquitous in AGN, but it is also commonly seen in star-forming galaxies starkUltravioletEmissionLines2014,rigbyIIIEmissionStarforming2015,masedaMUSEHubbleUltra2017a,ravindranathSemiforbiddenIIIL19092020 , in particular in those that show high intensity star bursts, so it is perhaps not surprising that it is seen here but as we will see, it does offer some extra information on the source.

From the unmodified spectrum I find a 1907,1909 / ratio of 1.66_ 0.48 ^ +0.97 , and from the corrected spectrum I find 0.51^ +0.16 _ 0.13 . Despite the substantial uncertainties, we can robustly conclude that the 1907,1909 flux is comparable to that of . This is indicative of either a non-thermal ionization source or a moderate to high ionization parameter in a star-burst.

sources.summary: JWST/NIRSpec observations show ID 4590 as a strongly lensed z=8.5 galaxy with high-ionization, low-metallicity gas, the sample’s lowest stellar mass and metallicity, and very high electron temperature.

quotes.quote: In this paper we investigate the emission-line properties of five z>5 galaxies with /NIRSpec spectroscopy from Early Release Observations of the galaxy cluster SMACS J0723.3–7327 (henceforth SMACS 0723). In Section 4 we compare our new z>5 line-ratio measurements with previous observations at lower redshift and with theoretical photoionization models, finding that the high-redshift galaxies have very high ionization ( (Q/ [ cm s ^ 1 ] ) 8-9 ) and low (but nonzero) metallicities ( Z/Z_ 0.1 ). In this paper we focus on the NIRSpec observations of the 5 galaxies at z>5 that were presented by carnall22 .

ID 4590 has an estimated electron temperature that exceeds the maximum value ( (T_ e / K ) 4.3 ) used in the calibration of perezmontero21 and so its low metallicity represents a (modest) extrapolation of the relation. The other three z>6 galaxies all have lower metallicities and lower stellar masses, with ID 4590 at z=8.5 having the lowest metallicity and lowest stellar mass of the sample.

sources.summary: The paper describes ID4590 as the highest-redshift target, a compact z=8.498 galaxy with a star-forming clump, young central population, high line equivalent widths, and gradients across physical maps.

quotes.quote: Figure  results_z8.498 shows the results for the galaxy ID4590, which is the highest redshift z=8.498 in this work. It is a very compact source, with only 31 pixels where S/N > 2 for both F150W and F200W. We see a star forming clump, which also corresponds to the highest inferred stellar mass, and towards the dustiest zone in the A_V map. We see a marginally unresolved centrally located clump of young stellar population, which is not entirely co-spatial with the star-forming burst, but traces perfectly the higher equivalent width of the inferred [ O 3 ] and H emission lines. On top of this, the empirical colour maps display a clear gradient, which follows the ones observed for the SFRD, SMD, A_V and maps.

gmw_00000372.name: ID 5144
gmw_00000372.overview: ID 5144 is a gravitationally lensed dwarf galaxy at a redshift of approximately 6.4 [arXiv:2306.07940, arXiv:2208.10450]. It features extended oxygen emission [arXiv:2306.07940], high ionization [arXiv:2207.12388], and multiple stellar clumps that indicate ongoing star formation over tens of millions of years [arXiv:2208.10450].

sources.summary: The paper identifies ERO 05144 as a magnified z=6.378 galaxy with extended [O III] emission and broad-line residuals favoring an outflow model.

quotes.quote: One of the four objects, ERO 05144, is a lensed galaxy with a magnification factor = 3.18 . In Figure fig:z6b_img , we present the rest-frame ultraviolet (UV), rest-frame optical continuum, and the [ O iii ] emission images, together with the radial profiles derived with respect to the center positions in the rest-frame optical continuum images. In the [ O iii ] emission line images, all of these four objects have [ O iii ] emission extended beyond the stellar continua, while the radial profiles also confirm such extensions beyond 1.5 kpc.

The remaining object, ERO 05144, has a 4-5 residual around the [ O iii ] 5007 emission line, indicating the possible existence of ongoing outflow. We fit the H and [ O iii ] 4959, 5007 emission lines of ERO 05144 again, adding a broad Gaussian component to the [ O iii ] 4959 and [ O iii ] 5007 emission line, respectively (hereafter ``outflow model"). We tie the velocity shifts ( v , in km s ^ 1 ) and the line widths (FWHM _ b , in km s ^ 1 ) of these two Gaussian components.
For ERO 05144, we obtain BIC =46.4 , suggesting that the outflow model better describes the observed data.

sources.summary: The paper identifies 05144 as a SMACS 0723 EoR galaxy with line ratios comparable to local dwarfs and large [O III] equivalent-width estimates.

quotes.quote: The other two objects (04590 and 05144) have comparable [ O III ] 5007 /H line ratios to z=0 dwarf galaxies. These results are entirely consistent with the (extreme ends) of the FLARES predictions, which do not include the AGN contribution to these lines. Hence the [ O III ] 5007 /H emission in these four galaxies is consistent with what is seen in what is considered extreme, vigorously star-forming dwarf galaxies in the local Universe see e.g. Izotov2021a .

We find colour-based rest-frame equivalent widths of 488 , 611 , 1033 \ and 660 , for galaxies 04590, 05144, 06355 and 10612, respectively. These are indeed roughly comparable (to within 0.15 dex) to our estimates from spectroscopy, namely 529 , 440 , 1002 \ and 944 .

As mentioned earlier, while the continuum is clearly detected for 06355, the derived measurements are more sensitive to the exact wavelength range adopted for continuum estimation for galaxies 04590, 05144 and 10612.

sources.summary: JWST/NIRSpec observations include ID 5144 as a modestly lensed z=6.4 galaxy whose measured line ratios indicate high ionization, high pressure, low metallicity, and the sample’s highest reported ratio.

quotes.quote: In this paper we investigate the emission-line properties of five z>5 galaxies with /NIRSpec spectroscopy from Early Release Observations of the galaxy cluster SMACS J0723.3–7327 (henceforth SMACS 0723). In Section 4 we compare our new z>5 line-ratio measurements with previous observations at lower redshift and with theoretical photoionization models, finding that the high-redshift galaxies have very high ionization ( (Q/ [ cm s ^ 1 ] ) 8-9 ) and low (but nonzero) metallicities ( Z/Z_ 0.1 ). In this paper we focus on the NIRSpec observations of the 5 galaxies at z>5 that were presented by carnall22 .

We note that there is not an obvious trend of / with redshift among the z>5 galaxies: for example a z=7.7 (ID 6355) galaxy has the lowest / and a z=6.4 (ID 5144) galaxy has the highest / , with ratios that are > 3 inconsistent given their observational uncertainties. This likely indicates a diversity of / ratios in individual z>5 galaxies, perhaps associated with the diversity of stellar mass, star formation rate, abundances, and/or age among this sample.

sources.summary: The paper describes I2 as a z=6.38 dwarf galaxy with blue and evolved clumps, long-lived structures, and ongoing star formation over tens of Myr.

quotes.quote: Very interestingly, the fourth galaxy I2, at redshift 6.38, hosts two blue clumps (C4 and C5, detected only in the F150W and f200W) for which we could not derive an age, and three evolved clumps with ages between 50 and 80 Myr. These three clumps have all > 1 , suggesting that are long-lived structures in the system and formed about redshift 6.9. Since this system is detected in O (see Table tab:sample ), we expect the two blue clumps to be fairly young, hinting that star formation can go on in these dwarf galaxies for 10s of Myr.

gmw_00000373.name: ID 6355
gmw_00000373.overview: ID 6355 is a gravitationally lensed [arXiv:2208.02562], metal-poor dwarf galaxy [arXiv:2208.10450] at a redshift of approximately 7.66 [arXiv:2207.14265, arXiv:2208.02562]. It exhibits a clumpy, extended morphology suggestive of a merger [arXiv:2207.12388, arXiv:2208.03281] and hosts a narrow-line active galactic nucleus [arXiv:2208.07467] alongside intense starburst activity [arXiv:2208.03281].

sources.summary: The paper presents 06355 as a z=7.664 EoR galaxy with rich rest-optical emission lines, extreme [O III] equivalent width, and colours potentially explainable by AGN.

quotes.quote: Spectral fitting process In order to outline our spectral fitting process, we show the spectrum of the z=7.664 galaxy (ID: 06355) in Fig. fig:06355_spectrum . This spectrum is a striking example of the quality of spectra that NIRSpec will obtain, clearly showing a multitude of emission lines in the rest-frame optical, but now, for the first time, having been obtained for a galaxy deep within the epoch of reionisation. Indeed, as demonstrated in Fig. fig:06355_spectrum , we see [ O III ] 4959, 5007 emission, the Balmer lines H , H , H , H , as well as [ Ne III ] 3869, 3967 , [ O II ] 3737, 3729 and even the characteristically faint emission from the [ O III ] 4363 auroral line. Thus, given the incredibly bright emission lines relative to the continuum level (which has not been subtracted) it is evident that the red (F356W F444W) colours, driven by the strong magnitude excess in the F444W filter, are attributable to the high EW emission of [ O III ] 5007 , rather than a prominent Balmer break in the galaxy spectrum.

We find colour-based rest-frame equivalent widths of 488 , 611 , 1033 \ and 660 , for galaxies 04590, 05144, 06355 and 10612, respectively. These are indeed roughly comparable (to within 0.15 dex) to our estimates from spectroscopy, namely 529 , 440 , 1002 \ and 944 .

From looking at the positions of our four EoR galaxies in the F150W F200W, F200W F277W plane, we see their colours are largely consistent with what one would expect for star-forming galaxies, with the possible exception of galaxy 06355 which has colours that could potentially be explained by AGN. Indeed, we note that this is also the galaxy with the most massive stellar mass in our sample, as estimated from our SED fitting.

sources.summary: The paper treats s06355 as a gravitationally lensed z>7 JWST/NIRSpec galaxy, detecting rest-frame optical nebular lines and measuring a systemic redshift of 7.665 plus nebular properties.

quotes.quote: We consider here the three lensed galaxies of the SMACS J0723.3-7327 galaxy cluster at z>7 , which have the JWST source IDs of s04590, s06355, and s10612. The object ID numbers come from the input source catalog used for the NIRSpec micro-shutter array MSA; ferruit22 configuration planning. These objects are gravitationally-lensed galaxies by a massive galaxy cluster at z = 0.3877 , discovered by the southern extension of the Massive Cluster Survey MACS; ebeling01, repp18 .

The JWST/NIRspec spectra detect several rest-frame optical nebular emission lines in the two different visits (o007 and o008) for s04590, s06355, and s10612, which provide the opportunity to measure the redshift and important physical properties, including the reddening, the total and relative metallicities, and the ionization parameter. Source Redshift We have measured the systemic redshift of s04590,s06355, and s10612 using the most reliable strong emission lines in the spectra covered by NIRSpec. Specifically, we measured the observed central wavelengths of the five strongest emission lines in the NIRSpec spectrum (H , H , H , and [ O 3 ] 4959,5007) using Gaussian profile fits. Relative to the rest-frame vacuum wavelengths, we determine average redshifts of 8.495 for s04590, 7.665 for s06355, and 7.659 for s10612.

sources.summary: JWST/NIRSpec observations include ID 6355 as a z=7.7 lensed galaxy with the sample’s lowest reported ratio, highest metallicity, bright lines, high star formation, and clumpy merger-like morphology.

quotes.quote: In this paper we investigate the emission-line properties of five z>5 galaxies with /NIRSpec spectroscopy from Early Release Observations of the galaxy cluster SMACS J0723.3–7327 (henceforth SMACS 0723). In Section 4 we compare our new z>5 line-ratio measurements with previous observations at lower redshift and with theoretical photoionization models, finding that the high-redshift galaxies have very high ionization ( (Q/ [ cm s ^ 1 ] ) 8-9 ) and low (but nonzero) metallicities ( Z/Z_ 0.1 ). In this paper we focus on the NIRSpec observations of the 5 galaxies at z>5 that were presented by carnall22 .

We note that there is not an obvious trend of / with redshift among the z>5 galaxies: for example a z=7.7 (ID 6355) galaxy has the lowest / and a z=6.4 (ID 5144) galaxy has the highest / , with ratios that are > 3 inconsistent given their observational uncertainties. The galaxy with the highest metallicity ( 12+ ( O/H )=8.1 for ID 6355) has very bright emission lines, implying a very high star formation rate. This galaxy also has a clumpy and extended morphology that is suggestive of a merger carnall22 .

sources.summary: The paper argues that 6355 is a z=7.665 low-mass galaxy hosting a narrow-line AGN, based on its clear Ne IV emission and energetic ionization requirements.

quotes.quote: Turning now to 6355, this is an interesting case because it shows a clear detection of 2422,2424 , as shown in Figure  fig:neiv . The creation of Ne ^ +++ requires photons with energies >63.45 eV and it is therefore seen only in very energetic environments. It is commonly used as a density indicator in bright planetary nebulae e.g. keenanEmissionLinesNeIV1998,allerRemarkablyHighExcitation1999 and it is also seen regularly in AGNs e.g. teraoMultilineAssessmentNarrowline2022 .

Taking the measurements at face value, the flux is 1.45 10^ 19 erg/s/cm ^2 , which gives a 2422,2424 / \ = 0.55 . I am not aware of a suitable comparison sample, but I note that  teraoMultilineAssessmentNarrowline2022 find that 1907,1909 is 2 times stronger than 2422,2424 in their sample of radio galaxies, and at low metallicity 1907,1909 can easily be comparable to , so this ratio seems entirely reasonable. In conclusion it seems that 6355 is hosting a narrow-line AGN in a galaxy with stellar mass <10^9 M _ at z=7.665 .

sources.summary: The paper treats I4 as a reionization-era metal-poor dwarf galaxy, estimating its delensed mass and finding clumps aged 1-8 Myr whose feedback likely contributes to ionizing emission.

quotes.quote: Three of these galaxies, I1, I4 and I10, have been recently studied by Schaerer2022 , who reported (M_*) of 9, 9.2, 8.9, respectively. the lensing model used in this work, predicts slightly lower magnification for system I1. We apply our mean magnification to estimate the delensed masses to be 2.3 10^8 , 1.3 10^9 , and 5.8 10^8 for system I1, I4, I10. These systems are all consistent with being metal poor, dwarf galaxies Schaerer2022 .

The three galaxies investigated by Schaerer2022 host all very young clumps. The two clumps in I1 have ages of 2 Myr. The clumps in I4 have ages from 1 to 8 Myr, while the two clumps in I10 have ages of 5 and 9 Myr. Feedback from these young, massive and compact clumps is most likely the source of ionisation in their host galaxies, responsible for very high O equivalent width and high O /O ratio.

sources.summary: The paper adds the z=7.665 star-forming galaxy ID 6355, using JWST/NIRSpec line ratios and non-detections to constrain its ionizing spectrum.

quotes.quote: We add a star forming galaxy at z=7.665 , ID 6355, recently identified in James Webb Space Telescope (JWST) observations with [ Ne iv ] 2424 line detection to our sample ( 2023MNRAS.518..425C, 2022arXiv220807467B ). The data was taken by JWST/NIRSpec in Early Release Observations (ERO; 2022ApJ...936L..14P ).

For ID 6355, we use emission line fluxes and flux ratios listed in Table JWST_fluxes . Because [ Ne v ] 3426 line is not detected in ID 6355, we use [ Ne iv ] 2424/ [ Ne iii ] 3869 instead of [ Ne v ] 3426/ [ Ne iii ] 3869. Because [ He ii ] 4686 emission line is not detected in ID 6355, we search the best-fit parameters that are consistent with three sigma non-detection of the [ He ii ] 4686 line.
For ID 6355, we extrapolate the power-law component of the best-fit spectrum up to 100 eV and calculate the L_ EUV .

sources.summary: The paper shows a combined spectrum for 06355 at z=7.7, detects its continuum, and includes it among z~8 galaxies with significant nebular and auroral lines.

quotes.quote: An example of the combined spectrum of 06355 at z 7.7 is shown in Fig. fig_spec . The spectra of the three sources show nebular emission lines, many of them detected with high significance ( >3 ). These include Balmer lines (H , H , H ), [ O ii ] and [ O iii ] , [ Ne iii ] , and He i . In particular, the auroral \ line -- key for accurate determinations of the O/H abundance using the direct method -- is detected in all galaxies with a significance of 3.9-5.7 . The continuum is clearly detected in the two z 7.7 sources (10612 and 06355, see Fig. fig_spec ) allowing the determination of the equivalent widths of the lines (Table tab_1 ).

sources.summary: The paper models Galaxy ID 06355 as the most massive of the three z>7 galaxies, an extended clumpy object undergoing a strong burst likely triggered by mergers or instability.

quotes.quote: The SFHs are increasing for all three galaxies and have similar stellar masses of about 10^8 M_ , with Galaxy ID 04590 and 10612 being the least massive ( M_ 10^ 7.8 M_ ) and and Galaxy ID 06355 being the most massive galaxy ( M_ 10^ 8.6 M_ ).

Galaxy ID 06355 is going through an even larger burst of star formation. In this case, older ages can largely be ruled out This galaxy has the reddest F444W-F356W colour, indicating strong emission lines. and we inferred the youngest age of this sample with 3_ 1 ^ +186 Myr. It is interesting to note that this galaxies is more extended than Galaxy ID 4590 and is a clumpy object (Figure fig:data6355 ), consistent with the idea that mergers or an internal, gravitational instability triggered this intense starburst.

The possible cause from this can be found in the right panel of Figure fig:metallicity_sfh : these two galaxies have very different sSFR surface densities with _ sSFR =328_ 94 ^ +156 Gyr ^ 1 kpc ^ 2 (Galaxy ID 04590) and 79_ 21 ^ +42 Gyr ^ 1 kpc ^ 2 (Galaxy ID 06355), given by the fact that Galaxy ID 04590 is a factor of 2-3 times more compact and lower in stellar mass than Galaxy ID 06355 (Figures fig:data4590 and fig:data6355 ).

sources.summary: The paper identifies SMACS0723 PC 6 as a spectroscopically confirmed z=7.665 protocluster galaxy, among the two more massive members and consistent with the z 7 star-forming main sequence.

quotes.quote: Two objects among the 35 observed have a similar redshift of z=7.665 and z=7.659 (herafter SMACS0723 PC 6 and SMACS0723 PC 7) and show several UV lines such as [ OIII ] 4959, 5007 and [ OII ] 3727, 3729 (Figure fig:spectra ). Our redshift measurements are consistent with what has been previously measured by other groups (e.g. 2022arXiv220712375C , 2022arXiv220708778C , 2022arXiv220712338A , 2022arXiv220713693K , 2022arXiv220714265T ).

As expected in a protocluster environment (e.g. 2015MNRAS.452.2528M , 2021MNRAS.501.1803L , 2021MNRAS.504.5054A , 2022A&A...664A.155G ), 2 members are more massive than the others, namely SMACS0723 PC 6 and SMACS0723 PC 7, with a stellar mass 10 ^9 M_ . Figure fig:main-sequence shows the position of the 8 galaxies in our sample on a M_ vs SFR diagram compared with previous findings at z 7 ( 2022arXiv220711135L , 2022arXiv220307392T ). It confirms that all these sources have properties consistent with what is expected in terms of the star-formation main sequence currently known at z \ 7.

sources.summary: The paper finds ID6355 at z=7.665 has the most extreme equivalent widths, an extended high-EW region, and a shell of older stars supported by colour gradients and pixel-level fits.

quotes.quote: Figure  results_z7.665 shows the resulting maps for the galaxy ID6355 at z=7.665 . It is the galaxy with most extreme EWs (reaching 4000  Å). We see that the region with very high EW is very extended for this galaxy, leaving barely a shell where we find underlying older stellar populations, which are otherwise outshined (or not present) by the younger stars in these strong line emission regions. The clear gradients in the empirical colours give us confidence that this shell is real and not an artifact of the age-dust degeneracy in the SED fitting process, which we also discuss in further detail in section:caveats . On top of this, the shell is larger than the PSF scale.

sources.summary: The paper identifies object 6355 as a high-redshift JWST source with a near-twin redshift object, extreme Balmer jump, highest mass/SFR in the sample, and possible merger structure.

quotes.quote: Interestingly, two objects (6355 and 10612) display almost identical redshifts. These objects are however far from the cluster centre, and are not listed as multiple images in currently available lensing analyses of this field ( Mahler2022, Pascale2022, Caminha2022 ).
The highest mass, SFR and most extreme Balmer jump all belong to object 6355, which shows clear structure in the F150W imaging, potentially indicative of an ongoing merger event.

gmw_00000374.name: ID 10612
gmw_00000374.overview: ID 10612 is a weakly magnified, gravitationally lensed galaxy at a redshift of 7.659 [arXiv:2208.02562, arXiv:2208.03281]. It consists of two dominant components that suggest a merger in progress [arXiv:2208.03281], and its extreme emission line ratios indicate high excitation and a possible active galactic nucleus [arXiv:2207.10034, arXiv:2208.07467].

sources.summary: The paper analyzes J072320-732604 as a z=7.659 JWST ERO galaxy with measured emission-line ratios, Magellanic-Cloud-like metallicity range, compact size, and Green Pea-like properties.

quotes.quote: J072326-732656 (04590) J072322-732606 (06355) J072320-732604 (10612) SMACS 0723 SMACS 0723.3-7327 table 1 Finding Peas in the Early Universe Rhoads et al. Finding Peas in the Early Universe with JWST James E. Rhoads Astrophysics Science Division, NASA Goddard Space Flight Center, 8800 Greenbelt Road, Greenbelt, Maryland, 20771, USA James.E.Rhoads@nasa.gov Isak G. B. Wold Astrophysics Science Division, NASA Goddard Space Flight Center, 8800 Greenbelt Road, Greenbelt, Maryland, 20771, USA Department of Physics, The Catholic University of America, Washington, DC 20064, USA Center for Research and Exploration in Space Science and Technology, NASA/GSFC, Greenbelt, MD 20771 Santosh Harish School of Earth and Space Exploration, Arizona State University, Tempe, AZ 85287, USA Keunho J. Kim Department of Physics, University of Cincinnati, Cincinnati, OH 4521, USA John Pharo School of Earth and Space Exploration, Arizona State University, Tempe, AZ 85287, USA Sangeeta Malhotra Astrophysics Science Division, NASA Goddard Space Flight Center, 8800 Greenbelt Road, Greenbelt, Maryland, 20771, USA Sangeeta.Malhotra@nasa.gov Austen Gabrielpillai Astrophysics Science Division, NASA Goddard Space Flight Center, 8800 Greenbelt Road, Greenbelt, Maryland, 20771, USA Tianxing Jiang School of Earth and Space Exploration, Arizona State University, Tempe, AZ 85287, USA Huan Yang Las Campanas Observatory, Carnegie Institution of Washington, Casilla 601, La Serena, Chile The Early Release Observations (EROs) of JWST beautifully demonstrate the promise of JWST in characterizing the universe at cosmic dawn. We analyze the ERO spectra of three z 8 galaxies to determine their metallicities, gas temperatures and ionization. These galaxies offer the first opportunity to understand the physical properties of epoch-of-reionization galaxies through detailed rest-optical emission line spectroscopy. We show that these objects have metal abundances 12+ O/H 6.9 - 8.2 , based on both the T_e method and on a recent calibration of the R_ 23 metallicity indicator. Since the spectra are some of the earliest science data from JWST, we compare several line ratios with values expected from robust physics, to validate our measurement procedures. We compare the abundances and emission line ratios to a nearby sample of Green Pea galaxies--- a population of nearby emission line galaxies whose UV properties resemble epoch-of-reionization galaxies, and which often have large Lyman continuum escape fractions. The JWST data show striking further similarities between these high redshift galaxies and nearby Green Peas.

We have analyzed the rest-frame optical spectra of three epoch-of-reionization galaxies from the JWST Early Release Observations on the \ field. These objects are all strong line emitters, with spectra reminiscent of nearby Green Pea galaxies, and also of narrowband-selected extreme emission line galaxies at intermediate redshifts. This result supports earlier conclusions that Green Peas are among the best nearby analogs to high redshift galaxies. The highest redshift and most extreme among these galaxies, \ at z= 8.495 , has a very low gas phase metallicity, 6.9 0.15 from the T_e method, and 7.1 from the Jiang2019 calibration of the strong line R _23 method. Its / \ ratio may demand the presence of a heating mechanism beyond photoheating by a young starburst. The other two galaxies, \ at z=7.664 and \ at z=7.659 have appreciably higher metallicities, in the range 7.7 < < 8.2 . They demonstrate empirically that galaxy formation and stellar nucleosynthesis can achieve a metal abundance comparable to the Magellanic Clouds Russell1992 within just 700 Myr after the Big Bang. All three of these galaxies share the compact sizes and high surface brightnesses that characterize \ emitting galaxies across a wide range of redshifts, from z 6.5 down to z 0 Malhotra2012,Kim2020,Kim2021 .

sources.summary: The paper treats 10612 as a distinct z=7.66 EoR source close to 06355, with extreme line ratios and strong [O III] equivalent-width estimates.

quotes.quote: We note, as has already been pointed out in Carnall2022 , that the two z 7.66 sources (IDs 6355, 10612) are closely separated both on the sky ( 11'' ) and in redshift ( z=0.005 ). Assuming a Planck2020 cosmology, this corresponds to a projected physical separation and line-of-sight separation of 56 kpc and 179 kpc respectively. Alternatively, the redshift differential translates into a velocity offset of 173 km s ^ 1 . We have inspected the lensing model maps and verified that these are two distinct (i.e. not multiply imaged) sources.

Just as the z 2 MOSDEF galaxies tend to have elevated [ O III ] 5007 /H line ratios at a given stellar mass compared to the local population which is attributed to z 2 galaxies having higher sSFR at a given stellar mass than galaxies at z 0 , Dickey2016 , we find that two of our sources (06355 and 10612) exhibit line ratios that are on the extreme end of the local trends.

We find colour-based rest-frame equivalent widths of 488 , 611 , 1033 \ and 660 , for galaxies 04590, 05144, 06355 and 10612, respectively. These are indeed roughly comparable (to within 0.15 dex) to our estimates from spectroscopy, namely 529 , 440 , 1002 \ and 944 .

sources.summary: The paper treats s10612 as a gravitationally lensed z>7 JWST/NIRSpec galaxy, detecting rest-frame optical nebular lines and measuring a systemic redshift of 7.659 plus nebular properties.

quotes.quote: We consider here the three lensed galaxies of the SMACS J0723.3-7327 galaxy cluster at z>7 , which have the JWST source IDs of s04590, s06355, and s10612. The object ID numbers come from the input source catalog used for the NIRSpec micro-shutter array MSA; ferruit22 configuration planning. These objects are gravitationally-lensed galaxies by a massive galaxy cluster at z = 0.3877 , discovered by the southern extension of the Massive Cluster Survey MACS; ebeling01, repp18 .

The JWST/NIRspec spectra detect several rest-frame optical nebular emission lines in the two different visits (o007 and o008) for s04590, s06355, and s10612, which provide the opportunity to measure the redshift and important physical properties, including the reddening, the total and relative metallicities, and the ionization parameter. Source Redshift We have measured the systemic redshift of s04590,s06355, and s10612 using the most reliable strong emission lines in the spectra covered by NIRSpec. Specifically, we measured the observed central wavelengths of the five strongest emission lines in the NIRSpec spectrum (H , H , H , and [ O 3 ] 4959,5007) using Gaussian profile fits. Relative to the rest-frame vacuum wavelengths, we determine average redshifts of 8.495 for s04590, 7.665 for s06355, and 7.659 for s10612.

sources.summary: The paper describes Galaxy ID 10612 as a weakly magnified z>7 galaxy with two dominant components, young stellar age, elevated earlier star formation, and a likely merger in progress.

quotes.quote: This yields half-light radii of 0.039'' 0.004 ( 0.18 0.02 kpc), 0.077'' 0.008 ( 0.39 0.04 kpc) and 0.100'' 0.010 ( 0.51 0.05 kpc) respectively for Galaxy ID 04590, 06355, and 10612. The size for Galaxy ID 10612 is driven by the separation between the two dominant components, which are each 0.03'' in half-light radius. We note that we have not corrected these sizes for lensing distortions (Section subsec:magnification ). We expect this effect to be small for Galaxy ID 06355 and 10612, because they are only weakly magnified.

Galaxy ID 10612 has an age of t_ 50 =3_ 1 ^ +59 Myr. The SFH shows a hint of elevated star formation 30-60 Myr ago. Together with insight that this source is made-up of two components (Figure fig:data10612 ), it is consistent with a picture where we are witnessing a merger in progress.

sources.summary: The paper identifies SMACS0723 PC 7 as a spectroscopically confirmed z=7.659 protocluster galaxy, the field's most massive z=7.66 candidate, with stellar and halo masses estimated from SED fitting.

quotes.quote: Two objects among the 35 observed have a similar redshift of z=7.665 and z=7.659 (herafter SMACS0723 PC 6 and SMACS0723 PC 7) and show several UV lines such as [ OIII ] 4959, 5007 and [ OII ] 3727, 3729 (Figure fig:spectra ). Our redshift measurements are consistent with what has been previously measured by other groups (e.g. 2022arXiv220712375C , 2022arXiv220708778C , 2022arXiv220712338A , 2022arXiv220713693K , 2022arXiv220714265T ).

As expected in a protocluster environment (e.g. 2015MNRAS.452.2528M , 2021MNRAS.501.1803L , 2021MNRAS.504.5054A , 2022A&A...664A.155G ), 2 members are more massive than the others, namely SMACS0723 PC 6 and SMACS0723 PC 7, with a stellar mass 10 ^9 M_ . Figure fig:main-sequence shows the position of the 8 galaxies in our sample on a M_ vs SFR diagram compared with previous findings at z 7 ( 2022arXiv220711135L , 2022arXiv220307392T ). It confirms that all these sources have properties consistent with what is expected in terms of the star-formation main sequence currently known at z \ 7.

Finally, we can also determine the halo mass of the most massive galaxy in our sample from its stellar mass, which should dominate the total halo mass of the protocluster. SMACS0723 PC 7 has a stellar mass of 5.31 ^ +0.54 _ 0.41 10^8 M _ corrected for magnification , which converts into a minimum halo mass of 6.86 ^ +2.51 _ 2.42 10 ^ 10 .

sources.summary: JWST/NIRSpec observations include ID 10612 among the z>5 lensed galaxies; one line ratio is measured from a single visit because the second visit’s 3728 line is contaminated.

quotes.quote: All 5 of these galaxies are gravitationally lensed by the foreground cluster: ID 4590 ( z=8.5 ) has a magnification factor of 8, while the other 4 galaxies have more modest magnification factors of 1.5-2 (using the parametric model of pascale22 ). We determined the spectroscopic redshift for each source using the best-fit line center for 5008 , which was the brightest emission line in each spectrum. Most of the emission lines are measured from the G395M spectrum, with bluer lines measured in the G235M spectrum for the lower-redshift sources.

In a few cases, a line flux cannot be measured due to contaminating emission that extends beyond the main spectral trace (likely from a detector artifact or serendipitous source) : ID 4590, second visit: 4960 ID 8140, first visit: 4364 ID 10612, second visit: 3728 Table tbl:ratios presents the measured line ratios, generally computed from the average line measurements of the two visits. The line ratio is measured from only one visit if a line of the ratio cannot be measured in the other visit, i.e. 5008/ 4960 for ID 4590 and / for ID 10612.

sources.summary: The paper treats I10 as a reionization-era metal-poor dwarf galaxy, estimating its delensed mass and finding two clumps aged 5 and 9 Myr that likely contribute ionizing feedback.

quotes.quote: Three of these galaxies, I1, I4 and I10, have been recently studied by Schaerer2022 , who reported (M_*) of 9, 9.2, 8.9, respectively. the lensing model used in this work, predicts slightly lower magnification for system I1. We apply our mean magnification to estimate the delensed masses to be 2.3 10^8 , 1.3 10^9 , and 5.8 10^8 for system I1, I4, I10. These systems are all consistent with being metal poor, dwarf galaxies Schaerer2022 .

The three galaxies investigated by Schaerer2022 host all very young clumps. The two clumps in I1 have ages of 2 Myr. The clumps in I4 have ages from 1 to 8 Myr, while the two clumps in I10 have ages of 5 and 9 Myr. Feedback from these young, massive and compact clumps is most likely the source of ionisation in their host galaxies, responsible for very high O equivalent width and high O /O ratio.

sources.summary: The paper treats 10612 as one of the two z=7.7 sources with detected continuum, high-excitation lines, and a possible nuclear-activity indication from line ratios.

quotes.quote: The continuum is clearly detected in the two z 7.7 sources (10612 and 06355, see Fig. fig_spec ) allowing the determination of the equivalent widths of the lines (Table tab_1 ).
From our measurements, one object (10612) shows a very high ratio / 10 and a strong \ line, which could indicate an active galaxy .

We have analysed the rest-frame optical spectra of two galaxies at z=7.7 and one at z=8.5 from the JWST Early Release Observations. The auroral \ line is significantly detected in all galaxies with 3.9-5.7 , allowing the determination of the O/H abundance (metallicity) using the direct method. All three galaxies show a high excitation, as measured by their line ratios of O32 =6-11 and Ne3O2 =0.4-0.7 , or even higher, according to and .

sources.summary: The paper uses ID10612 to show that integrated fitting recovers a very young burst, while resolved fitting reveals a wider star-formation history and older stellar mass build-up.

quotes.quote: We plot the sum of the SFH inferred for the spatial pixels, as well as the SFH estimated in the unresolved analysis, for the galaxy ID10612 at z=7.663 . The integrated SFH consists of a single burst with very young age ( 2  Myr), whereas the spatially resolved SFH is a distribution that covers a wider age range, reaching up to 300  Myr. For this galaxy, this would mean a formation redshift of z 12 . We see that, whereas the integrated analysis forms all stellar mass within less than 10 Myr, this only corresponds to 6 of the spatially resolved stellar mass, directly proving where the mass discrepancy is coming from.

sources.summary: The paper says 10612 lacks direct AGN lines but has extreme line ratios and ionization parameter values that suggest it might also harbour an AGN.

quotes.quote: I also show that 6355 is most likely a narrow-line active galactic nucleus (AGN) with M_*<10^9 M _ at z=7.66 , and argue that 10612 might also have an AGN contribution to its flux through comparison to photoionization models and low-redshift analogues.
In particular the 10612 which is easily the most extreme galaxy in the sample, must be questioned as to its ionization source as also pointed out by  schaererFirstLookJWST2022 .

However it is notable that 10612 is showing line ratios that at low- z are commonly seen in AGN, which coupled to an ionization parameter that reaches the model boundary at U=-1 , suggests that this galaxy also might harbour an AGN, thus one must interpret these results with some caution.

gmw_00000375.name: ID 8140
gmw_00000375.overview: ID 8140 is a gravitationally lensed galaxy at a redshift of approximately 5.3 [arXiv:2207.12388, arXiv:2212.08670]. It is a star-formation-dominated source [arXiv:2208.07467] featuring two distinct clumps, older stellar populations, and co-spatial star-formation and stellar-mass densities [arXiv:2212.08670].

sources.summary: JWST/NIRSpec observations include ID 8140 as a z=5.3 lensed galaxy, but its 4364 and related line measurements are marginal or unconstrained, preventing electron-temperature and metallicity estimates.

quotes.quote: All 5 of these galaxies are gravitationally lensed by the foreground cluster: ID 4590 ( z=8.5 ) has a magnification factor of 8, while the other 4 galaxies have more modest magnification factors of 1.5-2 (using the parametric model of pascale22 ). We determined the spectroscopic redshift for each source using the best-fit line center for 5008 , which was the brightest emission line in each spectrum. Most of the emission lines are measured from the G395M spectrum, with bluer lines measured in the G235M spectrum for the lower-redshift sources.

Neither 4364 nor \ are robustly ( > 3 ) detected in ID 8140 and so the 4364/ line ratio is unconstrained for this galaxy. ID 8140 ( z=5.3 ) is not shown in this Figure because its 4364 and \ lines are only marginally ( < 2 ) detected. Electron temperature and metallicity cannot be calculated for ID 8140 ( z=5.3 ) because its 4364/ line ratio is unconstrained.

sources.summary: The paper maps ID8140 at z=5.275 as a smooth high-redshift galaxy with co-spatial star-formation and stellar-mass density, older populations, strong gradients, and two clumps.

quotes.quote: Figure results_z5.275 displays the maps for galaxy ID8140, at redshift z=5.275 . We obtain smooth maps for all physical properties and colours. The SFRD and SMD appear entirely co-spatial, and the UV slope traces perfectly the dust obscuration map. This galaxy has 2 times older stellar populations compared with the average of the rest of the sample, in line with being the galaxy at lowest redshift. It shows strong colour, A_V and UV slope gradients, with two distinct clumps, one red and one blue.

sources.summary: The paper treats 8140 as a high-redshift, star-formation-dominated source: its direct-method 4363 detection is rejected, while photoionization-model abundances agree with CL01.

quotes.quote: For 8140 only  trumpPhysicalConditionsEmissionLine2022 present a temperature and abundance, I am unable to confirm their detection of 4363 and have decided to not use the direct method for this source.
The G16 model fits overall better but for 6355, 8140, and 10612 the resulting 12+ O/H constraints are in very good agreement with those found using CL01.

Turning now to 8140, this actually shows a strong 3426 line in the co-added spectrum. This is however not visible in 008 and there is no 4686 , as well as a very low 6584 / \ ratio so this should also be considered a star-formation dominated source with the 3426 detection considered spurious.

gmw_00000376.name: NGC 2179
gmw_00000376.overview: NGC 2179 is a disk galaxy that exhibits a strong central velocity gradient in its ionized gas [arXiv:astro-ph/0404575]. Observations of the galaxy's nucleus reveal disturbed gas kinematics alongside an irregular dust-lane morphology [arXiv:astro-ph/0404575].

sources.summary: The paper includes NGC 2179 among three disk galaxies selected for central ionized-gas gradients, finding disturbed gas kinematics and irregular dust-lane morphology in the STIS data.

quotes.quote: We present long-slit HST/STIS measurements of the ionized-gas kinematics in the nucleus of three disk galaxies, namely NGC 2179, NGC 4343, NGC 4435. The sample galaxies have been selected on the basis of their ground-based spectroscopy, for displaying a strong central velocity gradient for the ionized gas, which is consistent with the presence of a circum nuclear keplerian disk (CNKD, ; ) rotating around a super massive black hole (SMBH). For each target galaxy we obtained the H and [ NII ] 6583 \ kinematics along the major axis and two 0 25 parallel offset positions.
For NGC 2179 and NGC 4343 the spectrum reveals a disturbed gas kinematics. These two objects show an irregular dust-lane morphology, too.

gmw_00000377.name: NGC 4343
gmw_00000377.overview: NGC 4343 is a disk galaxy that exhibits a strong central velocity gradient in its ionized gas [arXiv:astro-ph/0404575]. Space telescope observations of the galaxy's nucleus reveal disturbed gas kinematics and an irregular dust-lane morphology [arXiv:astro-ph/0404575].

sources.summary: The paper includes NGC 4343 among three disk galaxies selected for central ionized-gas gradients, finding disturbed gas kinematics and irregular dust-lane morphology in the STIS data.

quotes.quote: We present long-slit HST/STIS measurements of the ionized-gas kinematics in the nucleus of three disk galaxies, namely NGC 2179, NGC 4343, NGC 4435. The sample galaxies have been selected on the basis of their ground-based spectroscopy, for displaying a strong central velocity gradient for the ionized gas, which is consistent with the presence of a circum nuclear keplerian disk (CNKD, ; ) rotating around a super massive black hole (SMBH). For each target galaxy we obtained the H and [ NII ] 6583 \ kinematics along the major axis and two 0 25 parallel offset positions.
For NGC 2179 and NGC 4343 the spectrum reveals a disturbed gas kinematics. These two objects show an irregular dust-lane morphology, too.

gmw_00000378.name: NGC 4435
gmw_00000378.overview: NGC 4435 is a lenticular galaxy [arXiv:astro-ph/0702057, arXiv:1811.03160] that is interacting with its companion, NGC 4438 [arXiv:astro-ph/0702057, arXiv:1001.0980]. The galaxy hosts a circumnuclear disk with a fading starburst [arXiv:astro-ph/0702057], regular ionized-gas kinematics [arXiv:astro-ph/0404575], and a potentially undermassive supermassive black hole [arXiv:1811.03160].

sources.summary: The paper presents NGC 4435 as an interacting S0 whose mid-infrared SED reveals a fading circumnuclear starburst, little AGN contribution, and nearly solar nuclear gas metallicity.

quotes.quote: NGC 4435 is an S0 galaxy interacting with NGC 4438 and it hosts a circumnuclear disk. Panuzzo et al. (2007) combined the Spitzer IRS spectra of NGC 4435 with IRAC and MIPS archival data and existing broad band measurements from X-ray to radio wavelengths to obtain an accurate panchromatic spectral energy distribution (SED) of this galaxy. The SED was analysed with GRASIL (Silva et al. 1998) and well reproduced at all wavelengths. The analysis shows that the circumnuclear disk experienced a burst of star formation activity which is now fading.

We fail to detect any high excitation nebular emission lines in the IRS spectrum; the [ NeIII ] 15.5/ [ NeII ] 12.8 ratio constrains the contribution of a possible AGN to the ionizing flux to be less than 2 . The upper limit on the temperature derived from H _2 S(1) and S(2) rotational lines is lower than expected for AGN excitation and PAH features are well reproduced by star formation models. Moreover, the X-ray emission is within the range expected from X-ray binaries in an advanced phase of the starburst. As for the metallicity of the nuclear disk, the comparison of observed MIR nebular lines with those predicted by the GRASIL model (Panuzzo et al 2003) indicates that it is almost solar.

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC 4435 to show that its low nuclear stellar dispersion, unlike expectations from scaling relations, supports an undermassive supermassive black hole.

quotes.quote: We illustrate how these \ measurements can be useful for constraining the mass \ of supermassive black holes (SBHs) by concentrating on the cases of the lenticular galaxies NGC 4435 and NGC 4459. These are characterized by similar ground-based half-light radii stellar velocity dispersion \ values but remarkably different \ as obtained from modelling their central ionised-gas kinematics, where NGC 4435 appears to host a significantly undermassive SBH compared to what is expected from the \ relation. Whereas this was found to agree with our nuclear \ measurement for NGC 4459, for NGC 4435 the observed \ is remarkably smaller than the predicted one, which further suggests that this galaxy could host an undermassive SBH.

Indeed, whereas the values for \ (the stellar velocity dispersion measured within a circular aperture corresponding to the galaxy effective radius r_ e ) obtained by Cappellari2013 using SAURON integral-field spectroscopy are fairly similar for these two galaxies (amounting to = 152.8 7.6 and 158.1 7.9 \ for NGC 4435 and NGC 4459, respectively), our STIS measurements return a much smaller _ ,fix value for NGC 4435 than for NGC 4459 (amounting to 87.3^ +10.2 _ 8.8 and 214.6^ +11.2 _ 12.0 , respectively, see Table  tab:fit ). In fact, the nuclear \ value for NGC 4435 is also at odds with what expected from the aperture velocity dispersion correction for early-type galaxies of FalconBarroso2017 , which for a r_ e = 23 5 would lead to a = 203.7 10.1 . Indeed, whereas NGC 4459 shows a \ value consistent with the \ relation Sarzi2001 , the \ of NGC 4435 falls significantly below it Coccato2006 in spite of their similar \ values.

sources.summary: The paper identifies NGC 4435 as the only target with regular ionized-gas kinematics and dust lanes, making it the suitable case for velocity-field modeling of the SMBH mass.

quotes.quote: Out of three objects only NGC 4435 turned out to have a disk of ionized gas in regular motion and a regular dust-lane morphology. Preliminary modeling indicates a SMBH mass ( M_ ) one order of magnitude lower than the one expected from the M_ _c relation for galaxies ( ; ). As demonstrated in Bertola et al. (1998), it is possible to detect from ground-based observations the presence of a CNKD around a SMBH. Its identification is possible from the study of the position velocity (PV) diagram as done by Funes et al. (2002). From this work and literature data we select three galaxies, namely NGC 2179, NGC 4343 and NGC 4435, which have a PV diagram consistent with the presence of a CNKD and a central stellar velocity dispersions ( _c ) which falls in a poorly sampled region of the M_ _c relation.

On the contrary, NGC 4435, the only one with a regular kinematics, shows also a regularity of the dust-lane morphology. This galaxy is a good candidate to model the velocity field for the determination of M_ . In the model we consider a constant velocity dispersion field, an exponential profile for the flux of the emission lines and we build the velocity field considering the contribution of the stellar potential (measured from the surface-brightness profile using a constant mass-to-light ratio and spherical symmetry) and the Keplerian potential of a SMBH.

sources.summary: The paper discusses NGC 4435 as a possible interaction partner that passed through NGC 4438's disk, with its compact molecular gas disk affecting collision scenarios.

quotes.quote: This lead to the following interaction scenario for the NGC 4438/NGC 4435 system: NGC 4435 passed through the disk of NGC 4438 100 Myr ago at a radial distance of 5-10 kpc. The encounter was rapid ( v 830 km s ^ 1 ) and retrograde (see also Combes at al. 1988). With an impact parameter < 10 kpc an ISM-ISM collision is unavoidable. Its importance depends on the initial gas distributions in NGC 4435 and NGC 4438. The current extent of NGC 4435's observed molecular gas disk is 1 kpc.

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC4435 as NGC4438's S0 companion and argues the observed plume near it is unlikely to be simple tidal debris from their interaction.

quotes.quote: Dynamical models suggest that NGC4438 has been perturbed by a high-velocity ( 800 ) interaction with the companion S0 NGC4435 (e.g., vollmer05 ).

Deep optical investigations mihosvirgo also reveal the presence of a faint stellar plume apparently extending from NGC4435. This feature has been interpreted in the past as a tidal debris, but it is not clear how it would fit into the NGC4438-M86 interaction scenario.

Its and CO recessional velocities ( 14 ) are roughly consistent with the velocity of NGC4438 ( V_ = 104 2 and W_ 50 = 244 5 , giovanelli07 ) but significantly different from the redshift of NGC4435 ( V 801 ). This apparently rules out a gravitational interaction between NGC4438 and NGC4435: given their large velocity difference, we should expect the stripping material to lie at a velocity intermediate between the two systems.

sources.summary: The paper reports a new nuclear source in companion galaxy NGC 4435, noting prior nuclear X-ray detections, no previous radio detection, and an estimated X-ray luminosity.

quotes.quote: NGC 4435, which is the companion galaxy to NGC 4438, also has a new nuclear source, namely ID LCX = 4 in Table t:cross_LCX in the NGC 4438 field. There appear to have been X-ray detections in the nucleus of this galaxy e.g. wan16 but no radio source has previously been detected. A NED https://ned.ipac.caltech.edu. search for NGC 4435 resulted in little new information about the X-ray source in the literature, including , except for the X-ray detection as 2CXO J122740.7+130447 with the net counts of 65.6 and a count rate of 2.5 counts/ks. We estimate the energy flux of the source as 5 10^ 14 ergs s^ 1 cm^ 2 or the luminosity as 4.0 10^ 38 ergs s^ 1 at the distance of the galaxy, assuming the same spectrum for the conversion as described in the caption to Table 8.

sources.summary: The paper contrasts NGC 4435 with passive early-type galaxies, arguing that its far-infrared dust mass requires high dust production or shielding in cool, likely star-forming material.

quotes.quote: For example, the dust mass of NGC 4435 of 1.2 10^ 6 M_ would require a total dust production rate of 0.03 M_ yr^ 1 , more than an order of magnitude greater than any value in Table table:1 . This object is known to host star formation (Panuzzo et al., 2007) and so dust is likely to be in cool clouds more typical of late-type galaxies; grain lifetimes in this case may be much longer. Dust may have been acquired from an interaction, but in any case it probably arrived as part of a cooler medium.

gmw_00000379.name: NGC 5430
gmw_00000379.overview: NGC 5430 is a barred spiral starburst galaxy located approximately 42 Mpc away [arXiv:1206.1656, arXiv:0910.0415]. It features a nuclear ring or spiral with active star formation, a dust lane oriented along its bar, and a bright Wolf-Rayet knot [arXiv:1206.1656, arXiv:0910.0415].

sources.summary: The paper studies NGC 5430 with SpIOMM and long-slit spectra, finding no strong AGN, flat abundance and age trends, and two galaxy-wide recent star-formation waves.

quotes.quote: In order to better understand the impact of the bar on the evolution of spiral galaxies, we measure the properties of giant H ii regions and the bar in the SB(s)b galaxy NGC 5430. We use two complementary data sets, both obtained at the Observatoire du Mont-M egantic: a hyperspectral data cube from the imaging Fourier transform spectrograph SpIOMM, and high-resolution spectra across the bar from a long-slit spectrograph. We flux-calibrate SpIOMM spectra for the first time, and produce H and [ N ii ] 6584 A intensity maps from which we identify 51 giant H ii regions in the spiral arms and bar. We evaluate the type of activity, the oxygen abundance and the age of the young populations contained in these giant H ii regions and in the bar.

NGC 5430 is a SB(s)b galaxy (RA 14 ^ h 00 ^ m 45 6, Dec. +59 19 ^ m 42 ^ s , J2000) located at a distance of 42 3 Mpc, using a redshift of cz =3061 9 km s ^ 1 1996ApJ...473..576F and H_0 = 73.0 km s ^ 1 Mpc ^ 1 . Two regions in the galaxy exhibit strong emission as shown in Fig. fig:n5430 . The brightest region is located 22 from the galaxy nucleus (within B2 in Fig. fig:n5430 ) and is identified as a Wolf-Rayet (WR) knot 1982PASP...94..765K,1987A&A...172...43K,2004MNRAS.355..728F . The second is the central region (within B4 in Fig. fig:n5430 ), which shows active star formation in two knots on a nuclear ring 1983ApJ...268..602B,1997A&A...324...41C,Cantin2004,Cantin2010 .

sources.summary: NGC 5430 is presented as a nearby barred starburst galaxy with a nuclear spiral or ring, dust lane, young emission regions, and mixed stellar populations.

quotes.quote: NGC 5430 is a nearby (42 Mpc) SB(s)b starburst galaxy. While continuum flux images show a double peak structure, a nuclear spiral/ring is clearly seen from the emission lines (Fig. fig1 ). The extinction map indicates a dust lane located between the emission knots, and oriented along the galaxy large scale bar. With the iteration technique we find a gas metallicity close to 3 Z _ . The emission regions L1 and L2 are only 6.2 0.8 Myr.

gmw_00000380.name: OJ 248
gmw_00000380.overview: OJ 248 is a flat-spectrum radio quasar and blazar located at a redshift of z=0.939 [arXiv:1505.00916, arXiv:1605.03599]. It features a highly relativistic jet with apparent superluminal motion [arXiv:0807.1796, arXiv:astro-ph/0511664] that exhibits a 90-degree bend in X-ray observations [arXiv:1605.03599, arXiv:2405.04935].

sources.summary: The paper studies OJ 248 as a z=0.939 blazar, reporting multiwavelength variability, delayed optical versus gamma-ray behavior, variable polarization, and a stable Mg II broad line.

quotes.quote: firstpage - lastpage 2015 We present an analysis of the multiwavelength behaviour of the blazar OJ 248 at z=0.939 in the period 2006--2013. We use low-energy data (optical, near-infrared, and radio) obtained by 21 observatories participating in the GLAST-AGILE Support Program (GASP) of the Whole Earth Blazar Telescope (WEBT), as well as data from the Swift (optical--UV and X-rays) and Fermi ( rays) satellites, to study flux and spectral variability and correlations among emissions in different bands. We take into account the effect of absorption by the damped Lyman intervening system at z=0.525 . Two major outbursts were observed in 2006--2007 and in 2012--2013 at optical and near-IR wavelengths, while in the high-frequency radio light curves prominent radio outbursts are visible peaking at the end of 2010 and beginning of 2013, revealing a complex radio-optical correlation. Cross-correlation analysis suggests a delay of the optical variations after the ray ones of about a month, which is a peculiar behaviour in blazars.

We also analyse optical polarimetric and spectroscopic data. The average polarization percentage P is less than 3 , but it reaches 19 during the early stage of the 2012--2013 outburst. A vague correlation of P with brightness is observed. There is no preferred electric vector polarisation angle and during the outburst the linear polarization vector shows wide rotations in both directions, suggesting a complex behaviour/structure of the jet and possible turbulence. The analysis of 140 optical spectra acquired at the Steward Observatory reveals a strong Mg II broad emission line with an essentially stable flux of 6.2 10^ 15 erg cm^ 2 s^ 1 and a full width at half-maximum of 2053 km s^ 1 . galaxies: active -- galaxies: quasars: general -- galaxies: quasars: individual: OJ 248 -- galaxies: jets.

sources.summary: The paper describes OJ 248 as a bright EGRET radio quasar with rapid apparent jet motion and a weak, discontinuous 1.4-year timescale in Metsahovi monitoring.

quotes.quote: OJ 248 ( 0827+243 ) : This radio quasar was found by EGRET to be a bright ray source. It has been monitored at Mets ahovi for about 11 years both at 22 and 37 GHz. It has also been a target of multiwavelength campaigns e.g. bach07 and has shown extremely fast apparent pattern velocity exceeding 25c in the jet piner06 . The kiloparsec-scale jet is studied in more detail in . We found a timescale of 1.4 years at both frequency bands but it appears quite weak in strength and is classified as a weak timescale at 22 GHz.

sources.summary: The paper identifies [ HB89 ] 0827 + 243 as a gamma-ray bright FSRQ with optical and infrared variability, a superluminal X-ray/radio jet, and significant internight variability.

quotes.quote: [ HB89 ] 0827 + 243: is classified as a FSRQ Healey07 . It is also a gamma-ray bright quasar, with a redshift z=0.939 Hewett10 . Variability on a day-long scale has been detected at B , V , and R optical bands Villata97,Raiteri98 , as well as long-term variability in the near-infrared J , H , and K' bands Enya02 . The jet emerging from the core of 0827 + 243 has been detected both at X-ray and radio bands, showing an apparent superluminal motion of over 20 Jorstad01, Piner06 . This jet has a highly unusual morphology bending almost 90^ at the X-ray band, while in radio only the external 90^ bended section is visible.

sources.summary: The paper identifies a well-tracked eastern jet feature in 0827+243, measures apparent motion near 25, and notes its large-scale jet remains highly relativistic to kiloparsec scales.

quotes.quote: 0827+243.---( z=0.939 ) Of the fastest components in our three sources, only C2 in 0827+243 has an `Excellent' quality code. This is because the bright eastern jet feature (made up of Gaussian components C2, C3, and C4) is a distinct feature that is not merged with the core or any other features, so that there is no ambiguity in tracking the feature from epoch to epoch. Figure 6 shows a mosaic of four of our six images of 0827+243, where the motion of this feature at 25 is clearly visible. The proper motion that we measure for component C2 agrees well with the proper motion of J01's component D2 (but note that these are not the same component), the difference in the apparent speeds for these components between this paper and J01 is due solely to the different redshifts used in the two papers (see footnote (1) of Jorstad Marscher, 2004). Analysis of the large-scale jet (Jorstad Marscher 2004) has shown it to be highly relativistic out to kiloparsec scales.

sources.summary: The paper fits Q 0827+243 XIS spectra with an absorbed power law, finding Galactic-consistent absorption, a 2-10 keV flux measurement, and no additional soft-band spectral feature.

quotes.quote: Q 0827+243 The time averaged spectra of Q 0827+243 collected with the XISs are well fitted by an absorbed power-law model with a photon index = 1.46 0.02 ( ^2_ = 0.84 for 194 dof). The absorption column is consistent with the Galactic value of N_ H = 3.62 10^ 20 cm ^ 2 (Dickey Lockman 1990), and the flux over 2-10 keV is F_ 2-10 keV = (1.37 0.04) 10^ 12 erg cm ^ 2 s ^ 1 . As shown in Figure fig:QHBspec , there is no evidence for any additional spectral feature in the soft band. This result is in good agreement with previous Chandra observations of the core (Jorstad Marscher 2004), revealing that the X-ray continuum is well described by a power-law model ( 1.4 ) with Galactic absorption.

sources.summary: The paper compares 0827+243 (OJ 248) to PKS 1127 145, noting its X-ray emission bends and fades, its radio jet begins beyond the bend, and a standing shock may decelerate the flow.

quotes.quote: A decrease of X-ray emission as moving away from the core accompanied by an increase of the radio emission, though not common, has been observed in other kpc-scale X-ray jets, like 3C 273 marshall01 , 1136 135 and PKS 1510 089 sambruna04 , and 0827+243 jorstad04 . In 0827+243 (OJ 248) the X-ray emission shows an apparent 90^ bend at about 5 arcsec from the core, and then fades away. On the other hand, at 5 and 15 GHz the large-scale jet is detected only from the bend up to the jet termination. The bend may correspond to a standing shock wave caused by, e.g., a jet-cloud interaction that originates a deflection and a deceleration of the jet flow jorstad04 .

gmw_00000381.name: DF2
gmw_00000381.overview: NGC 1052-DF2 is an ultra-diffuse, low-surface-brightness dwarf galaxy [arXiv:1804.04136, arXiv:2403.11403] located within the NGC 1052 group [arXiv:2309.08592, arXiv:2111.12104]. It is characterized by an extremely low velocity dispersion [arXiv:1806.04685, arXiv:1901.03711] and a population of unusually luminous globular clusters [arXiv:2309.08592, arXiv:1809.07831], suggesting a deficit of dark matter [arXiv:1806.04685, arXiv:2111.12104].

sources.summary: The paper argues that current globular-cluster velocities do not prove NGC1052-DF2 lacks dark matter; its dispersion and mass-to-light ratio are weakly constrained and compatible with dwarf galaxies.

quotes.quote: It was recently proposed that the globular cluster system of the very low surface-brightness galaxy NGC1052-DF2 is dynamically very cold, leading to the conclusion that this dwarf galaxy has little or no dark matter. Here, we show that a robust statistical measure of the velocity dispersion of the tracer globular clusters implies a mundane velocity dispersion and a poorly constrained mass-to-light ratio. Models that include the possibility that some of the tracers are field contaminants do not yield a more constraining inference.

It is evident from Figure ML that the current velocity data set on NGC1052-DF2 is not very constraining beyond pointing out that the dwarf galaxy is not massively dominated by dark matter. At the moment, it is not possible to rule out any mass-to-light ratio below M/L<6.7 within the half-light radius or M/L<8.1 within the radius covered by the tracers (at the 90-percent confidence level in both cases). Could NGC1052-DF2 host no dark matter and its inferred mass (or mass-to-light ratio) be entirely consistent with an old stellar population ( M/L_V 2 )? Certainly, but so could a much more mundane, dark-matter dominated mass-to-light ratio. The mass-to-light ratio of NGC1052-DF2 is compatible with that of other nearby dwarf galaxies.

sources.summary: The paper revises GC-98 kinematics for NGC 1052-DF2, finding a low intrinsic dispersion, halo-to-stellar mass near unity, and continued evidence that the galaxy is dark-matter deficient.

quotes.quote: We recently published velocity measurements of luminous globular clusters in the galaxy NGC 1052-DF2, concluding that it lies far off the canonical stellar mass - halo mass (SMHM) relation vd18a [ vD18 ] . Here we present a revised velocity for one of the globular clusters, GC-98. GC-98 has been the subject of some debate.
Although all these estimates imply a strongly dark matter-deficient galaxy (see Discussion), the quantitative constraints on the halo mass are sensitive to the treatment of this single object.

We derive a revised dispersion of _ intr =5.6^ +5.2 _ 3.8 km/s ( _ intr <12.4 km/s at 90 confidence). The likelihood gives _ intr =7.8^ +5.2 _ 2.2 km/s ( _ intr <14.6 km/s). The revised velocity dispersion is nearly identical to that expected from the stars alone, and does not significantly alter the analysis of vD18. The implied ratio M_ halo /M_ stars is of order unity and consistent with zero.

sources.summary: DF2 is discussed as an ultra-diffuse galaxy with debated distance, unusually luminous compact objects, and a low dynamical halo-mass upper limit questioned by later work.

quotes.quote: The second UDG is DF2 2018Natur.555..629V has recently been the subject of debate over its distance. Here we adopt the larger distance of 19 Mpc 2018arXiv180706025V rather than the smaller distance of 13 Mpc advocated by 2018arXiv180610141T . Its GC system is a little odd in that it includes 11 objects with M_V< -8.6 , i.e. more luminous than most GCs and approaching the luminosity regime of ultra compact dwarfs (UCDs).
