sources.summary: The paper measures NGC1052-DF4 with TRGB methods at 14.2 Mpc, places it in a nearer 13.5 Mpc group, and argues the shorter distance reduces its apparent dark-matter deficit.

quotes.quote: In this Letter, we analyze the CMD of the other dwarf ``lacking dark matter" galaxy: NGC1052-DF4. We will show that the location of the TRGB of this galaxy puts this object at a distance of 14.2 0.7 Mpc. Hubble Space Telescope (HST) imaging of the NGC1052-DF4 galaxy explored in this work was taken as part of the programme GO-14644 (PI: van Dokkum) and consists of two Advanced Camera for Survey (ACS) orbits for each object: one in F606W ( V _ 606 ; 2180s) and one in F814W ( I _ 814 ; 2320s).

We argue that the closest structure is formed by NGC1042 and NGC1035 and contains the dwarf galaxies: [ KKS2000 04 ] and NGC1052-DF4. This group of galaxies is located at 13.5 Mpc. The combination of these two factors would reduce the total stellar mass of NGC1052-DF4 by a factor of 3, alleviating significantly the absence of dark matter previously reported.

sources.summary: The paper treats DF4 as a dark-matter-deficient UDG paired with NGC1052-DF2, using its stellar mass and dynamical limits to test feedback-driven expansion models.

quotes.quote: vanDokkum18b and vanDokkum19 found two UDGs in a nearby galaxy group, NGC1052-DF2 and DF4, hosting populations of 10 unusually massive GCs ( M_ GC 9 10^5 ). Both galaxies have stellar masses 10^8 and seem to contain very little DM, with stars providing nearly the entire dynamical mass in the central 8 vanDokkum18a,vanDokkum19 . For an NFW DM halo density profile Navarro97 , this would imply a baryon fraction / 50 per cent, in contradiction with the 3 per cent expected in galaxies of the same mass in Papastergis12 .

The case of NGC1052-DF2 and DF4 The predictions for a galaxy with the stellar mass of DF2/DF4, 2 10^8 , are presented in Figure  fig:DF2 . The top row shows how the formation of the core impacts the DM density profiles of galaxies when measured at a galactocentric radius =8 , which approximately corresponds to the outermost dynamical constraints for DF2 and DF4.

The stellar and dynamical masses for NGC1052-DF4 were obtained from vanDokkum19 , and the limit on the halo mass was calculated by fitting a coreNFW profile Read16a of average concentration to the 2 upper limit on the DM mass within 7 .

sources.summary: The paper treats DF4 as a dark-matter-deficient companion candidate of NGC 1052, similar to DF2 in structure and possibly produced by close encounters.

quotes.quote: For this reason, the detection of DF2 and DF4, two low-mass galaxies devoid of dark matter, was not anticipated . The two observed galaxies lacking dark matter, DF2 and DF4 , are at close projected separations (80 kpc and 165 kpc, which set a lower limit to the 3D separations) from NGC 1052 (stellar mass M_ =1.05 10^ 11 M_ ). With this procedure, we also obtain S ersic indices ( n_ S e rsic ), which range from 0.60-0.77 , in excellent agreement with observations ( n_ S e rsic =0.60 and 0.79 for DF2 and DF4, respectively).

Our results support a scenario where DF2 and DF4 became dark-matter deficient due to past close encounters with NGC 1052, the only massive galaxy in their neighbourhood (note that our simulation allows hosts to have multiple dark-matter deficient satellites). Their projected (80 kpc and 165 kpc) and line-of-sight separations ( 2.1 0.5 Mpc) - as well as our estimates of the virial diameter of the NGC 1052 halo -- suggest that DF2 and DF4 could be satellites of the NGC 1052 group. In addition to being dark-matter deficient, DF2 and DF4 are intriguing because they (1) inhabit the same group and (2) are extremely similar to each other.

sources.summary: The paper focuses on NGC 1052-DF4 as a dark-matter-deficient UDG, testing globular-cluster rotation and finding non-rotation supports its low dark matter content.

quotes.quote: 2019ApJ...874L...5V discovered a second galaxy in the NGC 1052 group, naming it NGC 1052-DF4. This galaxy is also a UDG and is very similar to the NGC 1052-DF2 galaxy in size, surface brightness, and shape. 2019ApJ...874L...5V found that NGC 1052-DF4 also possessed a relatively low velocity dispersion, and hence concluded it is also dark matter deficient.

Inspired by the work of 2020MNRAS.491L...1L on NGC 1052-DF2, a similar statistical analysis of NGC 1052-DF4's globular clusters was undertaken. The primary objective of this study is to determine whether the globular cluster population rotates. The paper is structured as follows. Section Data describes the data for the NGC 1052-DF4 globular cluster population.

sources.summary: The paper presents NGC1052-DF4 as the brightest of several low surface brightness galaxies near NGC1052, with morphology, size, surface brightness, SBF magnitudes, and CMDs nearly identical to the target galaxy.

quotes.quote: Finally, we emphasize that \ is not alone: it is one of several low surface brightness galaxies in the NGC1052 field that are all consistent with the same distance (see C18). They are all located near NGC1052 in the Dragonfly frame (see Fig. \ 1 in C18) and none of them have a detected red giant branch in our I_ 814 images. In Fig. \ df4_comp.fig we show the brightest of these other galaxies, NGC1052-DF4. This galaxy has a similar morphology, size, and surface brightness as . The SBF magnitudes (Fig. \ distance.fig ) and CMDs (Figs. \ 1 and df4_comp.fig ) of the two galaxies are nearly identical.

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 1052-DF4 as a dark-matter-deficient satellite of NGC 1052, with low velocity dispersion and dark-to-stellar mass ratio motivating tidal-interaction simulations.

quotes.quote: Such a prediction has been challenged by the discovery of two galaxies (NGC 1052-DF2 and NGC 1052-DF4: DF2 and DF4 hereafter) that seem to lack dark matter VanDokkum2018, VanDokkum2019 in their central regions. These galaxies have a stellar mass of 10^8 and are satellites of NGC 1052 but see for a different interpretation for DF4 Montes2020 , a massive ( M_* 10^ 11 ) elliptical galaxy Muller2019 . Given their stellar mass, these objects are predicted to form in a dark matter halo of a mass 5 10^ 10 Moster2013 ; this mass translates into an expected (stellar) velocity dispersion of about 25 . Quite surprisingly, using the line-of-sight velocity of 9 globular clusters up to a distance of 10 kpc, van Dokkum and collaborators have estimated a maximum velocity dispersion below 10 . When translated back into a mass, such a low velocity dispersion suggests a dark matter to stellar mass ratio of two to three, quite far from values of 30-40 at that radius expected from galaxy formation theory for a halo velocity dispersion of 25 e.g. Behroozi2013 .

sources.summary: The paper analyzes NGC1052-DF4 as a second dark-matter-deficit ultra-diffuse galaxy, using its globular-cluster kinematics and density profile in modified-gravity fits.

quotes.quote: Another UDG observed within the same group NGC1052 i.e, NGC1052-DF4 (here on mentioned as DF4) shows similar nature. The spectroscopy and imaging confirmed the presence of 7 globular cluster like objects within. Similarly for DF4 the luminosity is estimated by the Sersic profile with Sersic index n=0.79 , axis ratio b/a=0.89 and major-axis half light radius 1.6 kpc. Similar analysis for DF4 showed intrinsic VD measurement of about 4.2^ 4.4 _ 2.2 km/s. The ratio of M _ DM /M _ stellar computed from the VD observation is of the O (1) , suggesting the existence of a second galaxy lacking DM .

sources.summary: The paper contrasts NGC 1052-DF4 with Dragonfly 44, citing its low velocity dispersion and identifying it as another diffuse galaxy in the low-dark-matter kinematic class.

quotes.quote: At the other are the galaxies NGC 1052-DF2 and NGC 1052-DF4, with dispersions of =8.5^ +2.2 _ 3.1 \ ( Danieli et al. 2019) and =4.2^ +4.4 _ 2.2 \ ( van Dokkum et al. 2019) respectively. These velocity dispersions are consistent with those expected from the stellar mass alone (van Dokkum et al. \ 2018a; Wasserman et al. \ 2018a). This large apparent difference in dark matter content is surprising as \ and the NGC 1052 galaxies have very similar stellar masses and morphologies, and are all rich in globular clusters.

With a robust velocity dispersion measurement for NGC 1052-DF2 from stellar kinematics ( Danieli et al. 2019), the identification of a second galaxy in the same class (NGC 1052-DF4; van Dokkum et al. 2019), and the results presented in this study, there can be little doubt that large, diffuse, spheroidal galaxies with stellar masses of a few 10^8 \ have a remarkable range in their kinematics and, hence, dark matter properties on kpc scales (see Fig. \ wolf.fig ).

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 1052-DF4 as a second dark-matter-deficient galaxy in the NGC 1052 group and part of the low-surface-brightness trail motivating the collision scenario.

quotes.quote: Subsequently, a second dark matter deficient galaxy (DMDG), NGC 1052-DF4 (hereafter DF4), was discovered in the same galaxy group vanDokkum2019 .

Recently, suggested that the trail of galaxies with low surface brightness, including two DMDGs (DF2 and DF4), in the vicinity of NGC 1052 might have been formed through a high-speed collision between two gas-rich dwarf galaxies at z 1 (8 Gyr ago). A burst of star formation activity is induced due to the strong compression of the galactic gas. As the DM and pre-existing stars in the progenitor galaxies pass through the gas, the stars formed in the compressed gas can create DMDGs Silk2019, Trujillo-Gomez2021 .

sources.summary: The paper compares Dragonfly-4 with model ghost galaxies, noting its claimed dark-matter deficiency, measured surface-brightness profile, larger stellar mass, and possible tidal distortion.

quotes.quote: Dragonfly-4 (DF-4) is a well-studied UDG that has been claimed to be deficient in dark matter PvD2019_2ndMissingDM . Montes:2020aa_df4 report the surface brightness profile of this object to 20 mag arcsec^ 2 . They measure a stellar mass of 3.6 10^ 7 M_ , greater than our ghosts; since we are interested here only in the shape of the surface brightness profile, we simply scale down the amplitude of the profile reported by Montes:2020aa_df4 by a factor of 10 in Fig. fig:density . Montes:2020aa_df4 also find evidence that the galaxy is tidally distorted by interaction with a more massive neighbour.

gmw_00000383.name: NGC 1035
gmw_00000383.overview: NGC 1035 is an edge-on, highly inclined spiral dwarf galaxy [arXiv:2203.00694, arXiv:1908.10858] located at a Tully-Fisher distance of approximately 14 Mpc [arXiv:1907.03761, arXiv:1903.07285]. It is identified as a likely perturber causing tidal stripping and S-shaped tidal tails in its neighboring galaxy, NGC 1052-DF4 [arXiv:2010.09719].

sources.summary: NGC1035 is treated as the neighboring disk galaxy whose proximity, tidal-radius calculation, and alignment with NGC1052-DF4's S-shaped tails make it the likely perturber.

quotes.quote: Deep optical imaging of the system has detected tidal tails in this object caused by its interaction with its neighbouring galaxy NGC1035. In fact, is very close both in velocity ( v 180 km s ^ 1 ) and in spatial projection ( R 4 ) to the galaxy NGC1035. If is being tidally stripped by NGC1035, their stripped GCs would be placed along the orbit traced by the satellite.

This larger value for the tidal radius would be in tension with the r_ distort measured in this paper. This suggests that NGC1035 is the likely responsible of the disruption of . The tails of the S are oriented directly to NGC1035 (see Fig. fig:iac80fov ) indicating that this disk galaxy Truong2017 is causing the tidal perturbation.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC1035 as a spiral near NGC1052-DF4, cites a Tully-Fisher distance of 14 Mpc, and groups it with NGC1042 and the DF4 dwarfs at 13.5 Mpc.

quotes.quote: The distance to NGC1035 Another bright galaxy in the FOV of NGC1052 is the spiral NGC1035. This galaxy is particularly interesting due to its vicinity to NGC1052-DF4. The most recent Tully-Fisher distance determination for this galaxy has been obtained by 2014MNRAS.444..527S . These authors quote a selection-bias-corrected distance estimate of 14 2.9 Mpc for NGC1035. This makes the galaxy distance compatible with NGC1042.

sources.summary: NGC 1035 is the most highly inclined spiral dwarf in the subsample; its CO rotation curve sits above the comparison curve but shares its shape, constraining dark-matter slope measurements.

quotes.quote: NGC 1035 --- This spiral galaxy is the most highly inclined of the subsample. Its CO \ lies systematically above the \ \ derived by relatores19a , but the shapes of the rotation curves agree well. The shape of the rotation curve is most important for determining the slope of the dark matter density profile . The amplitude depends on the inclination. For this galaxy, we use a somewhat higher inclination than relatores19a (79 compared to their 72 ), but this does not completely explain the offsets between the CO and \ rotation curves.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 1035 as an edge-on spiral close to DF4 whose external-field effect better reproduces DF4's observed velocity dispersion, while its distance remains part of the debate.

quotes.quote: However, the edge-on spiral galaxy NGC 1035 lies only 20 kpc away in projection. In this case, the EFE of NGC 1035 is much closer to reproducing the observed velocity dispersion. Although the distance to NGC 1052 (and its associated GCs and group galaxies) is secure at 20 Mpc we acknowledge the considerable debate in the literature on the true distances to DF2 and DF4. For example, Cohen et al. (2018) argued for a distance of 20 Mpc, while Monelli Trujillo (2019) argued that DF2 and DF4 (along with NGC 1035 and NGC 1042) lie at 13 Mpc. More work is needed to understand and verify these different distance determinations.

sources.summary: NGC 1035 is discussed as a spiral in the observed field whose velocity matches the group but whose Tully-Fisher distance places it foreground, with no tidal features.

quotes.quote: The remaining giant galaxy to be discussed in the observed field is the spiral galaxy NGC 1035. While it also has a systemic velocity consistent with the NGC 1052 group ( v=1249 km s ^ 1 ; 2004MNRAS.350.1195M ), its latest distance estimate -- based on Tully-Fisher measurements -- is 14 2.9 Mpc 2014MNRAS.444..527S . This places it in the foreground and is consistent with an absence of tidal features associated with this galaxy.

gmw_00000384.name: Alcyoneus
gmw_00000384.overview: Alcyoneus is a giant radio galaxy generated by an elliptical host galaxy [arXiv:2405.00232]. It is the longest known elliptical-generated radio galaxy, featuring a projected length of approximately 5 Mpc [arXiv:2210.10234, arXiv:2405.00232] and aging radio lobes [arXiv:2210.10234] with a proper volume of 2.5 Mpc³ [arXiv:2405.00232].

sources.summary: The paper uses Alcyoneus as the projected-longest known GRG example, with 5 Mpc projected length and lobes whose surface brightness matches LoTSS DR2 noise expectations.

quotes.quote: For Alcyoneus, at z_ Alc. 0.25 , f_ L_ = 0.3 and f_l = 0.3 Oei12022Alcyoneus . Assuming these parameter values, again in the top panel of Fig. fig:surfaceBrightness , we show luminosity density--projected length pairs of RGs at z = z_ Alc. whose peak (solid green curve) or mean (dash-dotted green curve) surface brightness equals the LoTSS DR2 noise level. Thus, each curve represents a family of borderline-detectable giants at Alcyoneus's redshift. The other dash-dotted curves indicate similar barely detectable families, but for other redshifts. Without optimising any free parameters, the curves correctly predict that Alcyoneus's lobes have surface brightnesses comparable to the 6'' LoTSS DR2 noise level and explain the absence of observations in the top-left corner of the figure.

The ideal length definition: physical relevance Alcyoneus , shown in Fig. fig:LoTSSDR2GRGs1 's top-right panel, is a 5 Mpc giant whose ageing lobes are revealed for the first time by the LoTSS DR2 blue Oei12022Alcyoneus . Future image sensitivity improvements shall reveal more hitherto unseen, fading lobes around known RGs that formed in the aftermath of earlier AGN activity episodes.

sources.summary: The paper supplements its GRG relation with Alcyoneus, calling it the longest elliptical-generated radio galaxy and citing its 4.99 Mpc projected length, 2.5 Mpc^3 lobe volume, and low lobe fields.

quotes.quote: To more reliably probe the projected proper length--two-lobe proper volume relation for giants, we supplemented this sample with the giant of NGC 6185, the longest spiral galaxy--generated RG known Oei2023NGC6185 , and with Alcyoneus, the longest elliptical galaxy--generated RG known Oei2022Alcyoneus . Oei2023NGC6185 estimated that the giant of NGC 6185 measures l_ p = 2.45 0.01 \ Mpc and has a two-lobe proper volume V = 0.35 0.03 \ Mpc ^3 . Similarly, Oei2022Alcyoneus estimated that Alcyoneus measures l_ p = 4.99 0.04 \ Mpc and has a two-lobe proper volume V = 2.5 0.3 \ Mpc ^3 .

At the moment, the lowest magnetic field strengths measured in giant radio galaxy lobes, as inferred from images of Alcyoneus and the giant generated by NGC 6185 assuming the equipartition or minimum energy condition, are 400 - 500 \ nG Oei2022Alcyoneus, Oei2023NGC6185 .

gmw_00000385.name: NGC 2300
gmw_00000385.overview: NGC 2300 is a massive early-type galaxy [arXiv:1812.05048] classified as either an S0 [arXiv:1108.3662] or an elliptical [arXiv:1108.3662, arXiv:2210.10234] that hosts a point-like radio core [arXiv:2111.02430]. It is the central dominant member of its galaxy group [arXiv:1812.05048, arXiv:2111.02430] and exhibits tidal interactions with the neighboring spiral galaxy NGC 2276 [arXiv:1812.05048, arXiv:2111.02430].

sources.summary: The paper classifies NGC 2300's outer profile as I and describes profile breaks, interaction signatures shaping its measured surface-brightness structure.

quotes.quote: NGC 2300 (I; SA0 ^0 ) : Some observers have classified this as an elliptical ( huchtmeier94 classified it as E3), but its profile belies this. Asymmetries are seen in the outer disk, notably in the NE where a lobe is seen at 170 (some 24 kpc) from the center, possibly due to an interaction. NGC 2276 is optically close (at 7.6 ), though the two redshifts ( 1905 km s ^ 1 and 2410 km s ^ 1 ) are sufficiently different to cast doubt on an interaction sandage94 . In the same direction from the center as the extended lobe, but at a slightly smaller radius (90 ), fabbiano92 found a source of X-ray emission with no optical counterpart. The profile is of Type I and it reaches 530 (75 kpc) from the center before reaching our 4.94 _ sky uncertainty limit.

sources.summary: The paper compares NGC 2300 observations with AGB-dust and diffuse-dust models, concluding diffuse dust alone cannot reproduce its mid-infrared features.

quotes.quote: In Figure fig:fir_martini we compare the observations of NGC 2300 with both the AGB dust model and two models that includes only diffuse dust. The fundamental difference between the latter two models is the adopted value of q_ PAH . In one case we adopt the fiducial value while in the other we adopt an unreasonably high value of 10 This is an extrapolation of the draine2007 models, the highest value draine2007 compute is 4.58 . . We added as much diffuse dust into the models as possible without exceeding the constraints of the FIR flux at 70 m and 160 m . It is clear that the model with only diffuse dust cannot reproduce the 8-24 m features seen in early-type galaxies with influential old stellar populations.

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 2300 as the massive early-type companion whose separation, stellar mass, I-band extension, and magnetic-field evidence support tidal forcing on NGC 2276.

quotes.quote: Here we study the spiral galaxy NGC 2276, which is currently falling into NGC 2300 group and interacting with the early-type galaxy NGC 2300. The NGC 2300 group has four members including NGC 2300 being the most massive one.

Tidal forces could be sufficient to trigger SF despite the large projected separation ( 75 kpc) to neighbor NGC 2300, as Scudder12 show in their simulations that SFR may be enhanced by 0.3-0.6 dex at large separations (up to 70 kpc) between merging galaxies. The presence of tidal forces in NGC 2276 has also been invoked to explain the extended south-east arm in radio emission of NGC 2276 ( Condon83 ), and truncation of the R-band continuum ( Gruendl93,Davis97 ). Additional evidence for tidal forces includes a north-east extension in the I-band continuum of NGC 2300 ( Forbes92,Davis97 ), and the enhanced magnetic fields ( Hummel95 ).

Q = _ 10 M_ comp M_ 2276 D_ 25 ^ 3 \ , where log(M_ comp /M_ )=10^ 11.3 and log(M_ 2276 /M_ )=10^ 10.7 are the stellar masses of NGC 2300 and NGC 2276, respectively; D_ 25 is the B-band optical diameter of NGC 2276, and =75 kpc is the projected separation between NGC 2300 and NGC 2276.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 2300 as a disturbed group-central galaxy whose pressure and entropy maps show gas compression, high dispersion, and likely merger or accretion signatures.

quotes.quote: NGC 2300, z=0.0046 The pressure and entropy structure consists of two blobs, centered on NGC 2300 and NGC 2276. The pressure map contain a large-scale bow-shape enhancement outside the low entropy gas centered on NGC 2300. Such gas compressions are possible in several cases: supersonic motion of NGC 2300 with respect to the group; supersonic interaction between NGC 2300 and NGC 2276; presence of substructure around NGC 2300. In any case, the dispersion in the pressure map of NGC 2300, reported in Tab. t:scatter , is high, in comparison to other groups. Since this does not seem to be an effect of confusion with a background object, as is sometimes the case (Mahdavi et al. 2005), we suggest, based on a similar work applied to simulations (Finoguenov et al. in prep.), that the NGC 2300 group exhibits the signs of a merger. The pressure and entropy deviations 2^ southwest of the center may be explained if there is ongoing gas accretion from the south.

sources.summary: The paper identifies the GRG associated with NGC 2300 as the largest-angular-length giant known in the Northern Sky and discusses an unlikely alternative host, NGC 2276.

quotes.quote: The GRG associated with NGC 2300 (see the middle-left panel of Fig. fig:LoTSSDR2GRGsLargerThanLuna ) is the largest-angular--length giant ever found, and the largest-angular--length radio galaxy in the Northern Sky.

It remains possible that the GRG has been generated by spiral galaxy NGC 2276 instead, with which elliptical galaxy NGC 2300 is interacting. However, this scenario seems unlikely, as only a fraction 10^ 3 of known GRGs are hosted by spirals. Its discovery emphasises that low-frequency interferometers like the LOFAR and the MWA, which are sensitive to degree-scale angular scales, are important to complete a low-redshift census of giant radio galaxies. Sky-wide, a simple extrapolation of our findings suggests that several ( 10^1 ) degree-scale angular length giants similar to the GRG of NGC 2300 still await discovery at the LoTSS DR2 depth. table Table tab:GRGPropertiesAll ranks, host names, angular lengths, and LoTSS DR2 image references for all 13 newly discovered giants that appear larger in the sky than the Moon ( > 30' ).

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 2300 as the central elliptical of NGC 2276's group, linking it to hot intragroup X-ray gas and possible tidal interaction with NGC 2276.

quotes.quote: The galaxy is known to be very active in many wavelengths, possibly due to gravitational interaction with the central elliptical of the group, NGC 2300.

NGC 2276 is a peculiar Sc galaxy belonging to the group of NGC 2300, the first small group of galaxies in which hot diffuse intragroup gas has been observed in X-rays with ROSAT (Mulchaey et al. 1993, Davis et al. 1996). The mass estimate of the hot gas is M _ gas = 1.25 0.13 10^ 12 M_ . The X-ray emission, of about 0.3 Mpc extent, is centered close but not exactly on the central elliptical NGC 2300.

sources.summary: The paper reports point-like radio morphology for NGC 2300 in VLA A and D data and notes tidal interaction with NGC 2276.

quotes.quote: NGC 2300* The radio emission from our new VLA A configuration data for NGC 2300, the central dominant galaxy of the NGC 2300 group, shows a point-like morphology (Figure fig:chandra_vla_ps_a g). The archival VLA D configuration data reveals radio point source emission, as well (Figure fig:chandra_vla_ps_a g) . \ NGC2300 is known to undergo tidal interactions with a late-type active star-forming galaxy NGC 2276 wolter2015 .

gmw_00000386.name: NGC 6185
gmw_00000386.overview: NGC 6185 is a massive spiral galaxy of Hubble-de Vaucouleurs class SAa located in the nearby Cosmic Web [arXiv:2210.10156]. Remarkably, this spiral galaxy has generated a giant radio galaxy [arXiv:2210.10156, arXiv:2210.10234], which is the largest known radio galaxy hosted by a spiral galaxy [arXiv:2210.10234, arXiv:2405.00232].

sources.summary: The paper analyzes NGC 6185 as a massive nearby spiral that created a giant radio galaxy, using its lobes and environment to infer low lobe pressure and group-boundary IGM temperature.

quotes.quote: In particular, we use a radio image of the giant radio galaxy (giant RG, or GRG) created by NGC 6185, a massive nearby spiral. We analyse this extraordinary object with a Bayesian 3D lobe model and deduce an equipartition pressure P_ eq = 6 10^ 16 \ Pa -- among the lowest found in RGs yet. Using an X-ray-based statistical conversion for Fanaroff--Riley II RGs, we find a true lobe pressure P = 1.5 +1.7 0.4 10^ 15 \ Pa .

NGC 6185 is a spiral galaxy at a spectroscopic redshift z = 0.0343 0.0002 Falco11999 . At a comoving distance of 146 \ Mpc and a luminosity distance of 151 \ Mpc , the galaxy lies in the nearby Cosmic Web. It is of Hubble--de Vaucouleurs class SAa Jansen12000 . Its stellar mass, M_ = 3.0 +1.2 0.9 10^ 11 \ M_ Kannappan12013 , is high for a spiral galaxy, though common for galaxies hosting GRGs Oei12022Alcyoneus .

sources.summary: The paper presents NGC 6185 as a rare spiral host of a giant radio galaxy, currently the largest known spiral-hosted radio galaxy and worthy of dedicated analysis.

quotes.quote: Spiral or lenticular host galaxies Remarkably, although NGC 6185 is a spiral galaxy of Hubble--de Vaucouleurs class SAa blue Jansen12000 , it appears to have generated the GRG shown in the top-left panel of Fig. fig:LoTSSDR2GRGsLargerThanLuna . Such systems are exceedingly rare: not only are few RGs G RGs, but also virtually all GRGs have an elliptical galaxy as their host. With l_ p = 2.54 0.01 \ Mpc , this is the largest known spiral galaxy--hosted radio galaxy. Hitherto, the largest spiral galaxy--hosted GRG in the literature has been J2345-0449 blue Bagchi12014 , with l_ p = 1.6 \ Mpc . Given the favourably low redshift of z = 0.03430 7 10^ 5 , NGC 6185 and its enigmatic GRG solicit a dedicated analysis Oei12022NGC6185 . Besides NGC 6185, spiral or lenticular galaxies J080403.40+404809.3 and J091459.66+294348.8 (known alternatively as NGC 2789; see the bottom-right panel of Fig. fig:LoTSSDR2GRGsLargerThanLuna ) have also generated GRGs; these have projected proper lengths l_ p = 1.1 \ Mpc and l_ p = 0.9 \ Mpc , respectively.

sources.summary: The paper uses the giant of NGC 6185 as supplemental GRG data, citing it as the longest spiral-generated RG with projected length 2.45 Mpc, lobe volume 0.35 Mpc^3, and 400-500 nG lobe fields.

quotes.quote: To more reliably probe the projected proper length--two-lobe proper volume relation for giants, we supplemented this sample with the giant of NGC 6185, the longest spiral galaxy--generated RG known Oei2023NGC6185 , and with Alcyoneus, the longest elliptical galaxy--generated RG known Oei2022Alcyoneus . Oei2023NGC6185 estimated that the giant of NGC 6185 measures l_ p = 2.45 0.01 \ Mpc and has a two-lobe proper volume V = 0.35 0.03 \ Mpc ^3 . Similarly, Oei2022Alcyoneus estimated that Alcyoneus measures l_ p = 4.99 0.04 \ Mpc and has a two-lobe proper volume V = 2.5 0.3 \ Mpc ^3 .

At the moment, the lowest magnetic field strengths measured in giant radio galaxy lobes, as inferred from images of Alcyoneus and the giant generated by NGC 6185 assuming the equipartition or minimum energy condition, are 400 - 500 \ nG Oei2022Alcyoneus, Oei2023NGC6185 .

gmw_00000387.name: B 1933+503
gmw_00000387.overview: B 1933+503 is a complex gravitational lens system containing ten lensed radio images produced by a foreground galaxy at redshift 0.76 [arXiv:astro-ph/0008390, arXiv:astro-ph/9810444]. The background source, located at a redshift of 2.62, consists of a core and two radio lobes [arXiv:astro-ph/0008390, arXiv:0912.0916].

sources.summary: The paper models B 1933+503 as a ten-image strong lens whose z_l=0.76 lens galaxy requires a cuspy, quasi-isothermal mass profile with an approximately flat rotation curve.

quotes.quote: The focus of this paper is one such system, the 10 image lens B 1933+503 (Sykes et al. , Marlow et al. , Chapman et al. , Browne et al. , Biggs et al. , Norbury et al. ). B 1933+503 consists of two quadruply imaged radio sources and one doubly imaged radio source produced by a z_l=0.76 lens galaxy. The source redshift is z_s = 2.62 (Biggs et al , Norbury et al. ). The images range from unresolved to a pair of thin arcs (see the schematic in Figure 1).

After fitting a wide range of parameterized, ellipsoidal mass distributions to the B 1933+503 system, we conclude that the mass distribution must have an approximately flat rotation curve with a cuspy central density distribution. Over the range spanned by our acceptable models the rotation curve deviates from the average by less than 8.5 between the inner and outer images ( 1.7h^ 1 kpc to 4.1h^ 1 kpc). The central cusp needs to be quasi-isothermal ( ^ 2 ) and models with shallow cusps ( 1/ ) fail to fit the data. Traditional power law models with finite central core radii are inconsistent with the data, as we would expect from our current understanding of galaxy mass distributions.

sources.summary: The paper studies B 1933+503 as a complex dusty gravitational-lens radio quasar system, modeling submillimeter emission and arguing that its source lies at redshift greater than two.

quotes.quote: 1998 000--000 We present a detailed mm-wave and optical study of the gravitational lens system B 1933+503, discovered by Sykes et al. (1998) in the radio. This object is probably the most complex lens system known, with 10 lensed components within a radius of one arcsecond. It is potentially important as a probe of the Hubble constant, although no optical counterpart has thus far been observed down to I = 24.2 .

A lensing model is constructed, taking the foreground elliptical galaxy at z = 0.755 as the lensing mass. From this we derive a scenario from which to model the sub-millimetre emission. Several arguments then point to the source in the B 1933+503 system lying above a redshift of 2. We speculate that unlensed relatives of this source may constitute a sizable fraction of the 850 \ source counts. galaxies: active -- galaxies: starburst -- galaxies: individual: B 1933+503 -- gravitational lensing -- cosmology: observations -- infrared: galaxies 1 #1 '' . '' ' .

sources.summary: The paper describes B1933+503 as a multi-image lens from three sources, with radio-lobe/core structure and flux ratios far above smooth-model geometric predictions.

quotes.quote: B1933+503 has ten images as a result of three lensed images. Two of the sources result in quad formations and the third appears doubly imaged. As noted by , the three sources seem to correspond to a core and both radio lobes.

The four images that appear in a standard quad formation are a cross-like fold image set. KGP model R_ fold in a rather wide range from about -0.1 to 0.45, with a sharp peak at 0.05 and a shorter, wider peak at 0.3. The observed flux ratios (at any wavelength) are much higher: in the range of 0.580 to 0.722 Sykes/Browne:1998 .

sources.summary: The paper treats B1933+503 as a two-source lens by one foreground galaxy, finding the added constraints make the multipole lens model unphysical.

quotes.quote: Finally, we consider the system B1933+503, where two sources are lensed by the same foreground galaxy. The additional constraints provided by the images of the second source render the multipole model unphysical. We conclude that external shear must be taken into account to obtain plausible models, and that a purely smooth angular structure for the lens galaxy does not provide a viable alternative to the prevailing CDM clump hypothesis.

To conclude this section, we study B1933+503, where two sources are lensed by a single galaxy into two four-image configurations and references accompanying their Table 1 Cohn_1933 . Both the image positions and flux ratios corresponding to one source are known, while only the image positions of the second source have been determined to reasonable precision.

sources.summary: The paper identifies B1933+503 as a radio fold-lens anomaly because its 4/3 image pair violates the fold relation despite not being especially close.

quotes.quote: Comparing these predictions with the data, we find that five of the the 12 known lenses with fold configurations have flux ratio anomalies: B0712+472, SDSS 0924+0219, PG 1115+080, B1555+375, and B1933+503.
B1933+503: Although the 4/3 image pair is not particularly close, image 4 is so bright that there is a clear violation of the fold relation in the radio data.
It also reveals strong new evidence for fold flux ratio anomalies in B1555+375 (radio) and B1933+503 (radio), plus good but slightly less strong evidence for a fold anomaly in PG 1115+080 (optical).

gmw_00000388.name: IRAS 17208-0014
gmw_00000388.overview: IRAS 17208-0014 is a starburst-dominated, ultraluminous infrared galaxy [arXiv:1905.06950, arXiv:1105.1731] representing an advanced, late-stage merger with two tidal tails [arXiv:2311.02230]. The system features a single nucleus containing an optically hidden LINER [arXiv:astro-ph/0509681] and hosts a multi-phase molecular gas outflow [arXiv:1708.09503, arXiv:1901.06723].

sources.summary: The paper describes IRAS 17208-0014 as a nearby ULIRG/late-stage merger with high CO isotopologue line ratios, very low 13CO/C18O, and molecular gas concentrated in a single nucleus.

quotes.quote: We present high-resolution ALMA observations of the C ^ 18 O, ^ 13 CO and ^ 12 CO J =1-0 isotopologues in 3 nearby ultra-luminous infrared galaxies (ULIRGS; Arp 220, IRAS 13120-5453, IRAS 17208-0014) and 1 nearby post-merger galaxy (NGC 2623). In all 4 systems, we measure high ^ 12 CO/C ^ 18 O and ^ 12 CO/ ^ 13 CO integrated line ratios while the ^ 13 CO/C ^ 18 O ratio is observed to be extremely low in comparison to typical star-forming disks, supporting previous work. We investigate whether these unusual line ratios are due to dynamical effects, astrochemistry within the gas, or nucleosynthesis in stars. Assuming both lines are optically thin, low ^ 13 CO/C ^ 18 O values suggest that C ^ 18 O is more abundant than ^ 13 CO in the interstellar medium of these systems.

This paper combines new and archival ALMA 12m-array observations of C ^ 18 O, ^ 13 CO and ^ 12 CO J =1-0 lines in 4 nearby ( < 200 Mpc) advanced mergers: Arp 220, IRAS 13120-5453, IRAS 17208-0014, and NGC 2623. With infrared luminosities, L _ IR , exceeding 10 ^ 12 L _ , Arp 220, IRAS 13120-5453, and IRAS 17208-0014 are all ULIRGs, while NGC 2623 is a post-merger galaxy with L _ IR = 10 ^ 11.6 L _ Armus2009 .

The double nuclei of the merger system, Arp 220, are visible in all 3 emission lines while, despite having undergone a major merger at some point in their past, IRAS 13120-5453, IRAS 17208-0014, and NGC 2623 all distribute their molecular gas over a single nucleus. Combined with their different infrared luminosities, these morphological differences suggest our sample can be considered snapshots of different stages of the merger sequence, evolving from the bright ongoing merger (Arp 220), through the late stages of merging (IRAS 13120-5453, IRAS 17208-0014) and, finally, resulting in the fainter post-merger system (NGC 2623).

sources.summary: IRAS 17208 0014 is a more evolved single-nucleus ULIRG; the paper identifies an optically hidden LINER nucleus, ordered rotation, and a distinct LINER-like extranuclear ionization origin.

quotes.quote: The sample of galaxies consists of six low- z ULIRGs (see general properties in table misulirgs ) covering a relative wide range of dynamical states of the merging process. Three of the galaxies (IRAS 08572+3915, IRAS 12112+0305, and IRAS 14348 1447) are interacting pairs separated by projected distances of up to 6 kpc, while the rest of the galaxies (IRAS 15206+3342, IRAS 15250+3609, and IRAS 17208 0014) are more evolved, single nucleus ULIRGs, some with a light profile and overall kinematics close to that of intermediate mass ellipticals (e.g. IRAS 17208 0014, Genzel et al. 2001). The two-dimensional kinematic properties (velocity field and velocity dispersion) have been studied in detail before using integral field optical spectroscopy (see Colina et al. 2005 and references therein).

For example, IRAS 08572+3915 was originally classified as Seyfert 2 san88 although both nuclei were classified as LINERs later on kim98 ; IRAS 14348 1447 has been classified either as LINER kim98 or Seyfert 2 san88 ; IRAS 15206+3342 has been identified both as a Seyfert 2 san88,sur00a and as an H ii kim98 , and IRAS 15250+3609 is classified both as H ii and LINER kim95,baa98 . The other two galaxies, IRAS 12112+0305 and IRAS 17208 0014 are classified as LINER vei99 and H ii kim95 , respectively. Our IFS data disagree with some of the previous classifications, in particular the two nuclei of IRAS 08572+3915 are classified as H ii (Arribas et al. 2000), and the true, optically hidden, nucleus of IRAS 17208 0014 is classified as a LINER arr03 .

Finally, IRAS 17208 0014 does not follow the mean behavior observed in the other systems, showing a wider range of line ratio values. This galaxy has been studied in detail by arr03 and col05 , and it is the only clear case in this sample showing rotation on scales of several kpc. This may be an indication that this system is in a different (probably more evolved) dynamical phase and/or it has had a different merging history. At any event, the fact that the gas kinematics indicates a more relaxed and virialized system suggests that shocks are not the dominant ionization mechanism in this galaxy and, therefore the above mentioned correlation should not be expected. For this galaxy the origin of the LINER-like ionization in the extranuclear region should be different (note that the three line ratios shown in Fig. 4 are consistent with LINER-like ionization).

sources.summary: The paper includes IRAS 17208-0014 as an ultraluminous infrared galaxy observed in 3 mm lines, with dense-gas ratios close to Arp 220.

quotes.quote: Our selected galaxies, have been detected previously in HCN, and have different degrees of nuclear activity --- one normal galaxy (NGC 6946), the starburst prototype (M 82) and two ultraluminous infrared galaxies (ULIRGs, IRAS 17208-0014 and Mrk 231). We plotted our data in the HCO ^+ /HCN vs. HCN/ ^ 13 CO diagnostic diagram finding that NGC 6946 and M 82 are located close to other normal galaxies; and that both IRAS 17208-0014 and Mrk 231 are close to the position of the well known ULIRG Arp 220 reported by  Snell2011 . We found that in Mrk 231 -- a galaxy with a well known active galactic nucleus -- the HCO ^+ /HCN ratio is similar to the ratio observed in other normal galaxies.

The observations discussed in this paper include M 82 which was observed with the RSR at the LMT during the first-light scientific demonstration observations phase in June 2011, and NGC 6946, IRAS 17208-0014 and Mrk 231 that where observed during the summer of 2013. In the observed 3 mm spectra of these galaxies we detected several lines including, in some cases, lines as weak as CN which traces very dense molecular gas (n(H _2) 10^6 cm ^ 3 ). However, we are reporting only our results on the HCN, HCO ^+ and ^ 13 CO lines.

M 82 and NGC 6946 are close to other normal galaxies reported by  Snell2011 . In that paper they also observed M 82 (marked with a D in the Figure) with the same receiver but mounted in the FCRAO 15 m antenna. The differences between the HCN, ^ 13 CO ratio can be explained by the different beam sizes between the LMT and the FCRAO and the relative distribution of the dense gas traced by HCN and the more diffuse gas traced by ^ 13 CO. The two ultra--luminous galaxies (ULIRGs) observed, IRAS 17208-0014 and Mrk 231, are close to the position of the well known ULIRG Arp 220, indicating the presence of high amounts of dense gas in both galaxies. Notice that Mrk 231, a well known active galaxy classified as a Seyfert 1, has not an HCO ^+ enhancement as predicted by the theoretical models. By using all the available data of this galaxy, which cover all the electromagnetic spectrum, we expect to better understand this discrepancy.

sources.summary: The paper describes IRAS 17208-0014 as a starburst-dominated ULIRG with strong 3.3 micron PAH emission, a clear 3.1 micron ice absorption feature, and no AGN indication at other wavelengths.

quotes.quote: IRAS 17208-0014 . The L-band spectrum is dominated by a strong PAH 3.3 m emission feature. The continuum slope 0.3 is indicative of a starburst origin of the L-band emission. An ice absorption feature at 3.1 m is clearly detected. A single Gaussian absorption component is significant at a 5 statistical level.

This, together with IRAS 23128-5919S, is the best constrained ice absorption feature among the sources in our sample, thanks to the high S/N spectrum which allows a precise continuum determination. The estimated optical depth is _ 3.1 =0.4 0.05 . In the mid-IR and X-rays IRAS 17208-0014 appears dominated by starburst activity.

sources.summary: The paper treats IRASF17207-0014 as a gas-rich merger with two measured black holes, a large scaling-relation offset, high star-formation requirement, and insufficient gas by its estimate.

quotes.quote: We also note that the two galaxies which fall furthest above the relation, IRASF01364-1042 and IRASF17207-0014, do not show atypically large residuals in the GALFIT bulge fits (indeed, the residuals of the former appear smaller than average for this sample), which suggests that their offset is not due to errors in the bulge luminosities of Haan11 . Again, our sample of merging galaxies falls above this relation, suggesting that either stars have not been forming in the bulge as quickly as the black holes have been growing, or that a large mass of stars is still strewn about in tidal tails and unrelaxed features that will eventually become part of the bulge.

For example, IRASF17207-0014 falls closer to the M_ BH _ * relation than to the M_ BH L_ H,bulge relation; for NGC 6240S, the reverse is true. This discrepancy could be caused by at least two possible mechanisms: 1) The host galaxy parameters trace different aspects of galaxy growth, which may evolve differently.

Taking these uncertainties into account, the gas masses could be sufficient in UGC5101 and NGC 6240, but perhaps not in NGC 2623 or IRASF17207-0014. However, in the case of IRASF17207-0014, the kinematics are sufficiently complex that the two black holes were fixed to the photometric center of each disk measured with GALFIT in nucleardisks .

sources.summary: The paper treats IRAS 17208-0014 as an ambiguous case: broad H2 emission is resolved near systemic velocity, but no hot H2 counterpart to the CO outflow is detected.

quotes.quote: IRAS 17208-0014 IRAS 17208-0014 has been associated with multi-phase gas outflows by various authors rup05,mar06,stu11,arr14,sot12,med15 . However, several integral-field studies did not find evidence for such outflows in the ionized phase arr03,wes12,rup13a , and arr03 even argued the presence of gas inflows. In our SINFONI data, the overall H _ 2 kinematics appear to follow the CO kinematics of cold molecular gas discussed in detail by gar15 .

The broad-component H _ 2 emission is only seen near the systemic velocity, and spreads across 2 kpc, which is a substantial fraction of the narrow-component disk. In Sect. sec:17208 , we discuss that this broad-component emission is not likely to be related to an outflow, although its true nature remains unclear. We do not detect a hot H _ 2 counterpart to the cold molecular outflow previously discovered in CO by gar15 .

sources.summary: The paper identifies IRAS 17208-0014 as a ULIRG merger system with high-excitation, warm molecular gas, low CO optical depths, large velocity gradients, and evidence for a second warmer, denser phase.

quotes.quote: The two ULIRGs ( L_ IR > 10^ 12 L_ ) IRAS 17208-0014 and IRAS 05189-2524 have been found to have very different CO line excitation (Figure 4) despite similar IR continuum and CO J=1--0 line luminosities and similar S_ 60 m /S_ 100 m ratios. For IRAS 17208-0014 the CO brightness temperature ratios measured are typical for the high-excitation star forming molecular gas found in many such galaxies, but those found for the ``cold'' IRAS 05189-2524 are surprisingly typical of the quiescent molecular clouds found in the Milky Way disk (Table 2). One-phase radiative transfer models using the Large Velocity Gradient (LVG) approximation and constrained by the line ratios of IRAS 17208-0014 are compatible with densities of n 10^3 cm^ 3 and mostly warm T_k (70-100) K . The high ^ 12 CO/ ^ 13 CO J=2--1 ratio measured for this merger system (Table 2) is responsible for the low CO J=1--0 optical depths ( _ 10 0.5 ) of this gas phase, a result of its high temperatures and large average velocity gradients dV/dR 30 km s^ 1 pc^ 1 (for an abundance of CO/H_2 =10^ 4 ). The latter corresponds to a parameter K_ vir = (dV/dR)_ obs (dV/dR)_ virial 30-50 , typical of an unbound gas phase ( ).

It is also worth noting that the high (4--3)/(1--0) CO ratio (comparable to that of CO(3--2)/(1--0)) cannot be accounted by a single gas phase model and probably signifies the emergence of a second warmer and denser phase.

sources.summary: The paper observes IRAS17208-0014 as a bright CO source, notes faint northwestern extension, and reports marginal line wings consistent with known CO outflow emission.

quotes.quote: We targeted IRAS05083+7936 (I05083), IRAS10035+4852 (I100035), IRAS17132+5313 (I17132), and IRAS17208-0014 (I17208), which C11 classified as bright (I05083, I17208) or intermediate (I100035, I17132) in CO flux.
I17208 also shows some faint extension to the northwest, but this appears approximately consistent with an extension of the velocity field of the rotating disk.

Faint Broad Wings Though we do not find a high-flux extended component, in both I05083 and I17208 we do detect line wings containing at most a few percent of the flux of the main line at marginal significance. These are visible in Figure fig:spectra and in the integrated line wing fluxes presented in Table tab:obs . Using higher spatial resolution observations, IRAS17208 has recently been demonstrated to have CO outflow GARCIABURILLO15 and the magnitude and velocity of the outflowing CO emission in their data ( 500 750 km s ^ 1 and a few mJy) is consistent with the faint emission in our spectrum).

sources.summary: The paper describes this luminous ULIRG as starburst-dominated, with composite line ratios, high velocity dispersion, blueshifted Na D, and outflow-driven low-velocity shocks.

quotes.quote: IRAS F17207-0014 This ULIRG is the second brightest in the GOALS sample, behind only MRK 231. Despite its high L _ IR , the X-ray data from Chandra and XMM are apparently dominated by the starburst Franceschini03,Iwasawa11 . The 6.2 PAH eqw is low (0.3 ), though there is no detected [ Ne V ] emission Petric11, Stierwalt13 . Arribas03 carried out early IFS observations of this system and suggest that this ULIRG is not evolving into a QSO. Our data show composite-like line ratios,though the overall velocity dispersion distribution is quite high, with exceeding 100 in nearly all measurable spaxels.

sources.summary: The paper discusses IRAS 17208-0014 as a ULIRG with newly detected HCN-vib J=3-2 emission, severe line blending, and possible blueshifted outflow signatures.

quotes.quote: Another ULIRG, IRAS 17208-0014, has previously been detected in the HCN-vib J=4 - 3 transition, but this is the first detection of the J=3 - 2 transition. We also note that HCN-vib J=3 - 2 emission, with a luminosity of 2.2 L _ , has recently been detected in NGC 1068 by ima20 . In Fig. fig:detections , we present the spectra of sources with new detections of HCN-vib J=3 - 2 emission. The transition is clearly visible in the spectra of all sources except for IRAS F14378-3651, in which the integrated intensity map was used to confirm the detection. In IRAS 17208-0014 the line widths are large, causing noticeable blending of HCN-vib with the HCO ^ + transition.

For IRAS 17208-0014, this is hindered by the large line widths which cause severe blending with nearby lines of CH _ 2 NH and vibrationally excited HC _ 3 N, but a possible outflow signature in the form of blueshifted ( 70 km s ^ 1 ) self-absorption features can be seen in the HCN and HCO ^ + ground-state lines in Fig. fig:detections .

This may be true also for the outflow in IRAS 17208-0014 gar15 , although its morphology still requires further study. IRAS F22491-1808 has outflow signatures on kpc scales in the J=2 - 1 transition of CO reported by per18 . In NGC 4418, a compact molecular outflow detected in low- J transitions of CO has been reported by flu19 and lut20 but its geometry is not known. The final CON, IRAS 17578-0400, does not have any published outflow signatures, but our HCN observations reveal a low-velocity elongation along its kinematic minor axis (Fig. fig:outflow_I17578 ). This may be a signature of a minor axis outflow which is directed almost perpendicular to our line of sight toward the galaxy. Possible signatures of noncircular motions are also seen in the ground state HCN and HCO ^ + lines in the form of asymmetric line profiles in some CONs. In most of them, the absorptions are stronger on the blueshifted side: Zw 049.057 aal15b , IRAS 17208-0014 (Fig. fig:detections ), and Arp 220 sak09,aal15b,mar16 or close to the line center: IRAS 17578-0400 (Fig. fig:detections ), and NGC 4418 sak13 .

sources.summary: The paper observes IRAS 17208-0014 with APEX, measures its water-line flux, and describes it as an advanced merger with two tidal tails.

quotes.quote: In this work, we present high-spectral resolution observations of the p- 211202 \ black in three low-redshift ULIRGs (IRAS 06035-7102, IRAS 17208-0014 and IRAS 09022-3615) using the single-dish APEX telescope with the SEPIA receiver in Band-9 Baryshev2015,Belitsky2018 . Throughout this work, we assumed a CDM cosmology with _ M =0.3 , _ =0.7 and H_0=75 km s^ 1 Mpc^ 1 . black Source selection black The three sources were selected from a sample of ULIRGs observed in water lines with Herschel and presented in Yang2013 .

The spectra show that p- 211202 is detected with a peak flux density of 0.45 0.09 Jy beam ^ 1 in IRAS 09022-3615, 1.28 0.34 Jy beam ^ 1 in IRAS 17208-0014 and 0.33 0.08 Jy beam ^ 1 in IRAS 06035-7102.
IRAS 17208-0014 is an advanced merger with two tidal tails toward the southeast and northwest.

sources.summary: The paper identifies IRAS 17208-0014 as a cold, starburst-dominated ULIRG with little AGN contribution, a 370 km/s terminal OH outflow, and mass loss comparable to its SFR.

quotes.quote: For this first study of outflow signatures in our data we use a sub-sample that is mainly constrained by the observing schedule of Herschel, but that covers a broad range of AGN and starburst activity, including a starburst template (NGC 253), a cold, starburst dominated ULIRG (IRAS 17208-0014,) warm ULIRGs (S25/S60 > 0.1) and/or ULIRGs with strong AGN contributions (Mrk 231, IRAS 13120-5453, IRAS 14378-3651), and a heavily obscured ULIRG (IRAS 08572+3915), which hosts a powerful AGN (e.g. Veilleux et al. 2009, V09).

The terminal velocity we measure in the OH outflow of our starburst template NGC 253 is 300 km/s. IRAS 17208-0014, a starburst dominated ULIRG with little AGN contribution has only a slightly higher v _ max (370 km/s).
The starburst-dominated objects of our sample (NGC 253, IRAS 13120-5453, and IRAS 17208-0014) have values of M similar to their star formation rates.

sources.summary: The paper studies IRAS F17207-0014 as a nearby late-stage major merger and ULIRG, using optical and near-infrared IFU data to interpret shocks from merger activity and outflows.

quotes.quote: This paper focuses on IRAS F17207-0014, the first target for which we have obtained both datasets. IRAS F17207-0014 (log( L_ IR /L_ ) = 12.46, 17h23m22.01s -00d17m00.2s, z=0.043, 0.848 kpc arcsec ^ 1 ) is a late-stage major merger merger stage classification 5 out of 6, `single or obscured nucleus with long tidal tails'; Haan11, Kim13 , and the most infrared luminous system in the southern sky.

We have presented additional analysis of integral-field spectroscopic observations of the ULIRG IRAS F17207-0014, a nearby late-stage major merger, making use of wide-field optical emission line ratios and complementary high spatial resolution AO-assisted near-infrared IFU data. We find plausible evidence for two potential causes of the shocks seen in IRAS F17207-0014.

sources.summary: The survey reports first HC3N and HNC detections for IRAS 17208-0014 and places it among the HC3N-luminous or HC3N-rich galaxies.

quotes.quote: New detections This is not only the first text to put together a survey of all extragalactic HC _3 N emission line data, but it also reports the first HC _3 N detections in six galaxies: Circinus , IC 860 , IRAS 17208-0014 , Maffei 2 , NGC 1068 , and NGC 3079 . Three of the HNC detections are also made in sources without earlier HNC detections: Circinus , IC 860 , and IRAS 17208-0014 . The galaxies thus seen as HC _3 N-luminous or HC _3 N-rich are NGC 4418 , IC 342 , Circinus , M82 , Maffei 2 , Arp 220 , and IRAS 17208-0014 .

sources.summary: The paper describes IRAS F17207 0014 as a late-stage ULIRG merger with two kinematically distinct nuclei, shocks near an outflow base, a possible hidden AGN, and offset star formation.

quotes.quote: IRAS F17207 0014 IRAS F17207 0014 is a late-stage merging ULIRG with two kinematically distinct nuclei, the collision of whose ISM has likely induced shocks tracking the base of a collimated outflow . High-resolution Plateau de Bure interferometer data had also found a CO molecular outflow plausibly associated with a hidden AGN in the western nucleus, as suggested by . From the SFR map, we find that the sites for star formation as traced by are to the west of the outflow base.

sources.summary: The paper treats IRAS 17208-0014 as one of the two strongest HCN-vib sources, with low inferred AGN contribution, a compact high-velocity CO outflow, and OH outflow signatures hidden at far-infrared wavelengths.

quotes.quote: Indeed, in the two sources with the highest absolute HCN-vib luminosities, IRAS 17208-0014 and Arp 220, less than 10 of the bolometric luminosities are estimated to come from AGN activity, based on the 15 to 30 m continuum ratio vei13 .

For example, a compact v>800 km s ^ 1 CO J=2 - 1 outflow has been found in the ULIRG IRAS 17208-0014 gar15 and an equally fast, compact, and collimated, HCN J=1 - 0 outflow has been detected in the ULIRG Arp 220 bar18 .

In fact, at least three of the five most obscured sources Arp 220, IRAS 17208-0014, and Zw 049.057; baa89,fal18 have outflows that are seen in the OH lines at centimeter wavelengths but not in the far-infrared.

sources.summary: The paper places IRAS 17208-0014 among lower-excitation sources in this work while noting prior high-J excitation and a CO ladder that remains significantly excited after its peak.

quotes.quote: For instance, the CO ladder of IRAS 17208-0014 does not fall down steeply after its peak, but remains significantly excited.

And indeed, some of the sources belonging to the `lower-excitation' group under our criteria, have been observed to have high-excitation CO SLEDs when probing CO( J>7 ) lines (e.g., IRAS 09022-3615, IRAS F05189-2524, IRAS 17208-0014) . showed that, while there are sources for which a single-component CO SLED fit is sufficient, the CO ladder of IRAS 09022-3615 (classified as a warm ULIRG in their study, and included also in our work) requires three excitation components in order to reproduce the observed CO fluxes up to J_ up =13 : (i) a cold (47 K) component peaking at J_ up 3 , (ii) a warm (205 K) component peaking at J_ up 6 , and (iii) a third hot (415 K) component that dominates at high- J transitions, peaking around J_ up 11 .
Sources IRAS 17208-0014 and IRAS F05189-2524 are classified as Class III objects in their study, while in this work they both lie in our lower-excitation group.
